<content>1: import type { Env } from '../types.js';
2: import { createCheckpointJob, deleteJob, generateJobId, updateJobStatus } from '../lib/db.js';
3: import { createCheckpointJobQueueMessage } from '../lib/checkpoint-queue.js';
4: import { normalizeProjectRoot } from '../lib/checkpoint-plan.js';
5: 
6: export const MAX_SOURCE_BUNDLE_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
7: 
8: const CHECKPOINT_ID_REGEX = /^[a-f0-9]{12}$/i;
9: const COMMIT_SHA_REGEX = /^[a-f0-9]{40}$/i;
10: 
11: const corsHeaders = {
12:   'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
13:   'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
14:   'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type',
15: };
16: 
17: export interface CheckpointJobMetadata {
18:   source: {
19:     type: 'checkpoint';
20:     checkpointId: string | null;
21:     commitSha: string;
22:     ref?: string;
23:     projectRoot?: string;
24:   };
25:   build: {
26:     runTestsIfPresent: boolean;
27:     runLintIfPresent: boolean;
28:   };
29: }
30: 
31: export interface ParsedCheckpointCreateRequest {
32:   metadata: CheckpointJobMetadata;
33:   bundle: {
34:     type: string;
35:   };
36:   bundleBytes: number;
37:   bundleSha256: string;
38:   bundleArrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer;
39: }
40: 
41: interface MultipartFileLike {
42:   size: number;
43:   type: string;
44:   arrayBuffer(): Promise<ArrayBuffer>;
45: }
46: 
47: function isMultipartFileLike(value: unknown): value is MultipartFileLike {
48:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
49:     return false;
50:   }
51: 
52:   const candidate = value as Partial<MultipartFileLike>;
53:   return (
54:     typeof candidate.size === 'number' &&
55:     typeof candidate.type === 'string' &&
56:     typeof candidate.arrayBuffer === 'function'
57:   );
58: }
59: 
60: function requireObject(value: unknown, message: string): Record<string, unknown> {
61:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) {
62:     throw new Error(message);
63:   }
64: 
65:   return value as Record<string, unknown>;
66: }
67: 
68: function parseOptionalString(value: unknown, fieldName: string): string | undefined {
69:   if (value === undefined || value === null) {
70:     return undefined;
71:   }
72: 
73:   if (typeof value !== 'string') {
74:     throw new Error(`Invalid ${fieldName}: expected string`);
75:   }
76: 
77:   const trimmed = value.trim();
78:   return trimmed ? trimmed : undefined;
79: }
80: 
81: export function parseCheckpointJobMetadata(metadataJson: string): CheckpointJobMetadata {
82:   let parsed: unknown;
83: 
84:   try {
85:     parsed = JSON.parse(metadataJson);
86:   } catch {
87:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata JSON');
88:   }
89: 
90:   const root = requireObject(parsed, 'Invalid metadata payload');
91:   const source = requireObject(root.source, 'Invalid metadata.source');
92:   const build = requireObject(root.build, 'Invalid metadata.build');
93: 
94:   if (source.type !== 'checkpoint') {
95:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata.source.type: expected "checkpoint"');
96:   }
97: 
98:   const checkpointIdValue = source.checkpointId;
99:   let checkpointId: string | null = null;
100:   if (checkpointIdValue !== undefined && checkpointIdValue !== null) {
101:     if (typeof checkpointIdValue !== 'string' || !CHECKPOINT_ID_REGEX.test(checkpointIdValue.trim())) {
102:       throw new Error('Invalid metadata.source.checkpointId: expected 12-char hex or null');
103:     }
104: 
105:     checkpointId = checkpointIdValue.trim().toLowerCase();
106:   }
107: 
108:   const commitShaValue = source.commitSha;
109:   if (typeof commitShaValue !== 'string' || !COMMIT_SHA_REGEX.test(commitShaValue.trim())) {
110:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata.source.commitSha: expected full 40-char git commit SHA');
111:   }
112: 
113:   const runTestsIfPresent = build.runTestsIfPresent;
114:   const runLintIfPresent = build.runLintIfPresent;
115: 
116:   if (typeof runTestsIfPresent !== 'boolean') {
117:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata.build.runTestsIfPresent: expected boolean');
118:   }
119: 
120:   if (typeof runLintIfPresent !== 'boolean') {
121:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata.build.runLintIfPresent: expected boolean');
122:   }
123: 
124:   let projectRoot: string | undefined;
125:   try {
126:     const parsedProjectRoot = parseOptionalString(source.projectRoot, 'metadata.source.projectRoot');
127:     if (parsedProjectRoot !== undefined) {
128:       projectRoot = normalizeProjectRoot(parsedProjectRoot);
129:     }
130:   } catch {
131:     throw new Error('Invalid metadata.source.projectRoot: expected safe relative directory path');
132:   }
133: 
134:   return {
135:     source: {
136:       type: 'checkpoint',
137:       checkpointId,
138:       commitSha: commitShaValue.trim().toLowerCase(),
139:       ref: parseOptionalString(source.ref, 'metadata.source.ref'),
140:       projectRoot,
141:     },
142:     build: {
143:       runTestsIfPresent,
144:       runLintIfPresent,
145:     },
146:   };
147: }
148: 
149: function toHex(bytes: Uint8Array): string {
150:   let result = '';
151:   for (const byte of bytes) {
152:     result += byte.toString(16).padStart(2, '0');
153:   }
154:   return result;
155: }
156: 
157: async function sha256Hex(input: ArrayBuffer): Promise<string> {
158:   const digest = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', input);
159:   return toHex(new Uint8Array(digest));
160: }
161: 
162: export async function parseCheckpointCreateRequest(
163:   request: Request,
164:   maxBundleBytes = MAX_SOURCE_BUNDLE_BYTES
165: ): Promise<ParsedCheckpointCreateRequest> {
166:   let formData: FormData;
167: 
168:   try {
169:     formData = await request.formData();
170:   } catch {
171:     throw new Error('Invalid multipart form body');
172:   }
173: 
174:   const metadataField = formData.get('metadata');
175:   if (typeof metadataField !== 'string') {
176:     throw new Error('Missing metadata form field');
177:   }
178: 
179:   const metadata = parseCheckpointJobMetadata(metadataField);
180: 
181:   const bundleField = formData.get('bundle');
182:   if (!isMultipartFileLike(bundleField)) {
183:     throw new Error('Missing bundle form file');
184:   }
185: 
186:   if (bundleField.size <= 0) {
187:     throw new Error('Source bundle is empty');
188:   }
189: 
190:   if (bundleField.size > maxBundleBytes) {
191:     throw new Error(`Source bundle exceeds max size of ${maxBundleBytes} bytes`);
192:   }
193: 
194:   const bundleArrayBuffer = await bundleField.arrayBuffer();
195:   const bundleSha256 = await sha256Hex(bundleArrayBuffer);
196: 
197:   return {
198:     metadata,
199:     bundle: bundleField,
200:     bundleBytes: bundleArrayBuffer.byteLength,
201:     bundleSha256,
202:     bundleArrayBuffer,
203:   };
204: }
205: 
206: function checkpointPrompt(checkpointId: string | null, commitSha: string): string {
207:   if (checkpointId) {
208:     return `Deploy checkpoint ${checkpointId}`;
209:   }
210: 
211:   return `Deploy commit ${commitSha.slice(0, 12)}`;
212: }
213: 
214: function sourceBundleR2Key(jobId: string, commitSha: string): string {
215:   return `jobs/${jobId}/source/${commitSha}.tar.gz`;
216: }
217: 
218: export async function handleCreateCheckpointJob(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
219:   if (!env.SOURCE_BUNDLES) {
220:     return new Response(
221:       JSON.stringify({ error: 'SOURCE_BUNDLES R2 binding is not configured' }),
222:       {
223:         status: 500,
224:         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
225:       }
226:     );
227:   }
228: 
229:   if (!env.CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE) {
230:     return new Response(
231:       JSON.stringify({ error: 'CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE binding is not configured' }),
232:       {
233:         status: 500,
234:         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
235:       }
236:     );
237:   }
238: 
239:   let parsed: ParsedCheckpointCreateRequest;
240: 
241:   try {
242:     parsed = await parseCheckpointCreateRequest(request);
243:   } catch (error) {
244:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
245:     return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: message }), {
246:       status: 400,
247:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
248:     });
249:   }
250: 
251:   const jobId = generateJobId();
252:   const sourceBundleKey = sourceBundleR2Key(jobId, parsed.metadata.source.commitSha);
253:   let bundleUploaded = false;
254:   let jobPersisted = false;
255:   let jobDeleted = false;
256: 
257:   try {
258:     await env.SOURCE_BUNDLES.put(sourceBundleKey, parsed.bundleArrayBuffer, {
259:       httpMetadata: {
260:         contentType: parsed.bundle.type || 'application/gzip',
261:       },
262:       customMetadata: {
263:         source_type: parsed.metadata.source.type,
264:         commit_sha: parsed.metadata.source.commitSha,
265:         checkpoint_id: parsed.metadata.source.checkpointId ?? '',
266:         source_project_root: parsed.metadata.source.projectRoot ?? '',
267:         build_run_tests_if_present: String(parsed.metadata.build.runTestsIfPresent),
268:         build_run_lint_if_present: String(parsed.metadata.build.runLintIfPresent),
269:       },
270:     });
271:     bundleUploaded = true;
272: 
273:     await createCheckpointJob(env.DB, {
274:       id: jobId,
275:       prompt: checkpointPrompt(parsed.metadata.source.checkpointId, parsed.metadata.source.commitSha),
276:       checkpointId: parsed.metadata.source.checkpointId,
277:       commitSha: parsed.metadata.source.commitSha,
278:       sourceRef: parsed.metadata.source.ref,
279:       sourceProjectRoot: parsed.metadata.source.projectRoot,
280:       buildRunTestsIfPresent: parsed.metadata.build.runTestsIfPresent,
281:       buildRunLintIfPresent: parsed.metadata.build.runLintIfPresent,
282:       sourceBundleKey,
283:       sourceBundleSha256: parsed.bundleSha256,
284:       sourceBundleBytes: parsed.bundleBytes,
285:     });
286:     jobPersisted = true;
287: 
288:     await env.CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE.send(createCheckpointJobQueueMessage(jobId));
289:   } catch (error) {
290:     if (jobPersisted) {
291:       try {
292:         await deleteJob(env.DB, jobId);
293:         jobDeleted = true;
294:       } catch (deleteError) {
295:         const deleteMessage = deleteError instanceof Error ? deleteError.message : String(deleteError);
296: 
297:         try {
298:           await updateJobStatus(env.DB, jobId, 'failed', {
299:             phase: 'failed',
300:             completed_at: new Date().toISOString(),
301:             error_message: `Queue enqueue failed and cleanup delete failed: ${deleteMessage}`,
302:           });
303:         } catch {
304:           // Best-effort fallback only.
305:         }
306: 
307:         // Best-effort cleanup only.
308:       }
309:     }
310: 
311:     if (bundleUploaded && (!jobPersisted || jobDeleted)) {
312:       try {
313:         await env.SOURCE_BUNDLES.delete(sourceBundleKey);
314:       } catch {
315:         // Best-effort cleanup only.
316:       }
317:     }
318: 
319:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
320:     return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: `Failed to create checkpoint job: ${message}` }), {
321:       status: 500,
322:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
323:     });
324:   }
325: 
326:   return new Response(
327:     JSON.stringify({
328:       jobId,
329:       status: 'queued',
330:       phase: 'queued',
331:       eventsUrl: `/api/jobs/${jobId}/events`,
332:       jobUrl: `/api/jobs/${jobId}`,
333:     }),
334:     {
335:       status: 202,
336:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
337:     }
338:   );
339: }

(End of file - total 339 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { getJob, listJobEvents } from '../lib/db.js';

4: const corsHeaders = {
5:   'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
6:   'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
7:   'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type',
8: };
9: 
10: function formatSseData(payload: unknown): string {
11:   return `data: ${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n\n`;
12: }
13: 
14: function formatSseDataWithId(seq: number, payload: unknown): string {
15:   return `id: ${seq}\ndata: ${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n\n`;
16: }
17: 
18: function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
19:   return Boolean(value) && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
20: }
21: 
22: function normalizeReplayEventPayload(payload: unknown): Record<string, unknown> {
23:   if (isRecord(payload)) {
24:     return payload;
25:   }
26: 
27:   return { value: payload };
28: }
29: 
30: function resolveFromSequence(request: Request): number {
31:   const url = new URL(request.url);
32:   const fromParam = Number.parseInt(url.searchParams.get('from') ?? '', 10);
33:   const lastEventId = Number.parseInt(request.headers.get('Last-Event-ID') ?? '', 10);
34: 
35:   if (Number.isFinite(lastEventId) && lastEventId >= 0) {
36:     return lastEventId;
37:   }
38: 
39:   if (Number.isFinite(fromParam) && fromParam >= 0) {
40:     return fromParam;
41:   }
42: 
43:   return 0;
44: }
45: 
46: /**
47:  * Handle GET /api/jobs/:id/events - lightweight snapshot stream
48:  */
49: export async function handleGetJobEvents(jobId: string, request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
50:   try {
51:     const job = await getJob(env.DB, jobId);
52: 
53:     if (!job) {
54:       return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Job not found' }), {
55:         status: 404,
56:         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
57:       });
58:     }
59: 
60:     const fromSeq = resolveFromSequence(request);
61:     const persistedEvents = await listJobEvents(env.DB, jobId, fromSeq);
62: 
63:     const replayBody = persistedEvents
64:       .map((item) =>
65:         formatSseDataWithId(item.seq, {
66:           type: item.eventType,
67:           jobId,
68:           phase: item.phase,
69:           seq: item.seq,
70:           createdAt: item.createdAt,
71:           ...normalizeReplayEventPayload(item.payload),
72:         })
73:       )
74:       .join('');
75: 
76:     const snapshotBody = formatSseData({
77:       type: 'snapshot',
78:       jobId,
79:       status: job.status,
80:       phase: job.phase,
81:     });
82: 
83:     const body = replayBody ? `${replayBody}${snapshotBody}` : snapshotBody;
84: 
85:     return new Response(body, {
86:       status: 200,
87:       headers: {
88:         'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream',
89:         'Cache-Control': 'no-cache',
90:         Connection: 'keep-alive',
91:         ...corsHeaders,
92:       },
93:     });
94:   } catch (error) {
95:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
96:     return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: message }), {
97:       status: 500,
98:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
99:     });
100:   }
101: }

329: export async function processCheckpointJob(env: Env, jobId: string): Promise<void> {
330:   const job = await getJob(env.DB, jobId);
331:   if (!job) {
332:     return;
333:   }
334: 
335:   if (job.sourceType !== 'checkpoint') {
336:     console.warn(`[checkpoint-runner] Ignoring queue message for non-checkpoint job ${job.id}`);
337:     return;
338:   }
339: 
340:   if (job.status === 'running' && (job.phase === 'building' || job.phase === 'validating')) {
341:     return;
342:   }
343: 
344:   if (job.status !== 'queued') {
345:     return;
346:   }
347: 
348:   const claimed = await claimQueuedCheckpointJob(env.DB, job.id);
349:   if (!claimed) {
350:     return;
351:   }
352: 
353:   try {
354:     await appendJobStartedEvent(env.DB, job);
355:   } catch (eventError) {
356:     const details = eventError instanceof Error ? eventError.message : String(eventError);
357:     try {
358:       await requeueForRetry(env.DB, job.id, `job-started-event: ${details}`);
359:     } catch (requeueError) {
360:       const requeueMessage = requeueError instanceof Error ? requeueError.message : String(requeueError);
361:       throw new QueueRetryError(
362:         `Failed to append job_started event: ${details}; additionally failed to requeue job status: ${requeueMessage}`
363:       );
364:     }
365: 
366:     throw new QueueRetryError(`Failed to append job_started event: ${details}`);
367:   }
368: 
369:   try {
370:     await executeCheckpointBuild(env, job);
371:   } catch (error) {
372:     if (error instanceof QueueRetryError) {
373:       try {
374:         await requeueForRetry(env.DB, job.id, error.message);
375:       } catch (requeueError) {
376:         const requeueMessage = requeueError instanceof Error ? requeueError.message : String(requeueError);
377:         throw new QueueRetryError(`${error.message}; additionally failed to requeue job status: ${requeueMessage}`);
378:       }
379: 
380:       throw error;
381:     }
382: 
383:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
384: 
385:     try {
386:       await markFailed(env.DB, job.id, message);

<content>1: export type CheckpointPackageManager = 'npm' | 'bun';
2: 
3: export interface CheckpointExecutionPlan {
4:   install: string;
5:   build: string | null;
6:   test: string | null;
7:   lint: string | null;
8: }
9: 
10: export function normalizeProjectRoot(sourceProjectRoot: string | null | undefined): string {
11:   if (!sourceProjectRoot || sourceProjectRoot === '.') {
12:     return '.';
13:   }
14: 
15:   const slashNormalized = sourceProjectRoot.replace(/\\/g, '/').trim();
16:   if (/^\/+$/u.test(slashNormalized)) {
17:     throw new Error('projectRoot must be a relative directory path');
18:   }
19: 
20:   const normalized = slashNormalized.replace(/^\.\//, '').replace(/\/+$/, '');
21:   if (!normalized) {
22:     return '.';
23:   }
24: 
25:   if (normalized.startsWith('/')) {
26:     throw new Error('projectRoot must be a relative directory path');
27:   }
28: 
29:   const segments = normalized.split('/');
30:   for (const segment of segments) {
31:     if (!segment || segment === '.' || segment === '..') {
32:       throw new Error('projectRoot contains invalid path traversal segments');
33:     }
34:   }
35: 
36:   return normalized;
37: }
38: 
39: export function resolveProjectDir(sourceRoot: string, sourceProjectRoot: string | null | undefined): string {
40:   const normalizedProjectRoot = normalizeProjectRoot(sourceProjectRoot);
41:   if (normalizedProjectRoot === '.') {
42:     return sourceRoot;
43:   }
44: 
45:   return `${sourceRoot}/${normalizedProjectRoot}`;
46: }
47: 
48: export function detectPackageManager(lockfiles: string[]): CheckpointPackageManager {
49:   const fileSet = new Set(lockfiles);
50: 
51:   if (fileSet.has('bun.lock') || fileSet.has('bun.lockb')) {
52:     return 'bun';
53:   }
54: 
55:   if (fileSet.has('package-lock.json')) {
56:     return 'npm';
57:   }
58: 
59:   if (fileSet.has('pnpm-lock.yaml')) {
60:     throw new Error('pnpm lockfile detected but pnpm is not supported yet for checkpoint execution');
61:   }
62: 
63:   if (fileSet.has('yarn.lock')) {
64:     throw new Error('yarn lockfile detected but yarn is not supported yet for checkpoint execution');
65:   }
66: 
67:   throw new Error('No lockfile found. Checkpoint execution requires lockfile-based deterministic install.');
68: }
69: 
70: function installCommand(packageManager: CheckpointPackageManager): string {
71:   if (packageManager === 'bun') {
72:     return 'bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts';
73:   }
74: 
75:   return 'npm ci --include=dev --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund';
76: }
77: 
78: function runScriptCommand(packageManager: CheckpointPackageManager, scriptName: string): string {
79:   if (packageManager === 'bun') {
80:     return `bun run ${scriptName}`;
81:   }
82: 
83:   return `npm run ${scriptName}`;
84: }
85: 
86: export function buildCheckpointExecutionPlan(input: {
87:   packageManager: CheckpointPackageManager;
88:   scripts: Record<string, string>;
89:   runTestsIfPresent: boolean;
90:   runLintIfPresent: boolean;
91: }): CheckpointExecutionPlan {
92:   const hasBuild = typeof input.scripts.build === 'string';
93:   const hasTest = typeof input.scripts.test === 'string';
94:   const hasLint = typeof input.scripts.lint === 'string';
95: 
96:   return {
97:     install: installCommand(input.packageManager),
98:     build: hasBuild ? runScriptCommand(input.packageManager, 'build') : null,
99:     test: input.runTestsIfPresent && hasTest ? runScriptCommand(input.packageManager, 'test') : null,
100:     lint: input.runLintIfPresent && hasLint ? runScriptCommand(input.packageManager, 'lint') : null,
101:   };
102: }

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { getSandbox, type Sandbox } from '@cloudflare/sandbox';
2: import type { Env } from '../../types.js';
3: 
4: const APP_DIR = '/root/app';
5: const BUILD_DIRS = ['dist', 'build', '.output', 'out'];
6: 
7: /**
8:  * Deploy built files to Cloudflare Pages using Wrangler
9:  * 
10:  * Note: Only destroys the sandbox on SUCCESS. On failure, the sandbox is kept alive
11:  * so the preview URL remains accessible as a fallback.
12:  */
13: export async function deployToPages(
14:   env: Env,
15:   jobId: string,
16:   sandboxNamespace: DurableObjectNamespace<Sandbox>,
17:   sandboxId: string
18: ): Promise<string> {
19:   const sandbox = getSandbox(sandboxNamespace, sandboxId);
20:   const projectName = env.PAGES_PROJECT_NAME || 'nimbus';
21: 
22:   // Step 1: Find deploy path
23:   const deployPath = await findDeployPath(sandbox);
24: 
25:   // Step 2: Deploy using Wrangler
26:   // Set environment variables for wrangler
27:   // Use npx to ensure wrangler is found (it's installed globally but PATH might not include it)
28:   const wranglerCmd = [
29:     `export CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="${env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN}"`,
30:     `export CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID="${env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID}"`,
31:     `npx wrangler pages deploy "${deployPath}" --project-name="${projectName}" --branch="${jobId}" --commit-message="Nimbus deployment: ${jobId}"`
32:   ].join(' && ');
33: 
34:   const result = await sandbox.exec(wranglerCmd);
35: 
36:   if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
37:     // Sanitize output to avoid leaking tokens in error messages
38:     const sanitizedOutput = (result.stderr || result.stdout)
39:       .replace(/CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="[^"]*"/g, 'CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="[REDACTED]"')
40:       .replace(/CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID="[^"]*"/g, 'CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID="[REDACTED]"');
41:     console.error('[Pages] Wrangler failed:', sanitizedOutput);
42:     // Don't destroy sandbox on failure - keep preview URL alive as fallback
43:     throw new Error(`Wrangler failed with exit code ${result.exitCode}`);
44:   }
45: 
46:   // Parse the deployment URL from wrangler output
47:   // Example output: "✨ Deployment complete! Take a peek over at https://abc123.project.pages.dev"
48:   const urlMatch = result.stdout.match(/https:\/\/[^\s]+\.pages\.dev/);
49:   if (!urlMatch) {
50:     console.error('[Pages] Could not parse URL from output:', result.stdout);
51:     // Don't destroy sandbox on failure - keep preview URL alive as fallback
52:     throw new Error('Could not parse deployment URL from wrangler output');
53:   }
54: 
55:   const deployedUrl = urlMatch[0];
56: 
57:   // Only destroy sandbox on successful deployment
58:   // (Preview URL no longer needed since we have a permanent Pages URL)
59:   try {
60:     await sandbox.destroy();
61:   } catch {
62:     // Ignore destruction errors
63:   }
64: 
65:   return deployedUrl;
66: }
67: 
68: async function findDeployPath(sandbox: ReturnType<typeof getSandbox>): Promise<string> {
69:   for (const dir of BUILD_DIRS) {
70:     const checkPath = `${APP_DIR}/${dir}`;
71:     const result = await sandbox.exec(`test -d ${checkPath} && echo "exists"`);
72:     if (result.stdout.includes('exists')) {
73:       return checkPath;
74:     }
75:   }
76:   return APP_DIR;
77: }

(End of file - total 77 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { getSandbox, type Sandbox } from '@cloudflare/sandbox';
2: import type { GeneratedFile, SSEEvent } from './types.js';
3: 
4: // Re-export Sandbox class for Durable Object binding
5: export { Sandbox } from '@cloudflare/sandbox';
6: 
7: type SSEWriter = (event: SSEEvent) => void;
8: 
9: // Timeouts in milliseconds
10: const INSTALL_TIMEOUT = 300000; // 5 minutes for dependency installation
11: const BUILD_TIMEOUT = 180000; // 3 minutes for build
12: 
13: export interface BuildResult {
14:   previewUrl: string;
15:   sandboxId: string;
16:   installDurationMs: number;
17:   buildDurationMs: number;
18: }
19: 
20: export async function buildInSandbox(
21:   sandboxNamespace: DurableObjectNamespace<Sandbox>,
22:   files: GeneratedFile[],
23:   sendEvent: SSEWriter,
24:   hostname: string
25: ): Promise<BuildResult> {
26:   // Create a unique sandbox instance for this build
27:   const sandboxId = `build-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
28:   const sandbox = getSandbox(sandboxNamespace, sandboxId);
29: 
30:   const APP_DIR = '/root/app';
31: 
32:   try {
33:     // Step 1: Create project directory
34:     sendEvent({ type: 'scaffolding' });
35:     await sandbox.exec(`mkdir -p ${APP_DIR}`);
36: 
37:     // Step 2: Write generated files
38:     sendEvent({ type: 'writing' });
39:     for (const file of files) {
40:       const fullPath = `${APP_DIR}/${file.path}`;
41:       // Ensure parent directory exists
42:       const dir = fullPath.substring(0, fullPath.lastIndexOf('/'));
43:       if (dir && dir !== APP_DIR) {
44:         await sandbox.exec(`mkdir -p ${dir}`);
45:       }
46:       await sandbox.writeFile(fullPath, file.content);
47:     }
48: 
49:     // Check if this is a Node.js project (has package.json)
50:     const hasPackageJson = files.some(f => f.path === 'package.json' || f.path.endsWith('/package.json'));
51: 
52:     let installDurationMs = 0;
53:     let buildDurationMs = 0;
54: 
55:     if (hasPackageJson) {
56:       // Step 3: Install dependencies using bun (much faster than npm)
57:       sendEvent({ type: 'installing' });
58:       const installStart = Date.now();
59: 
60:       // Use bun install - it's 10-100x faster than npm
61:       // Falls back to npm if bun fails
62:       let installResult = await sandbox.exec(`cd ${APP_DIR} && bun install --no-save`, {
63:         timeout: INSTALL_TIMEOUT,
64:       });
65: 
66:       // If bun fails, try npm as fallback
67:       if (installResult.exitCode !== 0) {
68:         installResult = await sandbox.exec(`cd ${APP_DIR} && npm install --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund`, {
69:           timeout: INSTALL_TIMEOUT,
70:         });
71:       }
72: 
73:       installDurationMs = Date.now() - installStart;
74: 
75:       if (installResult.exitCode !== 0) {
76:         const output = [installResult.stdout, installResult.stderr].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
77:         throw new Error(`Install failed (exit ${installResult.exitCode}):\n${output || 'No output'}`);
78:       }
79: 
80:       // Step 4: Build if there's a build script
81:       sendEvent({ type: 'building' });
82:       const packageJsonFile = files.find(f => f.path === 'package.json');
83:       const hasBuildScript = packageJsonFile && packageJsonFile.content.includes('"build"');
84: 
85:       if (hasBuildScript) {
86:         const buildStart = Date.now();
87:         // Use bun run for build - also faster
88:         // For Astro/Vite, need to set CI=true to avoid interactive prompts
89:         let buildResult = await sandbox.exec(`cd ${APP_DIR} && CI=true bun run build`, {
90:           timeout: BUILD_TIMEOUT,
91:         });
92: 
93:         // If bun fails, try npm as fallback
94:         if (buildResult.exitCode !== 0) {
95:           buildResult = await sandbox.exec(`cd ${APP_DIR} && CI=true npm run build`, {
96:             timeout: BUILD_TIMEOUT,
97:           });
98:         }
99: 
100:         buildDurationMs = Date.now() - buildStart;
101: 
102:         if (buildResult.exitCode !== 0) {
103:           // Include both stdout and stderr for better debugging
104:           const output = [buildResult.stdout, buildResult.stderr].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
105:           throw new Error(`Build failed (exit ${buildResult.exitCode}):\n${output || 'No output'}`);
106:         }
107:       }
108:     } else {
109:       // Static HTML/CSS project - no build needed
110:       sendEvent({ type: 'installing' });
111:       sendEvent({ type: 'building' });
112:     }
113: 
114:     // Start HTTP server and expose preview URL
115:     sendEvent({ type: 'starting' });
116: 
117:     // Determine what to serve and how
118:     const packageJsonFile = files.find(f => f.path === 'package.json');
119:     let packageJson: { scripts?: Record<string, string> } = {};
120:     if (packageJsonFile) {
121:       try {
122:         packageJson = JSON.parse(packageJsonFile.content);
123:       } catch {
124:         // Ignore parse errors
125:       }
126:     }
127: 
128:     // Check for preview/start scripts or determine serve directory
129:     const hasPreviewScript = packageJson.scripts?.preview;
130:     const hasStartScript = packageJson.scripts?.start;
131: 
132:     if (hasPreviewScript) {
133:       // Astro, Vite, etc. have a preview script that serves the built output
134:       await sandbox.startProcess(`cd ${APP_DIR} && bun run preview -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080`);
135:     } else if (hasStartScript) {
136:       // Some projects have a start script - try to set port via PORT env var (common convention)
137:       await sandbox.startProcess(`cd ${APP_DIR} && PORT=8080 bun run start`);
138:     } else {
139:       // Static files - use npx serve to serve the appropriate directory
140:       // Check if there's a dist/ or build/ folder after build
141:       const distExists = await sandbox.exec(`test -d ${APP_DIR}/dist && echo "yes" || echo "no"`);
142:       const buildExists = await sandbox.exec(`test -d ${APP_DIR}/build && echo "yes" || echo "no"`);
143: 
144:       let serveDir = APP_DIR;
145:       if (distExists.stdout?.trim() === 'yes') {
146:         serveDir = `${APP_DIR}/dist`;
147:       } else if (buildExists.stdout?.trim() === 'yes') {
148:         serveDir = `${APP_DIR}/build`;
149:       }
150: 
151:       // Use serve for static file serving (installed globally in Dockerfile)
152:       await sandbox.startProcess(`serve ${serveDir} -l 8080 --no-clipboard`);
153:     }
154: 
155:     // Wait for server to start
156:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 2000));
157: 
158:     // Expose the port and get preview URL
159:     const exposed = await sandbox.exposePort(8080, { hostname });
160: 
161:     return {
162:       previewUrl: exposed.url,
163:       sandboxId,
164:       installDurationMs,
165:       buildDurationMs,
166:     };
167:   } catch (error) {
168:     // On error, try to clean up the sandbox
169:     try {
170:       await sandbox.destroy();
171:     } catch {
172:       // Ignore cleanup errors
173:     }
174:     throw error;
175:   }
176: }

(End of file - total 176 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import type {
2:   GeneratedCode,
3:   GeneratedFile,
4:   OpenRouterRequest,
5:   OpenRouterResponse,
6:   OpenRouterGenerationResponse,
7: } from './types.js';
8: 
9: const OPENROUTER_API_URL = 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions';
10: const OPENROUTER_GENERATION_URL = 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation';
11: 
12: /**
13:  * Fetch cost information from OpenRouter's Generation API
14:  * This is the most reliable way to get accurate cost in USD
15:  */
16: async function fetchGenerationCost(apiKey: string, generationId: string): Promise<number> {
17:   // Small delay to ensure the generation is recorded
18:   await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
19: 
20:   const response = await fetch(`${OPENROUTER_GENERATION_URL}?id=${generationId}`, {
21:     method: 'GET',
22:     headers: {
23:       Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
24:     },
25:   });
26: 
27:   if (!response.ok) {
28:     throw new Error(`Failed to fetch generation: ${response.status}`);
29:   }
30: 
31:   const data = (await response.json()) as OpenRouterGenerationResponse;
32:   return data.data.total_cost ?? 0;
33: }
34: 
35: const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are an expert web developer. Generate a complete, working website based on the user's request.
36: 
37: OUTPUT FORMAT:
38: Return ONLY valid JSON with this exact structure:
39: {
40:   "files": [
41:     { "path": "index.html", "content": "..." },
42:     { "path": "styles.css", "content": "..." }
43:   ]
44: }
45: 
46: Include all files needed for the project to work.
47: Do not include markdown explanations, just the JSON.`;
48: 
49: // Result from generateCode including usage metrics
50: export interface GenerateCodeResult {
51:   files: GeneratedFile[];
52:   usage: {
53:     promptTokens: number;
54:     completionTokens: number;
55:     totalTokens: number;
56:     cost: number;
57:   };
58:   llmLatencyMs: number;
59: }
60: 
61: export async function generateCode(
62:   apiKey: string,
63:   model: string,
64:   prompt: string
65: ): Promise<GenerateCodeResult> {
66:   const startTime = Date.now();
67:   const request: OpenRouterRequest = {
68:     model,
69:     messages: [
70:       { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
71:       { role: 'user', content: prompt },
72:     ],
73:     max_tokens: 8192,
74:     temperature: 0.7,
75:   };
76: 
77:   const response = await fetch(OPENROUTER_API_URL, {
78:     method: 'POST',
79:     headers: {
80:       'Content-Type': 'application/json',
81:       Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
82:       'HTTP-Referer': 'https://github.com/dayhaysoos/nimbus',
83:       'X-Title': 'Nimbus LLM Benchmarking',
84:     },
85:     body: JSON.stringify(request),
86:   });
87: 
88:   if (!response.ok) {
89:     const errorText = await response.text();
90:     throw new Error(`OpenRouter API error (${response.status}): ${errorText}`);
91:   }
92: 
93:   const data = (await response.json()) as OpenRouterResponse;
94: 
95:   if (!data.choices || data.choices.length === 0) {
96:     throw new Error('OpenRouter returned no choices');
97:   }
98: 
99:   const content = data.choices[0].message.content;
100: 
101:   // Parse the JSON response from Claude
102:   // Handle potential markdown code blocks
103:   let jsonContent = content.trim();
104:   
105:   // Remove markdown code fences if present
106:   if (jsonContent.startsWith('```json')) {
107:     jsonContent = jsonContent.slice(7);
108:   } else if (jsonContent.startsWith('```')) {
109:     jsonContent = jsonContent.slice(3);
110:   }
111:   if (jsonContent.endsWith('```')) {
112:     jsonContent = jsonContent.slice(0, -3);
113:   }
114:   jsonContent = jsonContent.trim();
115: 
116:   try {
117:     const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonContent) as GeneratedCode;
118: 
119:     if (!parsed.files || !Array.isArray(parsed.files)) {
120:       throw new Error('Invalid response structure: missing files array');
121:     }
122: 
123:     // Validate each file has path and content
124:     for (const file of parsed.files) {
125:       if (typeof file.path !== 'string' || typeof file.content !== 'string') {
126:         throw new Error('Invalid file structure: each file must have path and content strings');
127:       }
128:     }
129: 
130:     const llmLatencyMs = Date.now() - startTime;
131: 
132:     // Get cost - try from direct response first, then query Generation API
133:     let cost = data.usage.cost ?? 0;
134: 
135:     // If cost is 0 or not provided, try to get it from the Generation API
136:     if (cost === 0 && data.id) {
137:       try {
138:         cost = await fetchGenerationCost(apiKey, data.id);
139:       } catch {
140:         // Silently fall back to 0 if we can't get the cost
141:         console.warn('Could not fetch generation cost from OpenRouter API');
142:       }
143:     }
144: 
145:     return {
146:       files: parsed.files,
147:       usage: {
148:         promptTokens: data.usage.prompt_tokens,
149:         completionTokens: data.usage.completion_tokens,
150:         totalTokens: data.usage.total_tokens,
151:         cost,
152:       },
153:       llmLatencyMs,
154:     };
155:   } catch (parseError) {
156:     throw new Error(
157:       `Failed to parse LLM response as JSON: ${parseError instanceof Error ? parseError.message : String(parseError)}\n\nRaw content:\n${content.slice(0, 500)}...`
158:     );
159:   }
160: }

(End of file - total 160 lines)

<content>1: import * as p from '@clack/prompts';
2: import { getWorkerUrl, createJob, parseSSE } from '../lib/api.js';
3: import { showModelPicker, getDefaultModel } from '../lib/models.js';
4: import { printSummary } from '../lib/summary.js';
5: import { saveReport } from '../lib/report.js';
6: import type { SSEEvent } from '../lib/types.js';
7: 
8: interface StartOptions {
9:   model?: string;
10: }
11: 
12: /**
13:  * Start command - creates a new job and streams progress
14:  */
15: export async function startCommand(prompt: string, options: StartOptions): Promise<void> {
16:   const workerUrl = getWorkerUrl();
17: 
18:   if (!workerUrl) {
19:     p.log.error('NIMBUS_WORKER_URL environment variable is required.');
20:     p.log.info('Set it to your self-hosted Nimbus worker URL.');
21:     p.log.info('');
22:     p.log.info('Example:');
23:     p.log.info('  NIMBUS_WORKER_URL=https://your-worker.com nimbus start "your prompt"');
24:     p.log.info('');
25:     p.log.info('Self-hosting guide: https://github.com/dayhaysoos/nimbus#self-hosting-guide');
26:     process.exit(1);
27:   }
28: 
29:   // Get model - either from flag or show picker
30:   let model: string;
31:   if (options.model) {
32:     model = options.model;
33:   } else {
34:     const selected = await showModelPicker();
35:     model = selected;
36:   }
37: 
38:   const spinner = p.spinner();
39:   let jobId: string | undefined;
40: 
41:   try {
42:     spinner.start('Creating job...');
43: 
44:     const response = await createJob(workerUrl, prompt, model);
45: 
46:     if (!response.body) {
47:       throw new Error('No response body from worker');
48:     }
49: 
50:     const reader = response.body.getReader();
51: 
52:     for await (const event of parseSSE(reader)) {
53:       await handleEvent(event, spinner, (id) => {
54:         jobId = id;
55:       });
56:     }
57:   } catch (error) {
58:     spinner.stop('Failed');
59:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
60:     p.log.error(message);
61: 
62:     if (jobId) {
63:       p.log.info(`Job ID: ${jobId}`);
64:       p.log.info(`Check status with: nimbus watch ${jobId}`);
65:     }
66: 
67:     process.exit(1);
68:   }
69: }
70: 
71: /**
72:  * Handle SSE events and update UI
73:  */
74: async function handleEvent(
75:   event: SSEEvent,
76:   spinner: ReturnType<typeof p.spinner>,
77:   setJobId: (id: string) => void
78: ): Promise<void> {
79:   switch (event.type) {
80:     case 'job_created':
81:       setJobId(event.jobId);
82:       spinner.stop(`Job created: ${event.jobId}`);
83:       spinner.start('Sending to LLM...');
84:       break;
85: 
86:     case 'generating':
87:       spinner.message('Generating code...');
88:       break;
89: 
90:     case 'generated':
91:       spinner.stop(`Generated ${event.fileCount} files`);
92:       spinner.start('Building in sandbox...');
93:       break;
94: 
95:     case 'scaffolding':
96:       spinner.message('Scaffolding project...');
97:       break;
98: 
99:     case 'writing':
100:       spinner.message('Writing generated files...');
101:       break;
102: 
103:     case 'installing':
104:       spinner.message('Running npm install...');
105:       break;
106: 
107:     case 'building':
108:       spinner.message('Running npm build...');
109:       break;
110: 
111:     case 'starting':
112:       spinner.stop('Build complete');
113:       spinner.start('Starting preview server...');
114:       break;
115: 
116:     case 'preview_ready':
117:       spinner.stop('Preview ready');
118:       p.log.info(`Preview: ${event.previewUrl}`);
119:       break;
120: 
121:     case 'deploying':
122:       spinner.start('Uploading to Cloudflare Pages...');
123:       break;
124: 
125:     case 'deploy_warning':
126:       spinner.stop('Pages deployment failed');
127:       p.log.warning(event.message);
128:       p.log.info('Falling back to preview URL (temporary)');
129:       break;
130: 
131:     case 'deployed':
132:       spinner.stop('Deployment complete');
133:       break;
134: 
135:     case 'complete': {
136:       spinner.stop('Done');
137: 
138:       // Print summary box
139:       printSummary(event.metrics);
140: 
141:       // Generate and save report
142:       try {
143:         const filename = await saveReport(event.metrics);
144:         console.log('');
145:         console.log(`Report saved: ./${filename}`);
146:         console.log('');
147:       } catch (error) {
148:         const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
149:         p.log.warning(`Failed to save report: ${message}`);
150:       }
151: 
152:       // Print final URL
153:       if (event.isPreviewFallback) {
154:         p.outro(`Preview (temporary): ${event.deployedUrl}`);
155:       } else {
156:         p.outro(`Deployed: ${event.deployedUrl}`);
157:       }
158:       process.exit(0);
159:       break;
160:     }
161: 
162:     case 'error':
163:       spinner.stop('Failed');
164:       p.log.error(event.message);
165:       process.exit(1);
166:   }
167: }

(End of file - total 167 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import * as p from '@clack/prompts';
2: import { getWorkerUrl, listJobs } from '../lib/api.js';
3: import { getShortModelName } from '../lib/models.js';
4: import type { JobListItem, JobStatus } from '../lib/types.js';
5: 
6: /**
7:  * List command - shows all past jobs
8:  */
9: export async function listCommand(): Promise<void> {
10:   const workerUrl = getWorkerUrl();
11: 
12:   if (!workerUrl) {
13:     p.log.error('NIMBUS_WORKER_URL environment variable is required.');
14:     process.exit(1);
15:   }
16: 
17:   const spinner = p.spinner();
18: 
19:   try {
20:     spinner.start('Fetching jobs...');
21: 
22:     const { jobs } = await listJobs(workerUrl);
23: 
24:     spinner.stop('Jobs retrieved');
25: 
26:     if (jobs.length === 0) {
27:       p.log.info('No jobs found. Create one with: nimbus start "your prompt"');
28:       return;
29:     }
30: 
31:     // Print table header
32:     console.log('');
33:     console.log(formatHeader());
34:     console.log(formatSeparator());
35: 
36:     // Print each job
37:     for (const job of jobs) {
38:       console.log(formatJob(job));
39:     }
40: 
41:     console.log('');
42:     p.log.info(`${jobs.length} job(s) total`);
43:   } catch (error) {
44:     spinner.stop('Failed');
45:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
46:     p.log.error(message);
47:     process.exit(1);
48:   }
49: }
50: 
51: /**
52:  * Format table header
53:  */
54: function formatHeader(): string {
55:   const id = 'ID'.padEnd(14);
56:   const status = 'Status'.padEnd(12);
57:   const model = 'Model'.padEnd(18);
58:   const created = 'Created'.padEnd(14);
59:   const url = 'URL';
60: 
61:   return `  ${id} ${status} ${model} ${created} ${url}`;
62: }
63: 
64: /**
65:  * Format separator line
66:  */
67: function formatSeparator(): string {
68:   return '  ' + '-'.repeat(80);
69: }
70: 
71: /**
72:  * Format a job row
73:  */
74: function formatJob(job: JobListItem): string {
75:   const id = job.id.padEnd(14);
76:   const status = formatStatus(job.status).padEnd(12);
77:   const model = getShortModelName(job.model).slice(0, 16).padEnd(18);
78:   const created = formatRelativeTime(job.createdAt).padEnd(14);
79:   const url = job.deployedUrl || '-';
80: 
81:   return `  ${id} ${status} ${model} ${created} ${url}`;
82: }
83: 
84: /**
85:  * Format job status with color indicator
86:  */
87: function formatStatus(status: JobStatus): string {
88:   const indicators: Record<JobStatus, string> = {
89:     queued: '[ ] queued',
90:     running: '[~] running',
91:     completed: '[+] completed',
92:     failed: '[x] failed',
93:     cancelled: '[-] cancelled',
94:   };
95:   return indicators[status] || status;
96: }
97: 
98: /**
99:  * Format ISO date as relative time
100:  */
101: function formatRelativeTime(isoDate: string): string {
102:   const date = new Date(isoDate);
103:   const now = new Date();
104:   const diffMs = now.getTime() - date.getTime();
105:   const diffSeconds = Math.floor(diffMs / 1000);
106:   const diffMinutes = Math.floor(diffSeconds / 60);
107:   const diffHours = Math.floor(diffMinutes / 60);
108:   const diffDays = Math.floor(diffHours / 24);
109: 
110:   if (diffSeconds < 60) {
111:     return 'just now';
112:   } else if (diffMinutes < 60) {
113:     return `${diffMinutes}m ago`;
114:   } else if (diffHours < 24) {
115:     return `${diffHours}h ago`;
116:   } else if (diffDays < 7) {
117:     return `${diffDays}d ago`;
118:   } else {
119:     return date.toLocaleDateString();
120:   }
121: }

(End of file - total 121 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import * as p from '@clack/prompts';
2: import { getWorkerUrl, getJob } from '../lib/api.js';
3: import { getShortModelName } from '../lib/models.js';
4: import type { JobResponse, JobStatus } from '../lib/types.js';
5: 
6: const POLL_INTERVAL = 2000; // 2 seconds
7: const MAX_POLL_COUNT = 150; // 5 minutes max (150 * 2s)
8: 
9: /**
10:  * Watch command - poll job status until completion
11:  */
12: export async function watchCommand(jobId: string): Promise<void> {
13:   const workerUrl = getWorkerUrl();
14: 
15:   if (!workerUrl) {
16:     p.log.error('NIMBUS_WORKER_URL environment variable is required.');
17:     process.exit(1);
18:   }
19: 
20:   const spinner = p.spinner();
21:   let lastStatus: JobStatus | undefined;
22:   let pollCount = 0;
23: 
24:   try {
25:     spinner.start(`Watching job ${jobId}...`);
26: 
27:     while (pollCount < MAX_POLL_COUNT) {
28:       const job = await getJob(workerUrl, jobId);
29:       pollCount++;
30: 
31:       // Update spinner based on status
32:       if (job.status !== lastStatus) {
33:         lastStatus = job.status;
34:         updateSpinner(spinner, job);
35:       }
36: 
37:       // Check for terminal states
38:       if (job.status === 'completed') {
39:         spinner.stop('Job completed');
40:         displayJobResult(job);
41:         process.exit(0);
42:       }
43: 
44:       if (job.status === 'failed') {
45:         spinner.stop('Job failed');
46:         displayJobError(job);
47:         process.exit(1);
48:       }
49: 
50:       if (job.status === 'cancelled') {
51:         spinner.stop('Job cancelled');
52:         displayJobCancelled(job);
53:         process.exit(1);
54:       }
55: 
56:       // Wait before polling again
57:       await sleep(POLL_INTERVAL);
58:     }
59: 
60:     // Timeout reached
61:     spinner.stop('Timeout');
62:     p.log.warning(`Job ${jobId} is still ${lastStatus || 'queued'} after 5 minutes.`);
63:     p.log.info('The job may still be running. Check again later with:');
64:     p.log.info(`  nimbus watch ${jobId}`);
65:     process.exit(1);
66:   } catch (error) {
67:     spinner.stop('Failed');
68:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
69:     p.log.error(message);
70:     process.exit(1);
71:   }
72: }
73: 
74: /**
75:  * Update spinner message based on job status
76:  */
77: function updateSpinner(spinner: ReturnType<typeof p.spinner>, job: JobResponse): void {
78:   switch (job.status) {
79:     case 'queued':
80:       spinner.message('Job is queued...');
81:       break;
82:     case 'running':
83:       spinner.message('Job is running...');
84:       break;
85:     case 'cancelled':
86:       spinner.message('Job was cancelled.');
87:       break;
88:   }
89: }
90: 
91: /**
92:  * Display completed job result
93:  */
94: function displayJobResult(job: JobResponse): void {
95:   console.log('');
96:   p.log.success('Build completed successfully!');
97:   console.log('');
98: 
99:   if (job.previewUrl) {

164: /**

167: function calculateDuration(start: string, end: string): string {
168:   const startDate = new Date(start);
169:   const endDate = new Date(end);
170:   const diffMs = endDate.getTime() - startDate.getTime();
171:   const diffSeconds = Math.floor(diffMs / 1000);
172:   const diffMinutes = Math.floor(diffSeconds / 60);
173: 
174:   if (diffSeconds < 60) {
175:     return `${diffSeconds}s`;
176:   } else if (diffMinutes < 60) {
177:     const remainingSeconds = diffSeconds % 60;
178:     return `${diffMinutes}m ${remainingSeconds}s`;
179:   } else {
180:     const hours = Math.floor(diffMinutes / 60);
181:     const remainingMinutes = diffMinutes % 60;
182:     return `${hours}h ${remainingMinutes}m`;
183:   }
184: }

188:  */
189: function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {

329: function printQueuedCheckpointSummary(
330:   summary: DryRunSummary,

333:     status: string;
334:     phase: string;

342:   console.log(`  Phase:          ${created.phase}`);
343:   console.log(`  Commit SHA:     ${summary.commitSha}`);
344:   console.log(`  Checkpoint ID:  ${summary.checkpointId ?? 'none'}`);
345:   console.log(`  Project Root:   ${summary.projectRoot}`);
346:   console.log(`  Job URL:        ${created.jobUrl}`);
347:   console.log(`  Events URL:     ${created.eventsUrl}`);

350: export async function deployCheckpointCommand(
351:   checkpointOrCommitish: string,
352:   options: DeployCheckpointOptions
353: ): Promise<void> {
354:   const spinner = p.spinner();
355:   spinner.start('Resolving checkpoint source and validating environment...');
356: 
357:   try {
358:     const summary = buildDryRunSummary(checkpointOrCommitish, options);
359: 
360:     if (options.dryRun) {
361:       spinner.stop('Dry run complete');
362:       printDryRunSummary(summary);
363:       return;
364:     }
365: 
366:     assertRequiredEnvKeysResolved(summary);
367: 
368:     const workerUrl = getWorkerUrl();
369:     if (!workerUrl) {
370:       throw new Error('NIMBUS_WORKER_URL environment variable is required for checkpoint job creation.');
371:     }
372: 
373:     spinner.message('Creating source archive from commit...');
374:     const sourceArchive = createSourceArchiveFromCommit(summary.commitSha);
375: 
376:     spinner.message('Uploading source bundle and creating checkpoint job...');
377:     const metadata = buildCheckpointCreateMetadata({
378:       checkpointId: summary.checkpointId,
379:       commitSha: summary.commitSha,
380:       ref: options.ref ?? summary.branchRef ?? undefined,
381:       projectRoot: summary.projectRoot,
382:       runTestsIfPresent: options.runTestsIfPresent,
383:       runLintIfPresent: options.runLintIfPresent,
384:     });
385: 
386:     const formData = buildCheckpointCreateFormData(
387:       metadata,
388:       sourceArchive,
389:       buildSourceBundleFilename(summary.commitSha)
390:     );
391: 
392:     const created = await createCheckpointJob(workerUrl, formData);
393: 
394:     spinner.stop('Checkpoint job created');
395:     printQueuedCheckpointSummary(summary, created);
396: 
397:     if (options.watch) {
398:       p.log.info('Live checkpoint watch SSE output will be added in a later slice.');
399:       p.log.info(`You can poll status now: nimbus watch ${created.jobId}`);
400:     }
401:   } catch (error) {
402:     spinner.stop('Checkpoint deploy failed');
403:     throw error;
404:   }
405: }

12: type FlagValue = string | boolean | string[];
13: type FlagRecord = Record<string, FlagValue>;
14: 
15: function readStringFlag(flags: FlagRecord, key: string): string | undefined {
16:   const raw = flags[key];
17:   if (typeof raw === 'string') {
18:     const value = raw.trim();
19:     return value ? value : undefined;
20:   }
21: 
22:   if (Array.isArray(raw)) {
23:     for (let index = raw.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {
24:       const value = raw[index].trim();
25:       if (value) {
26:         return value;
27:       }
28:     }
29: 
30:     return undefined;
31:   }
32: 
33:   return undefined;
34: }
35: 
36: function toFlagEntries(value: string | string[] | undefined): string[] {
37:   if (value === undefined) {
38:     return [];
39:   }
40: 
41:   const values = Array.isArray(value) ? value : [value];
42:   return values.map((entry) => entry.trim()).filter(Boolean);
43: }
44: 
45: function splitCommaEntries(entries: string[]): string[] {
46:   return entries.flatMap((entry) => entry.split(',')).map((entry) => entry.trim()).filter(Boolean);
47: }
48: 
49: function parseExplicitEnvValues(entries: string[]): Map<string, string> {
50:   const values = new Map<string, string>();
51: 
52:   for (const entry of entries) {
53:     const splitIndex = entry.indexOf('=');
54:     if (splitIndex <= 0) {
55:       throw new Error(`Invalid --env entry "${entry}". Expected KEY=VALUE.`);
56:     }
57: 
58:     const key = entry.slice(0, splitIndex).trim();
59:     const value = entry.slice(splitIndex + 1).trim();
60: 
61:     if (!/^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/.test(key)) {
62:       throw new Error(`Invalid --env key "${key}" in entry "${entry}".`);
63:     }
64: 
65:     values.set(key, value);
66:   }
67: 
68:   return values;
69: }
70: 
71: export function resolveDeployCheckpointOptions(flags: FlagRecord): DeployCheckpointOptions {
72:   const envFlag = flags.env;
73:   const envEntries = toFlagEntries(
74:     Array.isArray(envFlag) ? envFlag : typeof envFlag === 'string' ? envFlag : undefined
75:   );
76: 
77:   const envFileFlag = flags['env-file'];
78:   const envFiles = splitCommaEntries(
79:     toFlagEntries(
80:     Array.isArray(envFileFlag) ? envFileFlag : typeof envFileFlag === 'string' ? envFileFlag : undefined
81:     )
82:   );
83: 
84:   return {
85:     ref: readStringFlag(flags, 'ref'),
86:     projectRoot: readStringFlag(flags, 'project-root'),
87:     runTestsIfPresent: !Boolean(flags['no-tests']),
88:     runLintIfPresent: !Boolean(flags['no-lint']),
89:     watch: !Boolean(flags['no-watch']),
90:     dryRun: !Boolean(flags['no-dry-run']),
91:     envFiles,
92:     explicitEnv: parseExplicitEnvValues(envEntries),
93:   };

31:     const lines = buffer.split('\n\n');
32:     buffer = lines.pop() || ''; // Keep incomplete message in buffer
33: 
34:     for (const line of lines) {
35:       if (line.startsWith('data: ')) {
36:         try {
37:           const data = JSON.parse(line.slice(6)) as SSEEvent;
38:           yield data;

42:       }
43:     }
44:   }

<content>1: export interface ParsedCliArgs {
2:   command: string | null;
3:   flags: Record<string, string | boolean | string[]>;
4:   positional: string[];
5: }
6: 
7: const LONG_VALUE_FLAGS = new Set(['model', 'ref', 'project-root', 'env-file', 'env']);
8: const SHORT_VALUE_FLAGS = new Set(['m']);
9: 
10: function appendFlagValue(
11:   flags: Record<string, string | boolean | string[]>,
12:   key: string,
13:   value: string | boolean
14: ): void {
15:   const existing = flags[key];
16:   if (existing === undefined) {
17:     flags[key] = value;
18:     return;
19:   }
20: 
21:   if (typeof value === 'boolean') {
22:     flags[key] = value;
23:     return;
24:   }
25: 
26:   if (Array.isArray(existing)) {
27:     existing.push(value);
28:     flags[key] = existing;
29:     return;
30:   }
31: 
32:   if (typeof existing === 'string') {
33:     flags[key] = [existing, value];
34:     return;
35:   }
36: 
37:   flags[key] = value;
38: }
39: 
40: export function parseArgs(args: string[]): ParsedCliArgs {
41:   const flags: Record<string, string | boolean | string[]> = {};
42:   const positional: string[] = [];
43:   let command: string | null = null;
44: 
45:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
46:     const arg = args[i];
47: 
48:     if (arg.startsWith('--')) {
49:       const key = arg.slice(2);
50: 
51:       if (LONG_VALUE_FLAGS.has(key)) {
52:         if (i + 1 < args.length && !args[i + 1].startsWith('-')) {
53:           appendFlagValue(flags, key, args[++i]);
54:           continue;
55:         }
56: 
57:         throw new Error(`Missing value for --${key}`);
58:       } else {
59:         appendFlagValue(flags, key, true);
60:       }
61:     } else if (arg.startsWith('-')) {
62:       const key = arg.slice(1);
63: 
64:       if (SHORT_VALUE_FLAGS.has(key)) {
65:         if (i + 1 < args.length && !args[i + 1].startsWith('-')) {
66:           if (key === 'm') {
67:             appendFlagValue(flags, 'model', args[++i]);
68:           } else {
69:             appendFlagValue(flags, key, args[++i]);
70:           }
71: 
72:           continue;
73:         }
74: 
75:         throw new Error(`Missing value for -${key}`);
76:       } else if (key === 'h') {
77:         appendFlagValue(flags, 'help', true);
78:       } else if (key === 'v') {
79:         appendFlagValue(flags, 'version', true);
80:       } else {
81:         appendFlagValue(flags, key, true);
82:       }
83:     } else if (!command) {
84:       command = arg;
85:     } else {
86:       positional.push(arg);
87:     }
88:   }
89: 
90:   return { command, flags, positional };
91: }

<content>1: const CHECKPOINT_ID_REGEX = /^[a-f0-9]{12}$/i;
2: 
3: export type ParsedDeployInput =
4:   | { kind: 'checkpoint'; checkpointId: string; explicit: boolean }
5:   | { kind: 'commit'; commitish: string };
6: 
7: export interface ParsedTrailers {
8:   checkpointId: string | null;
9:   entireAttribution: string | null;
10:   entireSessionId: string | null;
11: }
12: 
13: export interface CommitHistoryEntry {
14:   sha: string;
15:   message: string;
16: }
17: 
18: export interface CheckpointResolutionFromHistory {
19:   selected: {
20:     sha: string;
21:     trailers: ParsedTrailers;
22:   };
23:   matchCount: number;
24:   matchedShas: string[];
25: }
26: 
27: export interface ProjectRootCandidate {
28:   path: string;
29:   signals: string[];
30: }
31: 
32: export interface TreeFileEntry {
33:   path: string;
34:   content?: string;
35: }
36: 
37: const LOCKFILE_NAMES = new Set([
38:   'pnpm-lock.yaml',
39:   'package-lock.json',
40:   'yarn.lock',
41:   'bun.lock',
42:   'bun.lockb',
43: ]);
44: 
45: const FRAMEWORK_CONFIG_REGEXES = [
46:   /^(next|nuxt|astro|vite|svelte|remix)\.config(\.[a-z]+)?$/,
47:   /^angular\.json$/,
48:   /^gatsby-config\.[a-z]+$/,
49: ];
50: 
51: export function isCheckpointId(value: string): boolean {
52:   return CHECKPOINT_ID_REGEX.test(value);
53: }
54: 
55: export function parseDeployInput(input: string): ParsedDeployInput {
56:   const raw = input.trim();
57:   if (!raw) {
58:     throw new Error('Missing checkpoint ID or commit-ish input');
59:   }
60: 
61:   if (raw.toLowerCase().startsWith('checkpoint:')) {
62:     const checkpointId = raw.slice('checkpoint:'.length).trim();
63:     if (!isCheckpointId(checkpointId)) {
64:       throw new Error('Checkpoint ID must be 12 hexadecimal characters');
65:     }
66:     return { kind: 'checkpoint', checkpointId: checkpointId.toLowerCase(), explicit: true };
67:   }
68: 
69:   if (isCheckpointId(raw)) {
70:     return { kind: 'checkpoint', checkpointId: raw.toLowerCase(), explicit: false };
71:   }
72: 
73:   if (raw.toLowerCase().startsWith('commit:')) {
74:     const commitish = raw.slice('commit:'.length).trim();
75:     if (!commitish) {
76:       throw new Error('Missing commit-ish after commit: prefix');
77:     }
78:     return { kind: 'commit', commitish };
79:   }
80: 
81:   return { kind: 'commit', commitish: raw };
82: }
83: 
84: function extractLastTrailerValue(message: string, key: 'Entire-Checkpoint' | 'Entire-Attribution'): string | null {
85:   const lines = message.split(/\r?\n/);
86:   let found: string | null = null;
87: 
88:   for (const line of lines) {
89:     const match = line.match(/^([A-Za-z0-9-]+):\s*(.*)$/);
90:     if (!match) {
91:       continue;
92:     }
93: 
94:     if (match[1].toLowerCase() === key.toLowerCase()) {
95:       found = match[2].trim();
96:     }
97:   }
98: 
99:   return found;
100: }
101: 
102: export function extractEntireSessionId(entireAttribution: string | null): string | null {
103:   if (!entireAttribution) {
104:     return null;
105:   }
106: 
107:   const value = entireAttribution.trim();
108:   if (!value) {
109:     return null;
110:   }
111: 
112:   try {
113:     const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
114:     if (parsed && typeof parsed === 'object') {
115:       const obj = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
116:       const direct = obj.sessionId;
117:       if (typeof direct === 'string' && direct.trim()) {
118:         return direct.trim();
119:       }
120: 
121:       const snake = obj.session_id;
122:       if (typeof snake === 'string' && snake.trim()) {
123:         return snake.trim();
124:       }
125:     }
126:   } catch {
127:     // Not JSON, continue with regex extraction.
128:   }
129: 
130:   const kvMatch = value.match(/session[_-]?id\s*[:=]\s*([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)/i);
131:   if (kvMatch) {
132:     return kvMatch[1];
133:   }
134: 
135:   return null;
136: }
137: 
138: export function parseCommitTrailers(message: string): ParsedTrailers {
139:   const checkpointIdRaw = extractLastTrailerValue(message, 'Entire-Checkpoint');
140:   const checkpointId = checkpointIdRaw && isCheckpointId(checkpointIdRaw) ? checkpointIdRaw.toLowerCase() : null;
141:   const entireAttribution = extractLastTrailerValue(message, 'Entire-Attribution');
142: 
143:   return {
144:     checkpointId,
145:     entireAttribution,
146:     entireSessionId: extractEntireSessionId(entireAttribution),
147:   };
148: }
149: 
150: export function resolveCheckpointFromHistory(
151:   checkpointId: string,
152:   commitsInPriorityOrder: CommitHistoryEntry[]
153: ): CheckpointResolutionFromHistory {
154:   const normalizedCheckpointId = checkpointId.toLowerCase();
155:   const matches: Array<{ sha: string; trailers: ParsedTrailers }> = [];
156: 
157:   for (const commit of commitsInPriorityOrder) {
158:     const trailers = parseCommitTrailers(commit.message);
159:     if (trailers.checkpointId === normalizedCheckpointId) {
160:       matches.push({ sha: commit.sha, trailers });
161:     }
162:   }
163: 
164:   if (matches.length === 0) {
165:     throw new Error(`No commit found with trailer Entire-Checkpoint: ${normalizedCheckpointId}`);
166:   }
167: 
168:   return {
169:     selected: matches[0],
170:     matchCount: matches.length,
171:     matchedShas: matches.map((match) => match.sha),
172:   };
173: }
174: 
175: export function selectProjectRoot(candidates: ProjectRootCandidate[]): ProjectRootCandidate {
176:   if (candidates.length === 0) {
177:     throw new Error('No deployable project root detected. Pass --project-root to choose a directory.');
178:   }
179: 
180:   if (candidates.length > 1) {
181:     const details = candidates.map((candidate) => `${candidate.path} (${candidate.signals.join(', ')})`).join('; ');
182:     throw new Error(
183:       `Multiple deployable project roots detected. Pass --project-root to disambiguate. Candidates: ${details}`
184:     );
185:   }
186: 
187:   return candidates[0];
188: }
189: 
190: function getDirectory(path: string): string {
191:   const normalized = path.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/^\.\//, '');
192:   if (!normalized.includes('/')) {
193:     return '.';
194:   }
195: 
196:   return normalized.slice(0, normalized.lastIndexOf('/'));
197: }
198: 
199: function getFileName(path: string): string {
200:   const normalized = path.replace(/\\/g, '/');
201:   const parts = normalized.split('/');
202:   return parts[parts.length - 1];
203: }
204: 
205: function hasBuildScript(packageJsonContent: string | undefined): boolean {
206:   if (!packageJsonContent) {
207:     return false;
208:   }
209: 
210:   try {
211:     const parsed = JSON.parse(packageJsonContent) as unknown;
212:     if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object') {
213:       return false;
214:     }
215: 
216:     const scripts = (parsed as Record<string, unknown>).scripts;
217:     if (!scripts || typeof scripts !== 'object') {
218:       return false;
219:     }
220: 
221:     return typeof (scripts as Record<string, unknown>).build === 'string';
222:   } catch {
223:     return false;
224:   }
225: }
226: 
227: export function detectProjectRootCandidates(treeFiles: TreeFileEntry[]): ProjectRootCandidate[] {
228:   const signalMap = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
229: 
230:   for (const file of treeFiles) {
231:     const fileName = getFileName(file.path);
232:     const directory = getDirectory(file.path);
233: 
234:     const signals = signalMap.get(directory) ?? new Set<string>();
235: 
236:     if (fileName === 'package.json') {
237:       signals.add('package_json');

48: function parseKeyValueLine(line: string): { key: string; value: string; comment: string } | null {

51:     return null;

54:   const withoutExport = trimmed.startsWith('export ') ? trimmed.slice('export '.length).trim() : trimmed;

56:   if (equalIndex < 0) {

73: function splitValueAndComment(rawValue: string): { value: string; comment: string } {
74:   let inSingleQuote = false;
75:   let inDoubleQuote = false;
76:   let escapeNext = false;
77: 
78:   for (let index = 0; index < rawValue.length; index++) {
79:     const char = rawValue[index];

81:     if (escapeNext) {

83:       continue;
84:     }
85: 
86:     if (char === '\\' && inDoubleQuote) {
87:       escapeNext = true;
88:       continue;

91:     if (char === "'" && !inDoubleQuote) {
92:       inSingleQuote = !inSingleQuote;

97:       inDoubleQuote = !inDoubleQuote;

168: export function parseEnvFileContent(content: string): Map<string, string> {
169:   const values = new Map<string, string>();
170: 
171:   for (const line of content.split(/\r?\n/)) {
172:     const parsed = parseKeyValueLine(line);

177:     values.set(parsed.key, parseValue(parsed.value));

179:

181: }
182: 
183: export function collectEnvTemplates(templateFiles: EnvTemplateFile[]): ParsedEnvTemplate {

249:     .join('\u0001');

# Cloudflare Sandbox base image with Node.js, npm, git, etc.
FROM docker.io/cloudflare/sandbox:0.1.3

# Install wrangler globally for Pages deployment

# Install serve globally for static file serving

CMD ["bun", "index.ts"]

29:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
30: 
31:   if (normalized === 'true' || normalized === '1' || normalized === 'yes' || normalized === 'on') {
32:     return true;
33:   }
34: 
35:   if (normalized === 'false' || normalized === '0' || normalized === 'no' || normalized === 'off') {
36:     return false;
37:   }
38: 
39:   return fallback;
40: }
41: 
42: function parseInteger(
43:   value: string | undefined,
44:   fallback: number,
45:   options?: { min?: number; max?: number }
46: ): number {
47:   if (value === undefined) {
48:     return fallback;
49:   }
50: 
51:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(value.trim(), 10);
52:   if (Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
53:     return fallback;
54:   }
55: 
56:   if (options?.min !== undefined && parsed < options.min) {
57:     return options.min;
58:   }
59: 
60:   if (options?.max !== undefined && parsed > options.max) {
61:     return options.max;
62:   }
63: 
64:   return parsed;
65: }
66: 
67: function normalizeRuntimeFlags(flags: RuntimeFlags): RuntimeFlags {
68:   return {
69:     ...flags,
70:     totalTimeoutMs: Math.max(flags.totalTimeoutMs, flags.attemptTimeoutMs),
71:   };
72: }
73: 
74: export function getRuntimeFlagsFromEnv(env: Env): RuntimeFlags {
75:   const resolved: RuntimeFlags = {
76:     v2Enabled: parseBoolean(env.V2_ENABLED, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.v2Enabled),
77:     v2CodeBrowserEnabled: parseBoolean(
78:       env.V2_CODE_BROWSER_ENABLED,
79:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.v2CodeBrowserEnabled
80:     ),
81:     maxAttempts: parseInteger(env.MAX_ATTEMPTS, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.maxAttempts, { min: 1, max: 10 }),
82:     attemptTimeoutMs: parseInteger(
83:       env.ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_MS,
84:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.attemptTimeoutMs,
85:       { min: 60000 }
86:     ),
87:     totalTimeoutMs: parseInteger(env.TOTAL_TIMEOUT_MS, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.totalTimeoutMs, {
88:       min: 60000,
89:     }),
90:     idempotencyTtlHours: parseInteger(
91:       env.IDEMPOTENCY_TTL_HOURS,
92:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.idempotencyTtlHours,
93:       { min: 1, max: 168 }
94:     ),
95:     maxRepairCycles: parseInteger(
96:       env.MAX_REPAIR_CYCLES,
97:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.maxRepairCycles,
98:       { min: 0, max: 10 }
99:     ),
100:     lintBlocking: parseBoolean(env.LINT_BLOCKING, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.lintBlocking),
101:     testBlocking: parseBoolean(env.TEST_BLOCKING, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.testBlocking),
102:     safeInstallIgnoreScripts: parseBoolean(
103:       env.SAFE_INSTALL_IGNORE_SCRIPTS,
104:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.safeInstallIgnoreScripts
105:     ),
106:     autoInstallScriptsFallback: parseBoolean(
107:       env.AUTO_INSTALL_SCRIPTS_FALLBACK,
108:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.autoInstallScriptsFallback
109:     ),
110:     rawRetentionDays: parseInteger(
111:       env.RAW_RETENTION_DAYS,
112:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.rawRetentionDays,
113:       { min: 1 }
114:     ),
115:     summaryRetentionDays: parseInteger(
116:       env.SUMMARY_RETENTION_DAYS,
117:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.summaryRetentionDays,
118:       { min: 1 }
119:     ),
120:   };
121: 
122:   return normalizeRuntimeFlags(resolved);
123: }
124: 
125: export function mergeRuntimeFlagOverrides(
126:   base: RuntimeFlags,
127:   rows: RuntimeFlagRow[]
128: ): RuntimeFlags {
129:   const merged: RuntimeFlags = { ...base };
130: 
131:   for (const row of rows) {
132:     switch (row.key) {
133:       case 'v2_enabled':
134:         merged.v2Enabled = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.v2Enabled);
135:         break;
136:       case 'v2_code_browser_enabled':
137:         merged.v2CodeBrowserEnabled = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.v2CodeBrowserEnabled);
138:         break;
139:       case 'max_attempts':
140:         merged.maxAttempts = parseInteger(row.value, merged.maxAttempts, { min: 1, max: 10 });
141:         break;
142:       case 'attempt_timeout_ms':
143:         merged.attemptTimeoutMs = parseInteger(row.value, merged.attemptTimeoutMs, { min: 60000 });
144:         break;
145:       case 'total_timeout_ms':
146:         merged.totalTimeoutMs = parseInteger(row.value, merged.totalTimeoutMs, { min: 60000 });
147:         break;
148:       case 'idempotency_ttl_hours':
149:         merged.idempotencyTtlHours = parseInteger(row.value, merged.idempotencyTtlHours, {
150:           min: 1,
151:           max: 168,
152:         });
153:         break;
154:       case 'max_repair_cycles':
155:         merged.maxRepairCycles = parseInteger(row.value, merged.maxRepairCycles, {
156:           min: 0,
157:           max: 10,
158:         });
159:         break;
160:       case 'lint_blocking':
161:         merged.lintBlocking = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.lintBlocking);
162:         break;
163:       case 'test_blocking':
164:         merged.testBlocking = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.testBlocking);
165:         break;
166:       case 'safe_install_ignore_scripts':
167:         merged.safeInstallIgnoreScripts = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.safeInstallIgnoreScripts);
168:         break;
169:       case 'auto_install_scripts_fallback':
170:         merged.autoInstallScriptsFallback = parseBoolean(
171:           row.value,
172:           merged.autoInstallScriptsFallback
173:         );
174:         break;
175:       case 'raw_retention_days':
176:         merged.rawRetentionDays = parseInteger(row.value, merged.rawRetentionDays, { min: 1 });
177:         break;
178:       case 'summary_retention_days':
179:         merged.summaryRetentionDays = parseInteger(row.value, merged.summaryRetentionDays, { min: 1 });
180:         break;
181:       default:
182:         break;
183:     }
184:   }
185: 
186:   return normalizeRuntimeFlags(merged);
187: }
188: 
189: export async function loadRuntimeFlags(env: Env): Promise<RuntimeFlags> {
190:   const fromEnv = getRuntimeFlagsFromEnv(env);
191: 
192:   try {
193:     const result = await env.DB
194:       .prepare('SELECT key, value FROM runtime_flags')
195:       .all<RuntimeFlagRow>();
196: 
197:     return mergeRuntimeFlagOverrides(fromEnv, result.results);
198:   } catch (error) {
199:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
200:     console.warn(`[flags] Failed to load runtime overrides, using env defaults: ${message}`);
201:     return fromEnv;
202:   }
203: }

(End of file - total 203 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import * as fs from 'node:fs/promises';
2: import * as path from 'node:path';
3: import type { BuildMetrics } from './types.js';
4: 
5: /**
6:  * Format duration from milliseconds to human-readable string
7:  */
8: export function formatDuration(ms: number): string {
9:   // Show milliseconds for sub-second durations
10:   if (ms < 1000) {
11:     return ms > 0 ? `${ms}ms` : '0s';
12:   }
13: 
14:   const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);
15:   const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);
16:   const remainingSeconds = seconds % 60;
17: 
18:   if (minutes > 0) {
19:     return `${minutes}m ${remainingSeconds}s`;
20:   }
21:   return `${seconds}s`;
22: }
23: 
24: /**
25:  * Format cost to dollar string with 4 decimal places
26:  */
27: export function formatCost(cost: number): string {
28:   return `$${cost.toFixed(4)}`;
29: }
30: 
31: /**
32:  * Format a number with locale-aware separators
33:  */
34: export function formatNumber(num: number): string {
35:   return num.toLocaleString();
36: }
37: 
38: /**
39:  * Generate a timestamped filename for the report
40:  */
41: export function generateReportFilename(): string {
42:   const now = new Date();
43:   const year = now.getFullYear();
44:   const month = String(now.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
45:   const day = String(now.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
46:   const hour = String(now.getHours()).padStart(2, '0');
47:   const min = String(now.getMinutes()).padStart(2, '0');
48:   const sec = String(now.getSeconds()).padStart(2, '0');
49: 
50:   return `nimbus-report-${year}-${month}-${day}-${hour}${min}${sec}.md`;
51: }
52: 
53: /**
54:  * Generate markdown report content from build metrics
55:  */
56: export function generateReportContent(metrics: BuildMetrics): string {
57:   const timestamp = new Date(metrics.completedAt).toLocaleString();
58:   const buildStatus = metrics.buildSuccess ? 'Success' : 'Failed';
59:   const deployStatus = metrics.deploySuccess ? 'Deployed' : 'Failed (using preview)';
60: 
61:   // Build deployment section based on success/failure
62:   let deploymentSection: string;
63:   if (metrics.deploySuccess) {
64:     deploymentSection = `## Output
65: 
66: - **Deployed URL:** ${metrics.deployedUrl}
67: - **Build ID:** ${metrics.id}`;
68:   } else {
69:     deploymentSection = `## Output
70: 
71: - **Preview URL (temporary):** ${metrics.deployedUrl}
72: - **Build ID:** ${metrics.id}
73: - **Deployment Error:** ${metrics.deployError || 'Unknown error'}
74: 
75: > **Warning:** Deployment to Cloudflare Pages failed. The preview URL above is temporary and will expire when the sandbox is cleaned up.`;
76:   }
77: 
78:   return `# Nimbus Build Report
79: 
80: **Generated:** ${timestamp}
81: 
82: ## Prompt
83: 
84: > ${metrics.prompt}
85: 
86: ## Summary
87: 
88: | Metric | Value |
89: |--------|-------|
90: | Model | ${metrics.model} |
91: | Total Tokens | ${formatNumber(metrics.totalTokens)} |
92: | Cost | ${formatCost(metrics.cost)} |
93: | Duration | ${formatDuration(metrics.totalDurationMs)} |
94: | Files Generated | ${metrics.filesGenerated} |
95: | Lines of Code | ${formatNumber(metrics.linesOfCode)} |
96: | Build | ${buildStatus} |
97: | Deploy | ${deployStatus} |
98: 
99: ## Token Usage
100: 
101: | Type | Count |
102: |------|-------|
103: | Prompt (input) | ${formatNumber(metrics.promptTokens)} |
104: | Completion (output) | ${formatNumber(metrics.completionTokens)} |
105: | **Total** | **${formatNumber(metrics.totalTokens)}** |
106: 
107: ## Timing Breakdown
108: 
109: | Phase | Duration |
110: |-------|----------|
111: | LLM Generation | ${formatDuration(metrics.llmLatencyMs)} |
112: | Install | ${formatDuration(metrics.installDurationMs)} |
113: | Build | ${formatDuration(metrics.buildDurationMs)} |
114: | Deploy | ${formatDuration(metrics.deployDurationMs)} |
115: | **Total** | **${formatDuration(metrics.totalDurationMs)}** |
116: 
117: ${deploymentSection}
118: 
119: ---
120: 
121: *Generated by [Nimbus](https://github.com/dayhaysoos/nimbus)*
122: `;
123: }
124: 
125: /**
126:  * Save the report to a file in the current working directory
127:  * Returns the filename
128:  */
129: export async function saveReport(metrics: BuildMetrics): Promise<string> {
130:   const filename = generateReportFilename();
131:   const filepath = path.join(process.cwd(), filename);
132:   const content = generateReportContent(metrics);
133: 
134:   await fs.writeFile(filepath, content, 'utf-8');
135: 
136:   return filename;
137: }

(End of file - total 137 lines)
</content>
