-
-## 7. Data Models
-
-### 7.1 Database Schema (D1)
-
-```sql
--- Jobs table: Top-level benchmarking session
-CREATE TABLE jobs (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- e.g., "job_abc123"
-    prompt TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- User's original prompt
-    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending|running|completed|failed|cancelled
-    config TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- JSON: { maxRetries, timeout, deployTo, etc. }
-    
-    -- Timestamps
-    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    started_at TEXT,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    
-    -- Aggregated metrics (populated on completion)
-    total_variants INTEGER,
-    completed_variants INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    failed_variants INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_cost REAL DEFAULT 0,
-    total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_duration_ms INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Output
-    report_url TEXT,                        -- R2 URL to markdown report
-    
-    -- Indexes for common queries
-    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled'))
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_status ON jobs(status);
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_created_at ON jobs(created_at);
-
--- Variants table: Individual framework+model builds
-CREATE TABLE variants (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- e.g., "var_xyz789"
-    job_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES jobs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-    
-    -- Configuration
-    framework TEXT NOT NULL,                -- e.g., "nextjs", "astro"
-    model TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- e.g., "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-    
-    -- Status
-    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending|generating|building|deploying|completed|failed|waiting_for_human|skipped|cancelled
-    
-    -- Timestamps
-    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    started_at TEXT,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    
-    -- Progress tracking
-    current_phase TEXT,                     -- e.g., "generating", "building", "deploying"
-    build_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    
-    -- Human escalation
-    waiting_for_human INTEGER DEFAULT 0,    -- Boolean: 1 if waiting
-    escalation_reason TEXT,
-    escalation_error TEXT,
-    
-    -- Metrics (aggregated)
-    total_llm_calls INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_prompt_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_completion_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    total_cost REAL DEFAULT 0,
-    total_duration_ms INTEGER,
-    avg_llm_latency_ms INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Build metrics
-    total_build_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    successful_builds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    failed_builds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    
-    -- Error recovery
-    total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    auto_recovered_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    human_escalated_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    
-    -- Code metrics
-    files_generated INTEGER,
-    lines_of_code INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Output URLs
-    preview_url TEXT,
-    deployed_url TEXT,
-    github_url TEXT,
-    source_archive_url TEXT,                -- R2 URL to source code zip
-    
-    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'generating', 'building', 'deploying', 'completed', 'failed', 'waiting_for_human', 'skipped', 'cancelled'))
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_variants_job_id ON variants(job_id);
-CREATE INDEX idx_variants_status ON variants(status);
-
--- LLM Calls table: Individual LLM API calls for detailed analysis
-CREATE TABLE llm_calls (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- UUID
-    variant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES variants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-    
-    -- Call details
-    purpose TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- initial_generation|error_fix|refinement|planning
-    model TEXT NOT NULL,
-    
-    -- Timing
-    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    latency_ms INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Token usage
-    prompt_tokens INTEGER,
-    completion_tokens INTEGER,
-    total_tokens INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Cost (from OpenRouter)
-    cost REAL,
-    
-    -- For debugging
-    prompt_preview TEXT,                    -- First 500 chars of prompt
-    response_preview TEXT,                  -- First 500 chars of response
-    
-    -- Status
-    success INTEGER DEFAULT 1,              -- Boolean
-    error_message TEXT
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_llm_calls_variant_id ON llm_calls(variant_id);
-CREATE INDEX idx_llm_calls_purpose ON llm_calls(purpose);
-
--- Build Attempts table: Each npm build attempt
-CREATE TABLE build_attempts (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- UUID
-    variant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES variants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-    
-    attempt_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
-    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    duration_ms INTEGER,
-    
-    -- Result
-    exit_code INTEGER,
-    success INTEGER DEFAULT 0,              -- Boolean
-    
-    -- Error details (if failed)
-    error_summary TEXT,                     -- Short description
-    stdout TEXT,                            -- Full stdout (truncated if too long)
-    stderr TEXT                             -- Full stderr (truncated if too long)
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_build_attempts_variant_id ON build_attempts(variant_id);
-
--- Errors table: All errors encountered
-CREATE TABLE errors (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- UUID
-    variant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES variants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-    
-    -- Error details
-    error_type TEXT NOT NULL,               -- build_error|runtime_error|timeout|sandbox_error|llm_error
-    message TEXT NOT NULL,
-    stack_trace TEXT,
-    
-    -- Recovery
-    recovery_method TEXT,                   -- auto_retry|llm_fix|human_intervention|unrecovered
-    recovery_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-    resolved INTEGER DEFAULT 0,             -- Boolean
-    
-    -- Timestamps
-    occurred_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    resolved_at TEXT
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_errors_variant_id ON errors(variant_id);
-CREATE INDEX idx_errors_error_type ON errors(error_type);
-
--- Human Interventions table: Track user responses to escalations
-CREATE TABLE human_interventions (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- UUID
-    variant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES variants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-    
-    -- Request
-    requested_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
-    error_context TEXT,
-    
-    -- Response
-    responded_at TEXT,
-    action TEXT,                            -- retry|change_model|provide_fix|skip|cancel
-    new_model TEXT,                         -- If action = change_model
-    fix_instructions TEXT,                  -- If action = provide_fix
-    
-    -- Outcome
-    outcome TEXT                            -- success|failed|skipped|cancelled
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_human_interventions_variant_id ON human_interventions(variant_id);
-```
-
-### 7.2 TypeScript Types

-## 8. API Specification
-
-### 8.1 Base URL
-
-```
-https://<your-worker>.workers.dev/api
-```
-
-### 8.2 Authentication
-
-No authentication for v1.0 (single-user, self-hosted). Future versions may add API key authentication.
-
-### 8.3 Endpoints
-
-#### POST /api/jobs
-
-Create a new benchmarking job.
-
-**Request:**
-```json
-{
-  "prompt": "Build an e-commerce site with product listing, cart, and checkout",
-  "variants": [
-    { "framework": "nextjs", "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4" },
-    { "framework": "astro", "model": "openai/gpt-4o" },
-    { "framework": "nextjs", "model": "openai/gpt-4o" }
-  ],
-  "config": {
-    "maxRetries": 3,
-    "timeout": "1h",
-    "deployTo": ["pages"],
-    "githubOrg": "myusername"
-  }
-}
-```
-
-**Response (201 Created):**
-```json
-{
-  "jobId": "job_abc123",
-  "status": "running",
-  "variants": [
-    { "id": "var_001", "framework": "nextjs", "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4", "status": "pending" },
-    { "id": "var_002", "framework": "astro", "model": "openai/gpt-4o", "status": "pending" },
-    { "id": "var_003", "framework": "nextjs", "model": "openai/gpt-4o", "status": "pending" }
-  ]
-}
-```
-
-**Errors:**
-- `400 Bad Request` - Invalid request body
-- `422 Unprocessable Entity` - Unknown framework or model
-- `429 Too Many Requests` - Concurrent build limit reached
-
----
-
-#### GET /api/jobs
-
-List all jobs with pagination.
-
-**Query Parameters:**
-- `page` (integer, default: 1)
-- `pageSize` (integer, default: 20, max: 100)
-- `status` (string, optional) - Filter by status
-
-**Response (200 OK):**
-```json
-{
-  "jobs": [
-    {
-      "id": "job_abc123",
-      "prompt": "Build an e-commerce site...",
-      "status": "completed",
-      "totalVariants": 3,
-      "completedVariants": 3,
-      "totalCost": 0.0847,
-      "createdAt": "2025-01-21T10:00:00Z"
-    }
-  ],
-  "pagination": {
-    "page": 1,
-    "pageSize": 20,
-    "totalCount": 42,
-    "totalPages": 3
-  }
-}
-```

-
-#### POST /api/jobs/:jobId/variants/:variantId/respond
-
-Respond to a human escalation request.
-
-**Request:**
-```json
-{
-  "action": "change_model",
-  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-}
-```
-
-Or:
-```json
-{
-  "action": "provide_fix",
-  "instructions": "Add @tanstack/react-query to dependencies in package.json and import useQuery from it in the ProductList component"
-}
-```
-
-Or:
-```json
-{
-  "action": "retry"
-}
-```
-
-Or:
-```json
-{
-  "action": "skip"
-}
-```
-
-Or:
-```json
-{
-  "action": "cancel"
-}
-```
-
-**Response (200 OK):**
-```json
-{
-  "success": true,
-  "variantId": "var_003",
-  "newStatus": "generating"
-}
-```
-
-**Errors:**
-- `400 Bad Request` - Invalid action or missing required fields
-- `404 Not Found` - Job or variant does not exist
-- `409 Conflict` - Variant is not waiting for human intervention
-

-## 9. Workflow Definitions
-
-### 9.1 Orchestrator Workflow
-
-**File:** `src/workflows/orchestrator.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Top-level workflow that manages a single job. Spawns variant workflows in parallel, waits for completion, and generates the final report.
-
-```typescript
-import { WorkflowEntrypoint, WorkflowStep, WorkflowEvent } from 'cloudflare:workers';
-
-interface OrchestratorInput {
-  jobId: string;
-  prompt: string;
-  variants: {
-    id: string;
-    framework: string;
-    model: string;
-  }[];
-  config: JobConfig;
-}
-
-interface VariantCompletedEvent {
-  type: 'variant_completed';
-  variantId: string;
-  status: 'completed' | 'failed' | 'skipped' | 'cancelled';
-  metrics: VariantMetrics;
-}
-
-export class OrchestratorWorkflow extends WorkflowEntrypoint<Env, OrchestratorInput> {
-  
-  async run(ctx: WorkflowStep, event: WorkflowEvent<OrchestratorInput>) {
-    const { jobId, prompt, variants, config } = event.payload;
-    
-    // Step 1: Update job status to running
-    await ctx.step.do('update-job-running', async () => {
-      await this.env.DB.prepare(
-        'UPDATE jobs SET status = ?, started_at = datetime("now") WHERE id = ?'
-      ).bind('running', jobId).run();
-    });
-    
-    // Step 2: Spawn all variant workflows in parallel
-    const variantWorkflows = await ctx.step.do('spawn-variants', async () => {
-      const workflows = [];
-      
-      for (const variant of variants) {
-        const instance = await this.env.VARIANT_BUILDER.create({
-          id: variant.id,
-          params: {
-            jobId,
-            variantId: variant.id,
-            prompt,
-            framework: variant.framework,
-            model: variant.model,
-            maxRetries: config.maxRetries,
-            timeout: config.timeout,
-            deployTo: config.deployTo,
-            githubOrg: config.githubOrg
-          }
-        });
-        
-        workflows.push({
-          variantId: variant.id,
-          instanceId: instance.id
-        });
-      }
-      
-      return workflows;
-    });
-    
-    // Step 3: Wait for all variants to complete
-    // Each variant will send an event when done
-    const results: Map<string, VariantCompletedEvent> = new Map();
-    
-    while (results.size < variants.length) {
-      const event = await ctx.step.waitForEvent<VariantCompletedEvent>(
-        'variant-completion',
-        { timeout: config.timeout }
-      );
-      
-      if (event) {
-        results.set(event.variantId, event);
-        
-        // Update progress
-        await ctx.step.do(`update-progress-${results.size}`, async () => {
-          const completed = Array.from(results.values()).filter(
-            r => r.status === 'completed'
-          ).length;
-          const failed = Array.from(results.values()).filter(
-            r => r.status === 'failed'
-          ).length;
-          
-          await this.env.DB.prepare(
-            'UPDATE jobs SET completed_variants = ?, failed_variants = ? WHERE id = ?'
-          ).bind(completed, failed, jobId).run();
-        });
-      }
-    }
-    
-    // Step 4: Aggregate metrics
-    const aggregatedMetrics = await ctx.step.do('aggregate-metrics', async () => {
-      let totalCost = 0;
-      let totalTokens = 0;
-      
-      for (const result of results.values()) {
-        totalCost += result.metrics.totalCost;
-        totalTokens += result.metrics.totalTokens;
-      }
-      
-      return { totalCost, totalTokens };
-    });
-    
-    // Step 5: Generate report
-    const reportUrl = await ctx.step.do('generate-report', async () => {
-      const report = await this.generateReport(jobId, prompt, results);
-      
-      // Save to R2
-      await this.env.ARTIFACTS.put(
-        `reports/${jobId}.md`,
-        report,
-        { contentType: 'text/markdown' }
-      );
-      
-      return `reports/${jobId}.md`;
-    });
-    
-    // Step 6: Update job as completed
-    const completedCount = Array.from(results.values()).filter(
-      r => r.status === 'completed'
-    ).length;
-    const failedCount = Array.from(results.values()).filter(
-      r => r.status === 'failed'
-    ).length;
-    
-    const finalStatus = failedCount === variants.length ? 'failed' : 'completed';
-    
-    await ctx.step.do('finalize-job', async () => {
-      await this.env.DB.prepare(`
-        UPDATE jobs 
-        SET status = ?, 
-            completed_at = datetime('now'),
-            completed_variants = ?,
-            failed_variants = ?,
-            total_cost = ?,
-            total_tokens = ?,
-            report_url = ?
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        finalStatus,
-        completedCount,
-        failedCount,
-        aggregatedMetrics.totalCost,
-        aggregatedMetrics.totalTokens,
-        reportUrl,
-        jobId
-      ).run();
-    });
-    
-    // Step 7: Optional webhook notification
-    if (config.webhookUrl) {
-      await ctx.step.do('notify-webhook', async () => {
-        await fetch(config.webhookUrl!, {
-          method: 'POST',
-          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
-          body: JSON.stringify({
-            event: 'job_completed',
-            jobId,
-            status: finalStatus,
-            reportUrl,
-            variants: Array.from(results.entries()).map(([id, r]) => ({
-              id,
-              status: r.status,
-              cost: r.metrics.totalCost
-            }))
-          })
-        });
-      });
-    }
-    
-    return {
-      jobId,
-      status: finalStatus,
-      completedVariants: completedCount,
-      failedVariants: failedCount,
-      reportUrl
-    };
-  }
-  
-  private async generateReport(
-    jobId: string, 
-    prompt: string, 
-    results: Map<string, VariantCompletedEvent>
-  ): Promise<string> {
-    // Implementation in Section 13
-  }
-}
-```
-
-### 9.2 Variant Builder Workflow
-
-**File:** `src/workflows/variant-builder.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Builds a single variant (framework + model combination). Handles code generation, building, error recovery, human escalation, and deployment.
-
-```typescript
-import { WorkflowEntrypoint, WorkflowStep, WorkflowEvent } from 'cloudflare:workers';
-import { getSandbox } from '@cloudflare/sandbox';
-
-interface VariantBuilderInput {
-  jobId: string;
-  variantId: string;
-  prompt: string;
-  framework: string;
-  model: string;
-  maxRetries: number;
-  timeout: string;
-  deployTo: string[];
-  githubOrg?: string;
-}
-
-interface HumanResponseEvent {
-  type: 'human_response';
-  action: 'retry' | 'change_model' | 'provide_fix' | 'skip' | 'cancel';
-  model?: string;
-  instructions?: string;
-}
-
-export class VariantBuilderWorkflow extends WorkflowEntrypoint<Env, VariantBuilderInput> {
-  
-  async run(ctx: WorkflowStep, event: WorkflowEvent<VariantBuilderInput>) {
-    const { 
-      jobId, variantId, prompt, framework, model, 
-      maxRetries, timeout, deployTo, githubOrg 
-    } = event.payload;
-    
-    let currentModel = model;
-    let attempt = 0;
-    let code: GeneratedCode | null = null;
-    let lastError: string | null = null;
-    
-    // Helper to update variant status
-    const updateStatus = async (status: string, extras?: Record<string, any>) => {
-      const sets = ['status = ?', ...Object.keys(extras || {}).map(k => `${k} = ?`)];
-      const values = [status, ...Object.values(extras || {}), variantId];
-      
-      await this.env.DB.prepare(
-        `UPDATE variants SET ${sets.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`
-      ).bind(...values).run();
-    };
-    
-    // Step 1: Initialize
-    await ctx.step.do('initialize', async () => {
-      await updateStatus('generating', { started_at: new Date().toISOString() });
-    });
-    
-    // Step 2: Initial code generation
-    code = await ctx.step.do('generate-initial', async () => {
-      return this.generateCode(variantId, prompt, framework, currentModel, 'initial_generation');
-    });
-    
-    // Step 3: Build loop with retries
-    while (attempt < maxRetries) {
-      attempt++;
-      
-      await ctx.step.do(`update-building-${attempt}`, async () => {
-        await updateStatus('building', { 
-          build_attempts: attempt,
-          current_phase: `build_attempt_${attempt}`
-        });
-      });
-      
-      // Try to build
-      const buildResult = await ctx.step.do(`build-${attempt}`, async () => {
-        return this.buildInSandbox(variantId, framework, code!);
-      });
-      
-      if (buildResult.success) {
-        // Build succeeded!
-        
-        // Step 4a: Expose preview
-        const previewUrl = await ctx.step.do('expose-preview', async () => {
-          return this.exposePreview(variantId, framework);
-        });
-        
-        await ctx.step.do('update-preview-url', async () => {
-          await updateStatus('deploying', { preview_url: previewUrl });
-        });
-        
-        // Step 4b: Deploy
-        let deployedUrl: string | null = null;
-        let githubUrl: string | null = null;
-        
-        if (deployTo.includes('pages')) {
-          deployedUrl = await ctx.step.do('deploy-pages', async () => {
-            return this.deployToPages(variantId, jobId, framework);
-          });
-        }
-        
-        if (deployTo.includes('github') && githubOrg) {
-          githubUrl = await ctx.step.do('deploy-github', async () => {
-            return this.pushToGitHub(variantId, jobId, framework, githubOrg);
-          });
-        }
-        
-        // Step 4c: Calculate final metrics
-        const metrics = await ctx.step.do('calculate-metrics', async () => {
-          return this.calculateMetrics(variantId);
-        });
-        
-        // Step 4d: Archive source code
-        const sourceArchiveUrl = await ctx.step.do('archive-source', async () => {
-          return this.archiveSource(variantId);
-        });
-        
-        // Step 4e: Mark as completed
-        await ctx.step.do('mark-completed', async () => {
-          await updateStatus('completed', {
-            completed_at: new Date().toISOString(),
-            deployed_url: deployedUrl,
-            github_url: githubUrl,
-            source_archive_url: sourceArchiveUrl,
-            ...this.metricsToDbFields(metrics)
-          });
-        });
-        
-        // Step 4f: Notify orchestrator
-        await ctx.step.do('notify-orchestrator', async () => {
-          const orchestrator = await this.env.ORCHESTRATOR.get(jobId);
-          await orchestrator.sendEvent({
-            type: 'variant_completed',
-            variantId,
-            status: 'completed',
-            metrics
-          });
-        });
-        
-        // Cleanup sandbox
-        await ctx.step.do('cleanup', async () => {
-          const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-          await sandbox.destroy();
-        });
-        
-        return { status: 'completed', metrics };
-      }
-      
-      // Build failed
-      lastError = buildResult.error;
-      
-      // Log the error
-      await ctx.step.do(`log-error-${attempt}`, async () => {
-        await this.logError(variantId, 'build_error', lastError!, attempt < maxRetries);
-      });
-      
-      if (attempt < maxRetries) {
-        // Try to auto-fix
-        await ctx.step.do(`update-fixing-${attempt}`, async () => {
-          await updateStatus('generating', { current_phase: `auto_fix_${attempt}` });
-        });
-        
-        code = await ctx.step.do(`auto-fix-${attempt}`, async () => {
-          return this.generateCode(
-            variantId, 
-            prompt, 
-            framework, 
-            currentModel, 
-            'error_fix',
-            { error: lastError, previousCode: code }
-          );
-        });
-      }
-    }
-    
-    // Exhausted retries - escalate to human
-    await ctx.step.do('escalate-to-human', async () => {
-      await updateStatus('waiting_for_human', {
-        waiting_for_human: 1,
-        escalation_reason: `Build failed after ${maxRetries} attempts`,
-        escalation_error: lastError
-      });
-      
-      // Log human escalation
-      await this.env.DB.prepare(`
-        INSERT INTO human_interventions (id, variant_id, reason, error_context, requested_at)
-        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
-      `).bind(
-        crypto.randomUUID(),
-        variantId,
-        `Build failed after ${maxRetries} attempts`,
-        lastError
-      ).run();
-    });
-    
-    // Wait for human response (up to 24 hours)
-    const humanResponse = await ctx.step.waitForEvent<HumanResponseEvent>(
-      'human_response',
-      { timeout: '24h' }
-    );
-    
-    if (!humanResponse) {
-      // Timeout - mark as failed
-      await ctx.step.do('timeout-failed', async () => {
-        await this.markFailed(variantId, 'Timed out waiting for human intervention');
-      });
-      
-      await this.notifyOrchestratorFailed(ctx, jobId, variantId);
-      return { status: 'failed', reason: 'timeout' };
-    }
-    
-    // Handle human response
-    switch (humanResponse.action) {
-      case 'retry':
-        // Reset attempt counter and retry with same model
-        attempt = 0;
-        // Recursively continue (workflow will restart from build loop)
-        return this.run(ctx, {
-          ...event,
-          payload: { ...event.payload, maxRetries: 1 }
-        });
-        
-      case 'change_model':
-        // Retry with new model
-        currentModel = humanResponse.model!;
-        attempt = 0;
-        return this.run(ctx, {
-          ...event,
-          payload: { ...event.payload, model: currentModel, maxRetries: 1 }
-        });
-        
-      case 'provide_fix':
-        // Generate code with human instructions
-        code = await ctx.step.do('apply-human-fix', async () => {
-          return this.generateCode(
-            variantId,
-            prompt,
-            framework,
-            currentModel,
-            'error_fix',
-            { 
-              error: lastError, 
-              previousCode: code,
-              humanInstructions: humanResponse.instructions 
-            }
-          );
-        });
-        
-        // Try one more build
-        const finalBuild = await ctx.step.do('final-build', async () => {
-          return this.buildInSandbox(variantId, framework, code!);
-        });
-        
-        if (finalBuild.success) {
-          // Continue to deployment (same as success path above)
-          // ... (abbreviated for brevity)
-        } else {
-          await this.markFailed(variantId, finalBuild.error);
-          await this.notifyOrchestratorFailed(ctx, jobId, variantId);
-          return { status: 'failed', reason: 'human_fix_failed' };
-        }
-        break;
-        
-      case 'skip':
-        await ctx.step.do('mark-skipped', async () => {
-          await updateStatus('skipped', { completed_at: new Date().toISOString() });
-        });
-        
-        await ctx.step.do('notify-skipped', async () => {
-          const orchestrator = await this.env.ORCHESTRATOR.get(jobId);
-          await orchestrator.sendEvent({
-            type: 'variant_completed',
-            variantId,
-            status: 'skipped',
-            metrics: await this.calculateMetrics(variantId)
-          });
-        });
-        
-        return { status: 'skipped' };
-        
-      case 'cancel':
-        await ctx.step.do('mark-cancelled', async () => {
-          await updateStatus('cancelled', { completed_at: new Date().toISOString() });
-        });
-        
-        await ctx.step.do('notify-cancelled', async () => {
-          const orchestrator = await this.env.ORCHESTRATOR.get(jobId);
-          await orchestrator.sendEvent({
-            type: 'variant_completed',
-            variantId,
-            status: 'cancelled',
-            metrics: await this.calculateMetrics(variantId)
-          });
-        });
-        
-        return { status: 'cancelled' };
-    }
-  }
-  
-  // Helper methods (implementations in Section 10)
-  private async generateCode(...args): Promise<GeneratedCode> { /* ... */ }
-  private async buildInSandbox(...args): Promise<BuildResult> { /* ... */ }
-  private async exposePreview(...args): Promise<string> { /* ... */ }
-  private async deployToPages(...args): Promise<string> { /* ... */ }
-  private async pushToGitHub(...args): Promise<string> { /* ... */ }
-  private async calculateMetrics(...args): Promise<VariantMetrics> { /* ... */ }
-  private async archiveSource(...args): Promise<string> { /* ... */ }
-  private async logError(...args): Promise<void> { /* ... */ }
-  private async markFailed(...args): Promise<void> { /* ... */ }
-  private async notifyOrchestratorFailed(...args): Promise<void> { /* ... */ }
-}
-```

-## 10. Component Specifications
-
-### 10.1 LLM Client
-
-**File:** `src/lib/llm/client.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Unified interface for calling LLMs via OpenRouter. Handles request formatting, response parsing, and metrics collection.
-
-```typescript
-interface LLMClientConfig {
-  apiKey: string;
-  baseUrl?: string;
-  defaultModel?: string;
-  appName?: string;
-  appUrl?: string;
-}
-
-interface CompletionRequest {
-  model: string;
-  messages: Message[];
-  temperature?: number;
-  maxTokens?: number;
-  
-  // For metrics tracking
-  variantId: string;
-  purpose: LLMCallPurpose;
-}
-
-interface CompletionResponse {
-  content: string;
-  model: string;
-  usage: {
-    promptTokens: number;
-    completionTokens: number;
-    totalTokens: number;
-    cost: number;
-  };
-  latencyMs: number;
-  llmCallId: string;  // ID of the logged call
-}
-
-export class LLMClient {
-  private config: LLMClientConfig;
-  private db: D1Database;
-  
-  constructor(config: LLMClientConfig, db: D1Database) {
-    this.config = {
-      baseUrl: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1',
-      ...config
-    };
-    this.db = db;
-  }
-  
-  async complete(request: CompletionRequest): Promise<CompletionResponse> {
-    const llmCallId = crypto.randomUUID();
-    const startTime = Date.now();
-    
-    // Log call start
-    await this.db.prepare(`
-      INSERT INTO llm_calls (id, variant_id, purpose, model, started_at, prompt_preview)
-      VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'), ?)
-    `).bind(
-      llmCallId,
-      request.variantId,
-      request.purpose,
-      request.model,
-      request.messages[request.messages.length - 1]?.content?.slice(0, 500)
-    ).run();
-    
-    try {
-      const response = await fetch(`${this.config.baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {
-        method: 'POST',
-        headers: {
-          'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.config.apiKey}`,
-          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
-          'HTTP-Referer': this.config.appUrl || 'https://github.com/your-org/project-name',
-          'X-Title': this.config.appName || 'AI Website Builder'
-        },
-        body: JSON.stringify({
-          model: request.model,
-          messages: request.messages,
-          temperature: request.temperature ?? 0.7,
-          max_tokens: request.maxTokens ?? 8192
-        })
-      });
-      
-      if (!response.ok) {
-        const error = await response.text();
-        throw new Error(`OpenRouter API error: ${response.status} - ${error}`);
-      }
-      
-      const data = await response.json();
-      const latencyMs = Date.now() - startTime;
-      
-      const result: CompletionResponse = {
-        content: data.choices[0].message.content,
-        model: data.model,
-        usage: {
-          promptTokens: data.usage.prompt_tokens,
-          completionTokens: data.usage.completion_tokens,
-          totalTokens: data.usage.total_tokens,
-          cost: data.usage.cost ?? this.estimateCost(request.model, data.usage)
-        },
-        latencyMs,
-        llmCallId
-      };
-      
-      // Log call completion
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE llm_calls 
-        SET completed_at = datetime('now'),
-            latency_ms = ?,
-            prompt_tokens = ?,
-            completion_tokens = ?,
-            total_tokens = ?,
-            cost = ?,
-            response_preview = ?,
-            success = 1
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        latencyMs,
-        result.usage.promptTokens,
-        result.usage.completionTokens,
-        result.usage.totalTokens,
-        result.usage.cost,
-        result.content.slice(0, 500),
-        llmCallId
-      ).run();
-      
-      // Update variant metrics
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE variants 
-        SET total_llm_calls = total_llm_calls + 1,
-            total_prompt_tokens = total_prompt_tokens + ?,
-            total_completion_tokens = total_completion_tokens + ?,
-            total_tokens = total_tokens + ?,
-            total_cost = total_cost + ?
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        result.usage.promptTokens,
-        result.usage.completionTokens,
-        result.usage.totalTokens,
-        result.usage.cost,
-        request.variantId
-      ).run();
-      
-      return result;
-      
-    } catch (error) {
-      // Log call failure
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE llm_calls 
-        SET completed_at = datetime('now'),
-            latency_ms = ?,
-            success = 0,
-            error_message = ?
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        Date.now() - startTime,
-        error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
-        llmCallId
-      ).run();
-      
-      throw error;
-    }
-  }
-  
-  private estimateCost(model: string, usage: { prompt_tokens: number; completion_tokens: number }): number {
-    // Fallback cost estimation if OpenRouter doesn't return it
-    const pricing: Record<string, { input: number; output: number }> = {
-      'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4': { input: 0.003, output: 0.015 },
-      'openai/gpt-4o': { input: 0.005, output: 0.015 },
-      'anthropic/claude-haiku-3.5': { input: 0.0008, output: 0.004 },
-      // Add more as needed
-    };
-    
-    const prices = pricing[model] || { input: 0.001, output: 0.002 };
-    return (usage.prompt_tokens / 1000 * prices.input) + 
-           (usage.completion_tokens / 1000 * prices.output);
-  }
-}

-### 10.2 Code Generator
-
-**File:** `src/lib/llm/code-generator.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Generates code for a specific framework using LLM. Handles prompt construction, response parsing, and file extraction.
-
-```typescript
-interface GeneratedCode {
-  files: {
-    path: string;
-    content: string;
-  }[];
-  dependencies?: Record<string, string>;
-  devDependencies?: Record<string, string>;
-}
-
-interface GenerateOptions {
-  error?: string;
-  previousCode?: GeneratedCode;
-  humanInstructions?: string;
-}
-
-export class CodeGenerator {
-  private llm: LLMClient;
-  private frameworkRegistry: FrameworkRegistry;
-  
-  constructor(llm: LLMClient, frameworkRegistry: FrameworkRegistry) {
-    this.llm = llm;
-    this.frameworkRegistry = frameworkRegistry;
-  }
-  
-  async generate(
-    variantId: string,
-    prompt: string,
-    framework: string,
-    model: string,
-    purpose: LLMCallPurpose,
-    options?: GenerateOptions
-  ): Promise<GeneratedCode> {
-    const frameworkInfo = this.frameworkRegistry.get(framework);
-    const systemPrompt = this.buildSystemPrompt(frameworkInfo);
-    const userPrompt = this.buildUserPrompt(prompt, frameworkInfo, purpose, options);
-    
-    const response = await this.llm.complete({
-      model,
-      messages: [
-        { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
-        { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }
-      ],
-      variantId,
-      purpose
-    });
-    
-    return this.parseResponse(response.content);
-  }
-  
-  private buildSystemPrompt(framework: FrameworkInfo): string {
-    return `You are an expert ${framework.name} developer. You generate complete, production-ready code.
-
-CRITICAL RULES:
-1. Output ONLY valid JSON with the structure specified
-2. Include ALL necessary files for a working application
-3. Use TypeScript
-4. Use modern best practices
-5. Include proper error handling
-6. Make the UI visually appealing with Tailwind CSS
-
-OUTPUT FORMAT:
-{
-  "files": [
-    { "path": "relative/path/to/file.tsx", "content": "file content here" }
-  ],
-  "dependencies": { "package-name": "^version" },
-  "devDependencies": { "package-name": "^version" }
-}
-
-FRAMEWORK SPECIFICS (${framework.name}):
-- Scaffold command: ${framework.scaffoldCommand}
-- Build command: ${framework.buildCommand}
-- Output directory: ${framework.outputDir}
-- Default port: ${framework.defaultPort}
-
-Generate code that will work with the standard ${framework.name} project structure.`;
-  }
-  
-  private buildUserPrompt(
-    prompt: string,
-    framework: FrameworkInfo,
-    purpose: LLMCallPurpose,
-    options?: GenerateOptions
-  ): string {
-    if (purpose === 'initial_generation') {
-      return `Create a ${framework.name} application for the following request:
-
-${prompt}
-
-Generate all necessary files including components, pages, styles, and configuration.
-Output valid JSON only.`;
-    }
-    
-    if (purpose === 'error_fix') {
-      let fixPrompt = `The previous code had a build error. Fix it.
-
-ORIGINAL REQUEST:
-${prompt}
-
-BUILD ERROR:
-${options?.error}
-
-PREVIOUS CODE:
-${JSON.stringify(options?.previousCode, null, 2)}
-`;
-
-      if (options?.humanInstructions) {
-        fixPrompt += `
-
-HUMAN GUIDANCE:
-${options.humanInstructions}
-`;
-      }
-      
-      fixPrompt += `
-
-Generate the corrected code. Output valid JSON only.`;
-      
-      return fixPrompt;
-    }
-    
-    throw new Error(`Unknown purpose: ${purpose}`);
-  }
-  
-  private parseResponse(content: string): GeneratedCode {
-    // Handle potential markdown code blocks
-    let jsonContent = content;
-    
-    if (content.includes('```json')) {
-      const match = content.match(/```json\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```/);
-      if (match) jsonContent = match[1];
-    } else if (content.includes('```')) {
-      const match = content.match(/```\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```/);
-      if (match) jsonContent = match[1];
-    }
-    
-    try {
-      const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonContent);
-      
-      // Validate structure
-      if (!parsed.files || !Array.isArray(parsed.files)) {
-        throw new Error('Invalid response: missing files array');
-      }
-      
-      for (const file of parsed.files) {
-        if (!file.path || typeof file.content !== 'string') {
-          throw new Error('Invalid response: file missing path or content');
-        }
-      }
-      
-      return {
-        files: parsed.files,
-        dependencies: parsed.dependencies || {},
-        devDependencies: parsed.devDependencies || {}
-      };
-      
-    } catch (error) {
-      throw new Error(`Failed to parse LLM response: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : error}`);
-    }
-  }
-}
-```

-### 10.3 Sandbox Manager
-
-**File:** `src/lib/sandbox/manager.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Manages Cloudflare Sandbox lifecycle for building code.
-
-```typescript
-import { getSandbox, Sandbox } from '@cloudflare/sandbox';
-
-interface BuildResult {
-  success: boolean;
-  error?: string;
-  stdout?: string;
-  stderr?: string;
-  durationMs: number;
-}
-
-export class SandboxManager {
-  private env: Env;
-  private db: D1Database;
-  
-  constructor(env: Env) {
-    this.env = env;
-    this.db = env.DB;
-  }
-  
-  async writeCode(variantId: string, code: GeneratedCode, framework: string): Promise<void> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    const frameworkInfo = getFrameworkInfo(framework);
-    
-    // Scaffold the project first
-    const scaffoldCmd = frameworkInfo.scaffoldCommand.replace('{{name}}', 'app');
-    await sandbox.exec(scaffoldCmd, { cwd: '/workspace' });
-    
-    // Write generated files
-    for (const file of code.files) {
-      const fullPath = `/workspace/app/${file.path}`;
-      const dir = fullPath.substring(0, fullPath.lastIndexOf('/'));
-      
-      await sandbox.mkdir(dir, { recursive: true });
-      await sandbox.writeFile(fullPath, file.content);
-    }
-    
-    // Update package.json with dependencies
-    if (code.dependencies || code.devDependencies) {
-      const pkgPath = '/workspace/app/package.json';
-      const pkgContent = await sandbox.readFile(pkgPath);
-      const pkg = JSON.parse(pkgContent);
-      
-      pkg.dependencies = { ...pkg.dependencies, ...code.dependencies };
-      pkg.devDependencies = { ...pkg.devDependencies, ...code.devDependencies };
-      
-      await sandbox.writeFile(pkgPath, JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2));
-    }
-    
-    // Install dependencies
-    await sandbox.exec('npm install', { cwd: '/workspace/app' });
-  }
-  
-  async build(variantId: string, framework: string): Promise<BuildResult> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    const frameworkInfo = getFrameworkInfo(framework);
-    const buildAttemptId = crypto.randomUUID();
-    
-    // Get current attempt number
-    const variant = await this.db.prepare(
-      'SELECT build_attempts FROM variants WHERE id = ?'
-    ).bind(variantId).first();
-    const attemptNumber = (variant?.build_attempts || 0) + 1;
-    
-    const startTime = Date.now();
-    
-    // Log build attempt start
-    await this.db.prepare(`
-      INSERT INTO build_attempts (id, variant_id, attempt_number, started_at)
-      VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
-    `).bind(buildAttemptId, variantId, attemptNumber).run();
-    
-    try {
-      const result = await sandbox.exec(frameworkInfo.buildCommand, { 
-        cwd: '/workspace/app',
-        timeout: 120000  // 2 minutes
-      });
-      
-      const durationMs = Date.now() - startTime;
-      const success = result.exitCode === 0;
-      
-      // Log build attempt result
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE build_attempts 
-        SET completed_at = datetime('now'),
-            duration_ms = ?,
-            exit_code = ?,
-            success = ?,
-            error_summary = ?,
-            stdout = ?,
-            stderr = ?
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        durationMs,
-        result.exitCode,
-        success ? 1 : 0,
-        success ? null : this.extractErrorSummary(result.stderr),
-        result.stdout?.slice(0, 10000),  // Truncate
-        result.stderr?.slice(0, 10000),  // Truncate
-        buildAttemptId
-      ).run();
-      
-      // Update variant build counts
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE variants 
-        SET total_build_attempts = total_build_attempts + 1,
-            ${success ? 'successful_builds = successful_builds + 1' : 'failed_builds = failed_builds + 1'}
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(variantId).run();
-      
-      return {
-        success,
-        error: success ? undefined : result.stderr,
-        stdout: result.stdout,
-        stderr: result.stderr,
-        durationMs
-      };
-      
-    } catch (error) {
-      const durationMs = Date.now() - startTime;
-      
-      await this.db.prepare(`
-        UPDATE build_attempts 
-        SET completed_at = datetime('now'),
-            duration_ms = ?,
-            exit_code = -1,
-            success = 0,
-            error_summary = ?
-        WHERE id = ?
-      `).bind(
-        durationMs,
-        error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error',
-        buildAttemptId
-      ).run();
-      
-      return {
-        success: false,
-        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Build failed',
-        durationMs
-      };
-    }
-  }
-  
-  async exposePreview(variantId: string, framework: string): Promise<string> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    const frameworkInfo = getFrameworkInfo(framework);
-    
-    // Start dev server
-    sandbox.exec(frameworkInfo.devCommand, { 
-      cwd: '/workspace/app',
-      background: true 
-    });
-    
-    // Wait for server to be ready
-    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 5000));
-    
-    // Expose port
-    const url = await sandbox.exposePort(frameworkInfo.defaultPort);
-    return url;
-  }
-  
-  async getSourceArchive(variantId: string): Promise<ArrayBuffer> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    
-    // Create zip archive
-    await sandbox.exec('zip -r /tmp/source.zip .', { cwd: '/workspace/app' });
-    
-    // Read the zip file
-    const content = await sandbox.readFile('/tmp/source.zip', 'binary');
-    return content;
-  }
-  
-  async destroy(variantId: string): Promise<void> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    await sandbox.destroy();
-  }
-  
-  private extractErrorSummary(stderr: string): string {
-    // Extract the most relevant error line
-    const lines = stderr.split('\n');
-    
-    // Look for common error patterns
-    for (const line of lines) {
-      if (line.includes('error') || line.includes('Error') || 
-          line.includes('failed') || line.includes('Failed')) {
-        return line.trim().slice(0, 500);
-      }
-    }
-    
-    return lines[0]?.trim().slice(0, 500) || 'Unknown error';
-  }
-}

-### 10.4 Deployment Manager
-
-**File:** `src/lib/deploy/manager.ts`
-
-**Purpose:** Handles deployment to Cloudflare Pages and GitHub.
-
-```typescript
-export class DeploymentManager {
-  private env: Env;
-  
-  constructor(env: Env) {
-    this.env = env;
-  }
-  
-  async deployToPages(variantId: string, jobId: string, framework: string): Promise<string> {
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    const frameworkInfo = getFrameworkInfo(framework);
-    
-    const projectName = `${jobId}-${framework}`.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, '-');
-    
-    // Deploy using Wrangler (available in sandbox)
-    const result = await sandbox.exec(
-      `npx wrangler pages deploy ${frameworkInfo.outputDir} --project-name=${projectName} --branch=main`,
-      { 
-        cwd: '/workspace/app',
-        env: {
-          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: this.env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN,
-          CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: this.env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
-        }
-      }
-    );
-    
-    if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
-      throw new Error(`Pages deployment failed: ${result.stderr}`);
-    }
-    
-    // Extract URL from output
-    const urlMatch = result.stdout.match(/https:\/\/[^\s]+\.pages\.dev/);
-    if (!urlMatch) {
-      throw new Error('Could not extract deployed URL from Wrangler output');
-    }
-    
-    return urlMatch[0];
-  }
-  
-  async pushToGitHub(
-    variantId: string, 
-    jobId: string, 
-    framework: string, 
-    org: string
-  ): Promise<string> {
-    if (!this.env.GITHUB_TOKEN) {
-      throw new Error('GitHub token not configured');
-    }
-    
-    const sandbox = getSandbox(this.env.Sandbox, variantId);
-    const repoName = `${jobId}-${framework}`;
-    
-    // Create repository via GitHub API
-    const createRepoResponse = await fetch('https://api.github.com/user/repos', {
-      method: 'POST',
-      headers: {
-        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.env.GITHUB_TOKEN}`,
-        'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json',
-        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
-      },
-      body: JSON.stringify({
-        name: repoName,
-        description: `Generated by Nimbus - ${framework} implementation`,
-        private: false,
-        auto_init: false
-      })
-    });
-    
-    if (!createRepoResponse.ok) {
-      const error = await createRepoResponse.text();
-      throw new Error(`Failed to create GitHub repo: ${error}`);
-    }
-    
-    const repo = await createRepoResponse.json();
-    
-    // Push code from sandbox
-    await sandbox.exec(`
-      git init &&
-      git add . &&
-      git commit -m "Initial commit - generated by Nimbus" &&
-      git branch -M main &&
-      git remote add origin https://${this.env.GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${org}/${repoName}.git &&
-      git push -u origin main
-    `, { cwd: '/workspace/app' });
-    
-    return repo.html_url;
-  }
-}

-## 11. CLI Specification
-
-### 11.1 Overview
-
-The CLI is a standalone npm package that communicates with the deployed Nimbus API. It prioritizes an **interactive, guided experience** over complex flags.
-
-**Installation:**
-```bash
-npm install -g nimbus-bench
-# or
-npx nimbus-bench <command>
-```
-
-### 11.2 Design Principles
-
-1. **Interactive by default** — Guide users through choices rather than requiring memorized flags
-2. **Prompts are natural language** — Framework/library choices live in the prompt, not structured flags
-3. **File-based prompts** — Support loading detailed specs from markdown/text files
-4. **Comparison is post-hoc** — Users decide what to compare after builds complete
-
-### 11.3 Commands
-
-```bash
-nimbus start                      # Interactive job creation
-nimbus run                        # Run from nimbus.json config
-nimbus list                       # List all jobs
-nimbus watch <jobId>              # Live progress
-nimbus respond <jobId>            # Handle human escalation
-nimbus report <jobId>             # Single job markdown report
-nimbus compare <id> [id] [id...]  # Visual comparison (opens browser, max 4)
-nimbus models                     # List models with live pricing
-nimbus config                     # Settings management
-```
-
----
-
-#### `nimbus start`
-
-Interactive job creation flow. This is the primary way to create builds.
-
-**Flow:**
-```
-$ nimbus start
-
-☁️  Nimbus - LLM Benchmark Runner
-
-? How would you like to provide your prompt?
-  › Type it in
-    Load from file
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-[If "Type it in":]
-
-? Enter your prompt:
-  Build an e-commerce site using use-shopping-cart with Astro
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-[If "Load from file":]
-
-? Select a prompt file:
-  › ./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md    (2.4 KB)
-    ./prompts/dashboard.md          (1.1 KB)
-    ./prompts/blog.txt              (0.8 KB)
-    Browse for file...
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-? Which LLM(s) would you like to use? (space to select, enter to confirm)
-  ◉ Claude Sonnet 4        anthropic/claude-sonnet-4     $0.003/$0.015 per 1K
-  ◯ Claude Opus 4          anthropic/claude-opus-4       $0.015/$0.075 per 1K
-  ◉ GPT-4o                 openai/gpt-4o                 $0.005/$0.015 per 1K
-  ◯ GPT-4o Mini            openai/gpt-4o-mini            $0.00015/$0.0006 per 1K
-  ◯ Deepseek V3            deepseek/deepseek-chat        $0.00014/$0.00028 per 1K
-  ◯ Gemini 2.0 Flash       google/gemini-2.0-flash       $0.0001/$0.0004 per 1K
-  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-  ◯ Enter custom model ID...
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-[If "Enter custom model ID":]
-
-? Enter OpenRouter model ID:
-  meta-llama/llama-3.1-405b-instruct
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-? Configure options:
-  Max retries:     3       (use ← → to adjust)
-  Deploy to:       ◉ Cloudflare Pages  ◯ GitHub  ◯ Both
-  
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-? Ready to start 2 builds:
-  
-  1. Claude Sonnet 4  →  "Build an e-commerce site using..."
-  2. GPT-4o           →  "Build an e-commerce site using..."
-
-  Estimated cost: ~$0.05 - $0.15
-
-  › Start builds
-    Save config for later (nimbus.json)
-    Cancel
-
-───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-
-☁️  Starting builds...
-
-  Job ID: nimbus_abc123
-  
-  Use these commands to interact:
-    nimbus watch nimbus_abc123     # Watch progress
-    nimbus respond nimbus_abc123   # If human input needed
-    nimbus report nimbus_abc123    # Get report when done
-```
-
-**Non-interactive mode (for scripts/CI):**
-```bash
-nimbus start \
-  --prompt "Build a blog with Astro" \
-  --models claude-sonnet-4,gpt-4o \
-  --no-interactive
-
-# Or with a prompt file
-nimbus start \
-  --prompt ./prompts/spec.md \
-  --models claude-sonnet-4 \
-  --no-interactive
-```
-

-
-#### `nimbus run`
-
-Run a build from a saved `nimbus.json` configuration file.
-
-**nimbus.json format:**
-```json
-{
-  "prompt": "./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md",
-  "models": [
-    "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
-    "openai/gpt-4o"
-  ],
-  "options": {
-    "maxRetries": 3,
-    "deploy": ["pages"],
-    "githubOrg": "myusername"
-  }
-}
-```
-
-**Or with inline prompt:**
-```json
-{
-  "prompt": "Build a landing page with Next.js and Tailwind CSS",
-  "models": ["anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"],
-  "options": {
-    "maxRetries": 3,
-    "deploy": ["pages"]
-  }
-}
-```
-
-**Usage:**
-```bash
-$ nimbus run
-
-☁️  Loading config from nimbus.json...
-
-  Prompt: ./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md (2.4 KB)
-  Models: Claude Sonnet 4, GPT-4o
-  Deploy: Cloudflare Pages
-
-? Start builds? (Y/n) 
-```
-
-**Options:**
-```bash
-nimbus run                      # Use ./nimbus.json
-nimbus run --config ./other.json   # Use specific config file
-nimbus run --yes                # Skip confirmation
-```

-

-
-**Share link:**
-
-The "Copy Share Link" button generates a URL that encodes the job IDs:
-```
-https://nimbus.yoursite.dev/compare?ids=nimbus_abc123,nimbus_def456,nimbus_ghi789
-```
-
-When opened, this fetches the job data from the Nimbus API and renders the comparison.
-
-**Limits:**
-- Maximum 4 builds per comparison (UI constraint)
-- All builds must be from the same Nimbus deployment (same API)

-### 11.4 Prompt Files
-
-For complex specifications, users can write prompts in markdown or text files.
-
-**Example: `./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md`**
-
-```markdown
-# E-Commerce Site Specification
-
-Build a modern e-commerce website with the following requirements:
-
-## Tech Stack
-- Astro for the framework
-- React for interactive components  
-- use-shopping-cart for cart management
-- Tailwind CSS for styling
-- TypeScript throughout
-
-## Pages
-
-### Home Page
-- Hero section with featured products
-- Product categories grid
-- Newsletter signup
-
-### Products Page
-- Grid of products with filtering by category
-- Sort by price, name, newest
-- Pagination or infinite scroll
-
-### Product Detail Page
-- Large product images with gallery
-- Product description and specs
-- Add to cart with quantity selector
-- Related products section
-
-### Cart Page
-- List of cart items with quantity controls
-- Remove item functionality
-- Cart total with subtotal breakdown
-- Proceed to checkout button
-
-### Checkout Page
-- Shipping information form
-- Order summary
-- Form validation
-
-## Sample Data
-Include 8 sample products across 3 categories:
-- Electronics (3 products)
-- Clothing (3 products)  
-- Home & Garden (2 products)
-
-Each product should have:
-- Name, description, price
-- Placeholder image (use https://placehold.co)
-- Category
-- SKU
-
-## Design Requirements
-- Responsive (mobile-first)
-- Dark mode support
-- Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)
-- Loading states for async operations
-```
-
-**Loading in CLI:**
-```
-$ nimbus start
-
-? How would you like to provide your prompt?
-    Type it in
-  › Load from file
-
-? Select a prompt file:
-  › ./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md    (2.4 KB, 847 words)
-    
-  Preview:
-  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-  │ # E-Commerce Site Specification                                │
-  │                                                                │
-  │ Build a modern e-commerce website with the following           │
-  │ requirements:                                                  │
-  │                                                                │
-  │ ## Tech Stack                                                  │
-  │ - Astro for the framework                                      │
-  │ - React for interactive components                             │
-  │ ...                                                            │
-  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-
-? Use this prompt? (Y/n)
-```

-
-## 12. Configuration
-
-### 12.1 Environment Variables
-
-```bash
-# .env (user creates from .env.example)
-
-# Required
-OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-...
-
-# Optional - for GitHub integration
-GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
-
-# Optional - for Cloudflare Pages deployment
-CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=...
-CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=...
-
-# Optional - webhook notifications
-WEBHOOK_URL=https://your-webhook.com/notify
-```
-
-### 12.2 Wrangler Configuration
-
-```toml
-# wrangler.toml
-
-name = "[project-name]"
-main = "src/index.ts"
-compatibility_date = "2025-01-21"
-compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]
-
-# ============================================
-# USER CONFIGURATION
-# ============================================
-
-[vars]
-# Default settings (can be overridden per-job)
-DEFAULT_MODEL = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-MAX_AUTO_RETRIES = 3
-BUILD_TIMEOUT = "1h"
-CONCURRENT_BUILDS_LIMIT = 2
-

-## 13. Metrics & Reporting
-
-### 13.1 Collected Metrics
-
-#### Per-Variant Metrics
-
-| Category | Metric | Description |
-|----------|--------|-------------|
-| **LLM** | `totalLLMCalls` | Number of LLM API calls |
-| | `totalPromptTokens` | Total input tokens |
-| | `totalCompletionTokens` | Total output tokens |
-| | `totalTokens` | Sum of prompt + completion |
-| | `totalCost` | Total LLM cost in USD |
-| | `avgLLMLatencyMs` | Average response time |
-| **Build** | `totalBuildAttempts` | Number of build tries |
-| | `successfulBuilds` | Builds that succeeded |
-| | `failedBuilds` | Builds that failed |
-| | `totalDurationMs` | Total time from start to completion |
-| **Errors** | `totalErrors` | All errors encountered |
-| | `autoRecoveredErrors` | Errors fixed by LLM |
-| | `humanEscalatedErrors` | Errors requiring human help |
-| **Code** | `filesGenerated` | Number of files created |
-| | `linesOfCode` | Total lines of code |
-
-#### Per-Job Aggregate Metrics
-
-| Metric | Description |
-|--------|-------------|
-| `totalVariants` | Number of variants attempted |
-| `completedVariants` | Variants that completed successfully |
-| `failedVariants` | Variants that failed |
-| `totalCost` | Sum of all variant costs |
-| `totalTokens` | Sum of all variant tokens |
-| `totalDurationMs` | Time from job start to completion |
-
-### 13.2 Report Template
-
-**File:** `src/lib/metrics/report-template.ts`
-
-```typescript

-

-| **Total Duration** | ${formatDuration(job.totalDurationMs)} |
-| **Total Cost** | $${job.totalCost.toFixed(4)} |
-| **Total Tokens** | ${job.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} |
-| **Variants** | ${job.completedVariants}/${job.totalVariants} completed |
-
----
-
-## Original Prompt
-
-> ${job.prompt}
-
----
-
-${job.variants.map(v => generateVariantSection(v)).join('\n\n---\n\n')}
-
----
-
-## Comparison
-
-### Cost Efficiency
-
-| Variant | Framework | Model | Tokens | Cost | Cost/1K Tokens |
-|---------|-----------|-------|--------|------|----------------|
-${job.variants.map(v => 
-  `| ${v.id} | ${v.framework} | ${v.model.split('/')[1]} | ${v.metrics.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} | $${v.metrics.totalCost.toFixed(4)} | $${(v.metrics.totalCost / v.metrics.totalTokens * 1000).toFixed(4)} |`
-).join('\n')}
-

-| Variant | Framework | Model | Duration | Attempts | Errors | Recovery Rate |

-}).join('\n')}

-| Variant | Framework | Model | Files | Lines of Code |

-).join('\n')}
-
----
-

-| Variant | Preview | Production | GitHub |

-\`\`\`mermaid

-    axisFormat %M:%S

-${job.variants.map(v => {
-  const startOffset = v.startedAt ? new Date(v.startedAt).getTime() - new Date(job.startedAt!).getTime() : 0;
-  return `    section ${v.framework} (${v.model.split('/')[1]})
-    ${v.status}: ${startOffset}, ${v.metrics.totalDurationMs || 0}ms`;

-\`\`\`

-
-function generateVariantSection(v: Variant): string {
-  return `## ${v.framework.toUpperCase()} + ${v.model.split('/')[1].toUpperCase()}
-
-| Metric | Value |
-|--------|-------|
-| **Status** | ${statusEmoji(v.status)} ${v.status} |
-| **Duration** | ${formatDuration(v.metrics.totalDurationMs)} |
-| **Cost** | $${v.metrics.totalCost.toFixed(4)} |
-| **Tokens** | ${v.metrics.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} |
-| **Build Attempts** | ${v.metrics.totalBuildAttempts} |
-| **Errors** | ${v.metrics.totalErrors} (${v.metrics.autoRecoveredErrors} auto-recovered) |
-
-${v.deployedUrl ? `🔗 **Live URL:** [${v.deployedUrl}](${v.deployedUrl})` : ''}
-${v.previewUrl ? `👁️ **Preview:** [${v.previewUrl}](${v.previewUrl})` : ''}
-${v.githubUrl ? `📦 **Source:** [${v.githubUrl}](${v.githubUrl})` : ''}`;
-}
-
-function generateDetailedVariantReport(v: Variant): string {
-  return `### ${v.id}: ${v.framework} + ${v.model}
-
-#### LLM Calls
-
-| # | Purpose | Tokens | Cost | Latency |
-|---|---------|--------|------|---------|
-${v.llmCalls?.map((call, i) => 
-  `| ${i + 1} | ${call.purpose} | ${call.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} | $${call.cost.toFixed(4)} | ${call.latencyMs}ms |`
-).join('\n') || 'No LLM call data available'}
-
-#### Build Attempts
-
-| # | Duration | Result |
-|---|----------|--------|
-${v.buildAttempts?.map(b => 
-  `| ${b.attemptNumber} | ${b.durationMs}ms | ${b.success ? '✅ Success' : `❌ ${b.errorSummary?.slice(0, 50) || 'Failed'}`} |`
-).join('\n') || 'No build attempt data available'}
-
-${v.errors && v.errors.length > 0 ? `
-#### Errors
-
-${v.errors.map(e => `
-- **${e.errorType}** (${e.resolved ? '✅ Resolved' : '❌ Unresolved'})
-  - Message: \`${e.message.slice(0, 100)}\`
-  - Recovery: ${e.recoveryMethod} (${e.recoveryAttempts} attempts)
-`).join('\n')}
-` : ''}`;
-}
-
-function statusEmoji(status: string): string {
-  const map: Record<string, string> = {
-    completed: '✅',
-    failed: '❌',
-    running: '🔄',
-    pending: '⏳',
-    waiting_for_human: '👤',
-    skipped: '⏭️',
-    cancelled: '🚫'
-  };
-  return map[status] || '❓';
-}
-
-function formatDuration(ms?: number): string {
-  if (!ms) return 'N/A';
-  
-  const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);
-  const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);
-  const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);
-  
-  if (hours > 0) {
-    return `${hours}h ${minutes % 60}m ${seconds % 60}s`;
-  }
-  if (minutes > 0) {
-    return `${minutes}m ${seconds % 60}s`;
-  }
-  return `${seconds}s`;
-}
-```
-

-
-## 14. Error Handling & Recovery
-
-### 14.1 Error Categories
-
-| Category | Examples | Recovery Strategy |
-|----------|----------|-------------------|
-| **Build Errors** | Compilation failures, missing dependencies, type errors | LLM attempts to fix; escalate after max retries |
-| **Runtime Errors** | Dev server crashes, import errors | Restart sandbox, retry build |
-| **Timeout Errors** | Build takes too long, LLM times out | Retry with smaller context or escalate |
-| **Sandbox Errors** | Container OOM, disk full, network issues | Recreate sandbox, retry |
-| **LLM Errors** | API rate limits, invalid responses | Retry with backoff; try different model |
-
-### 14.2 Recovery Flow
-
-```
-                    ┌─────────────┐
-                    │   Error     │
-                    │  Detected   │
-                    └──────┬──────┘
-                           │
-                           ▼
-                    ┌─────────────┐
-                    │ Classify    │
-                    │   Error     │
-                    └──────┬──────┘
-                           │
-           ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
-           ▼               ▼               ▼
-    ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
-    │ Recoverable │ │ Potentially │ │   Fatal     │
-    │   (auto)    │ │ Recoverable │ │             │
-    └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘
-           │               │               │
-           ▼               ▼               ▼
-    ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
-    │  Retry or   │ │ LLM Fix     │ │  Mark as    │
-    │  Auto-fix   │ │ Attempt     │ │  Failed     │
-    └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ └─────────────┘
-           │               │
-           │      ┌────────┴────────┐
-           │      ▼                 ▼
-           │ ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
-           │ │ Success │     │ Exhausted   │
-           │ └────┬────┘     │  Retries    │
-           │      │          └──────┬──────┘
-           │      │                 │
-           └──────┴─────────────────┤
-                                    ▼
-                             ┌─────────────┐
-                             │  Escalate   │
-                             │  to Human   │
-                             └─────────────┘
-```
-
-### 14.3 Error Classification
-
-```typescript
-// src/lib/errors/classifier.ts
-
-export function classifyError(error: string, context: BuildContext): ErrorClassification {
-  // Missing dependency
-  if (error.includes('Module not found') || error.includes('Cannot find module')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'build_error',
-      subtype: 'missing_dependency',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'llm_fix',
-      hint: 'Add the missing package to dependencies'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // Type errors
-  if (error.includes('Type error') || error.includes('TS')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'build_error',
-      subtype: 'type_error',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'llm_fix',
-      hint: 'Fix TypeScript type issues'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // Syntax errors
-  if (error.includes('SyntaxError') || error.includes('Unexpected token')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'build_error',
-      subtype: 'syntax_error',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'llm_fix',
-      hint: 'Fix syntax error in generated code'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // Out of memory
-  if (error.includes('ENOMEM') || error.includes('out of memory')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'sandbox_error',
-      subtype: 'oom',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'recreate_sandbox',
-      hint: 'Sandbox ran out of memory, recreating'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // Timeout
-  if (error.includes('timeout') || error.includes('ETIMEDOUT')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'timeout',
-      subtype: 'build_timeout',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'retry',
-      hint: 'Build timed out, retrying'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // LLM rate limit
-  if (error.includes('rate_limit') || error.includes('429')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'llm_error',
-      subtype: 'rate_limit',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'backoff_retry',
-      hint: 'LLM rate limited, waiting and retrying'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // LLM invalid response
-  if (error.includes('Failed to parse LLM response')) {
-    return {
-      type: 'llm_error',
-      subtype: 'invalid_response',
-      recoverable: true,
-      strategy: 'retry',
-      hint: 'LLM returned invalid JSON, retrying'
-    };
-  }
-  
-  // Unknown - default to potentially recoverable
-  return {
-    type: 'build_error',
-    subtype: 'unknown',
-    recoverable: true,
-    strategy: 'llm_fix',
-    hint: 'Unknown error, attempting LLM fix'
-  };
-}
-```
-

-
-## 15. Security Considerations
-
-### 15.1 Threat Model
-
-| Threat | Mitigation |
-|--------|------------|
-| **API Key Exposure** | Keys stored in Wrangler secrets, never logged |
-| **Sandbox Escape** | Cloudflare Sandboxes provide container isolation |
-| **Malicious Code Generation** | Sandboxes are ephemeral, destroyed after use |
-| **Data Exfiltration** | Sandboxes have limited network access |
-| **Prompt Injection** | LLM output is always treated as untrusted code |
-| **Denial of Service** | Configurable limits on concurrent builds |
-
-### 15.2 Best Practices
-
-1. **Secrets Management**
-   - All API keys stored via `wrangler secret put`
-   - Never logged or included in error messages
-   - Rotated periodically
-
-2. **Sandbox Isolation**
-   - Each variant gets its own sandbox
-   - Sandboxes destroyed after job completion
-   - No persistent state between jobs
-
-3. **Input Validation**
-   - Prompt length limits
-   - Framework/model validation against registries
-   - Rate limiting per job
-
-4. **Output Sanitization**
-   - LLM responses parsed, not executed directly
-   - Build output truncated in logs
-   - Sensitive data stripped from reports
-

-## 17. Implementation Guide (Vertical Slices)
-
-This project uses **vertical slice architecture** — each slice delivers a complete, working feature end-to-end rather than building horizontal layers across the whole system.
-
-### 17.1 Principles
-
-Each slice must be:
-- **Deployable** — You could ship it to users
-- **Testable** — You can verify it works
-- **Valuable** — It does something useful, even if limited
-
-```
-❌ Horizontal (risky):          ✅ Vertical (safe):
-                                
-Week 1: Entire DB schema        Week 1: Single build works E2E
-Week 2: Entire API layer        Week 2: Can see build progress
-Week 3: Entire workflow         Week 3: Auto-retry works
-Week 4: Entire CLI              Week 4: Human escalation works
-Week 5: Hope it works 🤞        Week 5: Multi-variant works
-                                ...each week = something WORKS
-```
-
----
-
-### 17.2 Slice Overview
-
-| Slice | Feature | Time Est. | Cumulative |
-|-------|---------|-----------|------------|
-| 1 | Hello World Build | 3-5 days | 5 days |
-| 2 | Build Visibility | 2-3 days | 8 days |
-| 3 | Auto-Recovery | 2-3 days | 11 days |
-| 4 | Human Escalation | 3-4 days | 15 days |
-| 5 | Multi-Model Parallel | 3-4 days | 19 days |
-| 6 | Reports & Compare UI | 3-4 days | 23 days |
-| 7 | Change Model | 1-2 days | 25 days |
-| 8 | GitHub Export | 2 days | 27 days |
-| 9 | Prompt Templates | 2-3 days | 30 days |
-| 10 | Polish & Docs | 3-5 days | 35 days |
-
-**Key insight:** After Slice 1 (~5 days), you have something that *works*. Every slice after that adds value to a working system.
-
----
-
-### Slice 1: "Hello World" Build
-
-**Goal:** User can trigger a build and get a deployed site.
-
-**Scope:**
-```
-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│  Input:  POST /api/jobs { prompt, framework, model }       │
-│                         (single variant only)               │
-│                                                             │
-│  Process:                                                   │
-│    1. Create job record in D1 (minimal schema)             │
-│    2. Call OpenRouter (hardcoded prompt template)          │
-│    3. Write files to sandbox                                │
-│    4. Run npm build                                         │
-│    5. If success → deploy to Pages                         │
-│    6. If fail → mark as failed (no retry yet)              │
-│                                                             │
-│  Output: GET /api/jobs/:id returns { status, deployedUrl } │
-│                                                             │
-│  CLI: build --prompt "..." --framework nextjs --model ...  │
-│        status <jobId>                                       │
-└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-```
-

-### Slice 3: Auto-Recovery
-
-**Goal:** Failed builds automatically retry with LLM fixes.
-
-**Scope:**
-```
-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│  New capabilities:                                          │
-│    - On build failure, LLM is asked to fix the error       │
-│    - Up to N retries (configurable, default 3)             │
-│    - Each attempt logged with metrics                       │
-│    - Error classification (what type of error)             │
-│                                                             │
-│  Config: --max-retries 3                                   │
-└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-```
-
-**What to Build:**
-
-1. **Schema Additions**
-   ```sql
-   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN max_retries INTEGER DEFAULT 3;
-   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN current_attempt INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
-   
-   ALTER TABLE build_attempts ADD COLUMN error_type TEXT;
-   ALTER TABLE build_attempts ADD COLUMN recovery_action TEXT;
-   ```
-
-2. **Error Classifier**
-   ```typescript
-   // src/lib/errors/classifier.ts
-   export function classifyError(stderr: string): ErrorClassification {
-     if (stderr.includes('Module not found')) {
-       return { type: 'missing_dependency', recoverable: true };
-     }
-     if (stderr.includes('SyntaxError')) {
-       return { type: 'syntax_error', recoverable: true };
-     }
-     // ... more patterns
-   }
-   ```
-
-3. **Code Generator Updates**
-   - New "error_fix" mode that includes error context
-   - Prompt template for fixing specific errors
-
-4. **Workflow Updates**
-   ```typescript
-   async run(ctx, event) {
-     let attempt = 0;
-     let code = await ctx.step.do('generate-initial', ...);
-     
-     while (attempt < maxRetries) {
-       attempt++;
-       const result = await ctx.step.do(`build-${attempt}`, ...);
-       
-       if (result.success) {
-         // Deploy and complete
-         return { status: 'completed' };
-       }
-       
-       if (attempt < maxRetries) {
-         // Ask LLM to fix
-         code = await ctx.step.do(`fix-${attempt}`, () =>
-           this.generateFix(code, result.error)
-         );
-       }
-     }
-     
-     // Exhausted retries
-     return { status: 'failed', error: lastError };
-   }
-   ```
-
-5. **CLI Updates**
-   - `build --max-retries 5` option
-   - `watch` shows attempt number
-
-**What's NOT Included:**
-- Human escalation (still just fails after max retries)
-- Multiple variants
-- Reports
-
-**Definition of Done:**
-- [ ] Build with missing dependency → LLM adds it → build succeeds
-- [ ] Build with syntax error → LLM fixes it → build succeeds
-- [ ] Can see "Attempt 1 failed... Attempt 2 succeeded" in watch
-- [ ] After N failures, job is marked failed (not stuck forever)
-- [ ] Each attempt's error is logged and visible

-src/lib/llm/prompts.ts          # Add error fix prompt
-src/lib/llm/code-generator.ts   # Add fix mode
-src/workflows/builder.ts        # Retry loop
-
-db/migrations/0003_retries.sql
-```

-**Estimated Time:** 2-3 days

-### Slice 4: Human Escalation
-
-**Goal:** User can intervene when auto-recovery fails.
-
-**Scope:**
-```
-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│  New capabilities:                                          │
-│    - After max retries, status = "waiting_for_human"       │
-│    - Workflow pauses with waitForEvent                     │
-│    - User can: retry, skip, cancel, provide instructions   │
-│    - Workflow resumes based on user action                  │
-│                                                             │
-│  API: POST /api/jobs/:id/respond                           │
-│                                                             │
-│  CLI: respond <jobId> --action retry                       │
-│        respond <jobId> --action fix --instructions "..."   │
-│        respond <jobId> --action skip                       │
-│        respond <jobId> --action cancel                     │
-└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-```
-
-**What to Build:**
-
-1. **Schema Additions**
-   ```sql
-   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN waiting_for_human INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
-   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN escalation_reason TEXT;
-   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN escalation_error TEXT;
-   
-   CREATE TABLE human_interventions (
-     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
-     job_id TEXT REFERENCES jobs(id),
-     requested_at TEXT,
-     responded_at TEXT,
-     action TEXT,
-     instructions TEXT,
-     outcome TEXT
-   );
-   ```
-
-2. **API Endpoint**
-   ```typescript
-   // POST /api/jobs/:id/respond
-   // Body: { action: 'retry' | 'fix' | 'skip' | 'cancel', instructions?: string }
-   
-   async function handleRespond(jobId: string, body: RespondRequest) {
-     // Get the workflow instance
-     const instance = await env.BUILDER.get(jobId);
-     
-     // Send event to resume workflow
-     await instance.sendEvent({
-       type: 'human_response',
-       action: body.action,
-       instructions: body.instructions
-     });
-     
-     return { success: true };
-   }
-   ```
-
-3. **Workflow Updates**
-   ```typescript
-   // After exhausting retries:
-   await ctx.step.do('escalate', () => this.markWaitingForHuman(jobId, lastError));
-   
-   // Wait for human (24h timeout)
-   const response = await ctx.step.waitForEvent('human_response', { timeout: '24h' });
-   
-   if (!response) {
-     return { status: 'failed', reason: 'escalation_timeout' };
-   }
-   
-   switch (response.action) {
-     case 'retry':
-       // Reset and try again
-       break;
-     case 'fix':
-       // Generate with human instructions
-       code = await this.generateWithInstructions(response.instructions);
-       break;
-     case 'skip':
-       return { status: 'skipped' };
-     case 'cancel':
-       return { status: 'cancelled' };
-   }
-   ```
-
-4. **CLI Commands**
-   - `respond <jobId> --action retry`
-   - `respond <jobId> --action fix --instructions "..."`
-   - `respond <jobId> --action skip`
-   - `respond <jobId> --action cancel`
-   - Update `watch` to show escalation state and prompt for action
-
-**What's NOT Included:**
-- Multiple variants
-- Change model option (coming in Slice 7)
-- Reports
-
-**Definition of Done:**
-- [ ] Build fails 3 times → status shows "waiting_for_human"
-- [ ] `watch` clearly shows the error and available actions
-- [ ] `respond --action retry` resumes the build
-- [ ] `respond --action fix --instructions "..."` feeds context to LLM
-- [ ] `respond --action skip` marks job as skipped
-- [ ] After 24h with no response, job auto-fails
-

-src/workflows/builder.ts        # Add waitForEvent + handling
-
-cli/src/commands/respond.ts     # New command
-cli/src/commands/watch.ts       # Show escalation UI
-
-db/migrations/0004_escalation.sql
-```

-**Estimated Time:** 3-4 days

-**What to Build:**
-
-1. **Schema Refactor**
-   ```sql
-   -- Jobs table becomes parent only
-   CREATE TABLE jobs_v2 (
-     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
-     prompt TEXT NOT NULL,
-     status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
-     config TEXT,  -- JSON
-     created_at TEXT,
-     started_at TEXT,
-     completed_at TEXT,
-     total_builds INTEGER,
-     completed_builds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-     failed_builds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-     total_cost REAL DEFAULT 0,
-     total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0
-   );
-   
-   -- New builds table (one per model)
-   CREATE TABLE builds (
-     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
-     job_id TEXT REFERENCES jobs_v2(id),
-     model TEXT NOT NULL,
-     status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
-     current_phase TEXT,
-     -- ... all the fields that were on jobs
-     preview_url TEXT,
-     deployed_url TEXT,
-     total_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
-     total_cost REAL DEFAULT 0,
-     -- etc.
-   );
-   
-   -- Update llm_calls and build_attempts to reference build_id
-   ```
-
-2. **Two-Workflow Architecture**
-   ```typescript
-   // OrchestratorWorkflow - manages the job
-   async run(ctx, event) {
-     const { jobId, prompt, models, config } = event.payload;
-     
-     // Spawn all build workflows in parallel
-     for (const model of models) {
-       await ctx.step.do(`spawn-${model}`, () =>
-         this.env.BUILDER.create({
-           id: `${jobId}-${model}`,
-           params: { jobId, buildId: `${jobId}-${model}`, prompt, model }
-         })
-       );
-     }
-     
-     // Wait for all to complete
-     let completed = 0;
-     while (completed < models.length) {
-       const event = await ctx.step.waitForEvent('build_done', { timeout: '2h' });
-       completed++;
-       // Update job progress
-     }
-     
-     // Finalize job
-     return { status: 'completed' };
-   }
-   
-   // BuilderWorkflow - builds with one model (existing logic)
-   async run(ctx, event) {
-     // ... existing build logic from Slices 1-4
-     
-     // When done, notify orchestrator
-     await this.notifyOrchestrator(jobId, buildId, status, metrics);
-   }
-   ```

-   - `GET /api/jobs/:id` returns nested build statuses
-   - `POST /api/jobs/:id/builds/:buildId/respond` for per-build escalation

-   - `nimbus watch` shows all builds with their individual progress
-   - `nimbus respond` handles specific builds
