**Share link:**

The "Copy Share Link" button generates a URL that encodes the job IDs:
```
https://nimbus.yoursite.dev/compare?ids=nimbus_abc123,nimbus_def456,nimbus_ghi789
```

When opened, this fetches the job data from the Nimbus API and renders the comparison.

**Limits:**
- Maximum 4 builds per comparison (UI constraint)
- All builds must be from the same Nimbus deployment (same API)

### 11.4 Prompt Files

For complex specifications, users can write prompts in markdown or text files.

**Example: `./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md`**

```markdown
# E-Commerce Site Specification

Build a modern e-commerce website with the following requirements:

## Tech Stack
- Astro for the framework
- React for interactive components  
- use-shopping-cart for cart management
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- TypeScript throughout

## Pages

### Home Page
- Hero section with featured products
- Product categories grid
- Newsletter signup

### Products Page
- Grid of products with filtering by category
- Sort by price, name, newest
- Pagination or infinite scroll

### Product Detail Page
- Large product images with gallery
- Product description and specs
- Add to cart with quantity selector
- Related products section

### Cart Page
- List of cart items with quantity controls
- Remove item functionality
- Cart total with subtotal breakdown
- Proceed to checkout button

### Checkout Page
- Shipping information form
- Order summary
- Form validation

## Sample Data
Include 8 sample products across 3 categories:
- Electronics (3 products)
- Clothing (3 products)  
- Home & Garden (2 products)

Each product should have:
- Name, description, price
- Placeholder image (use https://placehold.co)
- Category
- SKU

## Design Requirements
- Responsive (mobile-first)
- Dark mode support
- Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Loading states for async operations
```

**Loading in CLI:**
```
$ nimbus start

? How would you like to provide your prompt?
    Type it in
  › Load from file

? Select a prompt file:
  › ./prompts/ecommerce-spec.md    (2.4 KB, 847 words)
    
  Preview:
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ # E-Commerce Site Specification                                │
  │                                                                │
  │ Build a modern e-commerce website with the following           │
  │ requirements:                                                  │
  │                                                                │
  │ ## Tech Stack                                                  │
  │ - Astro for the framework                                      │
  │ - React for interactive components                             │
  │ ...                                                            │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

? Use this prompt? (Y/n)
```

### 12.2 Wrangler Configuration

```toml
# wrangler.toml

name = "[project-name]"
main = "src/index.ts"
compatibility_date = "2025-01-21"
compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]

# ============================================
# USER CONFIGURATION
# ============================================

[vars]
# Default settings (can be overridden per-job)
DEFAULT_MODEL = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
MAX_AUTO_RETRIES = 3
BUILD_TIMEOUT = "1h"
CONCURRENT_BUILDS_LIMIT = 2

# Job retention (days)
JOB_RETENTION_DAYS = 30

# ============================================
# INFRASTRUCTURE
# ============================================

# D1 Database
[[d1_databases]]
binding = "DB"
database_name = "[project-name]-db"
database_id = "auto"  # Will be populated by setup script

# R2 Bucket
[[r2_buckets]]
binding = "ARTIFACTS"
bucket_name = "[project-name]-artifacts"

# KV Namespace
[[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "CONFIG"
id = "auto"  # Will be populated by setup script

# Workflows
[[workflows]]
name = "orchestrator"

class_name = "OrchestratorWorkflow"

[[workflows]]
name = "variant-builder"
binding = "VARIANT_BUILDER"
class_name = "VariantBuilderWorkflow"

# Sandboxes (Containers)
[containers]
dockerfile = "./docker/Dockerfile"
max_instances = 10

[[durable_objects.bindings]]

## 13. Metrics & Reporting

### 13.1 Collected Metrics

#### Per-Variant Metrics

| Category | Metric | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| **LLM** | `totalLLMCalls` | Number of LLM API calls |
| | `totalPromptTokens` | Total input tokens |
| | `totalCompletionTokens` | Total output tokens |
| | `totalTokens` | Sum of prompt + completion |
| | `totalCost` | Total LLM cost in USD |
| | `avgLLMLatencyMs` | Average response time |
| **Build** | `totalBuildAttempts` | Number of build tries |
| | `successfulBuilds` | Builds that succeeded |
| | `failedBuilds` | Builds that failed |
| | `totalDurationMs` | Total time from start to completion |
| **Errors** | `totalErrors` | All errors encountered |
| | `autoRecoveredErrors` | Errors fixed by LLM |
| | `humanEscalatedErrors` | Errors requiring human help |
| **Code** | `filesGenerated` | Number of files created |
| | `linesOfCode` | Total lines of code |

#### Per-Job Aggregate Metrics

| Metric | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `totalVariants` | Number of variants attempted |
| `completedVariants` | Variants that completed successfully |
| `failedVariants` | Variants that failed |
| `totalCost` | Sum of all variant costs |
| `totalTokens` | Sum of all variant tokens |
| `totalDurationMs` | Time from job start to completion |

### 13.2 Report Template

**File:** `src/lib/metrics/report-template.ts`

```typescript
export function generateReport(job: Job): string {
  return `# Build Report: ${job.id}

> Generated by Nimbus on ${new Date().toISOString()}

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Status** | ${statusEmoji(job.status)} ${job.status} |
| **Prompt** | ${job.prompt.slice(0, 100)}${job.prompt.length > 100 ? '...' : ''} |
| **Total Duration** | ${formatDuration(job.totalDurationMs)} |
| **Total Cost** | $${job.totalCost.toFixed(4)} |
| **Total Tokens** | ${job.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} |
| **Variants** | ${job.completedVariants}/${job.totalVariants} completed |

---

## Original Prompt

> ${job.prompt}

---

${job.variants.map(v => generateVariantSection(v)).join('\n\n---\n\n')}

---

## Comparison

### Cost Efficiency

| Variant | Framework | Model | Tokens | Cost | Cost/1K Tokens |
|---------|-----------|-------|--------|------|----------------|
${job.variants.map(v => 
  `| ${v.id} | ${v.framework} | ${v.model.split('/')[1]} | ${v.metrics.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} | $${v.metrics.totalCost.toFixed(4)} | $${(v.metrics.totalCost / v.metrics.totalTokens * 1000).toFixed(4)} |`
).join('\n')}

### Build Performance

| Variant | Framework | Model | Duration | Attempts | Errors | Recovery Rate |
|---------|-----------|-------|----------|----------|--------|---------------|
${job.variants.map(v => {
  const recoveryRate = v.metrics.totalErrors > 0 
    ? `${((v.metrics.autoRecoveredErrors / v.metrics.totalErrors) * 100).toFixed(0)}%`
    : 'N/A';
  return `| ${v.id} | ${v.framework} | ${v.model.split('/')[1]} | ${formatDuration(v.metrics.totalDurationMs)} | ${v.metrics.totalBuildAttempts} | ${v.metrics.totalErrors} | ${recoveryRate} |`;
}).join('\n')}

### Code Output

| Variant | Framework | Model | Files | Lines of Code |
|---------|-----------|-------|-------|---------------|
${job.variants.filter(v => v.metrics.filesGenerated).map(v => 
  `| ${v.id} | ${v.framework} | ${v.model.split('/')[1]} | ${v.metrics.filesGenerated} | ${v.metrics.linesOfCode?.toLocaleString() || 'N/A'} |`
).join('\n')}

---

## Deployments

| Variant | Preview | Production | GitHub |
|---------|---------|------------|--------|
${job.variants.map(v => 
  `| ${v.framework} (${v.model.split('/')[1]}) | ${v.previewUrl ? `[Preview](${v.previewUrl})` : 'N/A'} | ${v.deployedUrl ? `[Live](${v.deployedUrl})` : 'N/A'} | ${v.githubUrl ? `[Repo](${v.githubUrl})` : 'N/A'} |`
).join('\n')}

---

## Timeline

\`\`\`mermaid
gantt
    title Build Timeline
    dateFormat x
    axisFormat %M:%S
    
${job.variants.map(v => {
  const startOffset = v.startedAt ? new Date(v.startedAt).getTime() - new Date(job.startedAt!).getTime() : 0;
  return `    section ${v.framework} (${v.model.split('/')[1]})
    ${v.status}: ${startOffset}, ${v.metrics.totalDurationMs || 0}ms`;
}).join('\n')}
\`\`\`

---

## Detailed Variant Reports

${job.variants.map(v => generateDetailedVariantReport(v)).join('\n\n')}

---

*Report generated by Nimbus v${VERSION}*
`;
}

function generateVariantSection(v: Variant): string {
  return `## ${v.framework.toUpperCase()} + ${v.model.split('/')[1].toUpperCase()}

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Status** | ${statusEmoji(v.status)} ${v.status} |
| **Duration** | ${formatDuration(v.metrics.totalDurationMs)} |
| **Cost** | $${v.metrics.totalCost.toFixed(4)} |
| **Tokens** | ${v.metrics.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} |
| **Build Attempts** | ${v.metrics.totalBuildAttempts} |
| **Errors** | ${v.metrics.totalErrors} (${v.metrics.autoRecoveredErrors} auto-recovered) |

${v.deployedUrl ? `🔗 **Live URL:** [${v.deployedUrl}](${v.deployedUrl})` : ''}
${v.previewUrl ? `👁️ **Preview:** [${v.previewUrl}](${v.previewUrl})` : ''}
${v.githubUrl ? `📦 **Source:** [${v.githubUrl}](${v.githubUrl})` : ''}`;
}

function generateDetailedVariantReport(v: Variant): string {
  return `### ${v.id}: ${v.framework} + ${v.model}

#### LLM Calls

| # | Purpose | Tokens | Cost | Latency |
|---|---------|--------|------|---------|
${v.llmCalls?.map((call, i) => 
  `| ${i + 1} | ${call.purpose} | ${call.totalTokens.toLocaleString()} | $${call.cost.toFixed(4)} | ${call.latencyMs}ms |`
).join('\n') || 'No LLM call data available'}

#### Build Attempts

| # | Duration | Result |
|---|----------|--------|
${v.buildAttempts?.map(b => 
  `| ${b.attemptNumber} | ${b.durationMs}ms | ${b.success ? '✅ Success' : `❌ ${b.errorSummary?.slice(0, 50) || 'Failed'}`} |`
).join('\n') || 'No build attempt data available'}

${v.errors && v.errors.length > 0 ? `
#### Errors

${v.errors.map(e => `
- **${e.errorType}** (${e.resolved ? '✅ Resolved' : '❌ Unresolved'})
  - Message: \`${e.message.slice(0, 100)}\`
  - Recovery: ${e.recoveryMethod} (${e.recoveryAttempts} attempts)
`).join('\n')}
` : ''}`;
}

function statusEmoji(status: string): string {
  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    completed: '✅',
    failed: '❌',
    running: '🔄',
    pending: '⏳',
    waiting_for_human: '👤',
    skipped: '⏭️',
    cancelled: '🚫'
  };
  return map[status] || '❓';
}

function formatDuration(ms?: number): string {
  if (!ms) return 'N/A';
  
  const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);
  const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);
  const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);
  
  if (hours > 0) {
    return `${hours}h ${minutes % 60}m ${seconds % 60}s`;
  }
  if (minutes > 0) {
    return `${minutes}m ${seconds % 60}s`;
  }
  return `${seconds}s`;
}
```

## 14. Error Handling & Recovery

### 14.1 Error Categories

| Category | Examples | Recovery Strategy |
|----------|----------|-------------------|
| **Build Errors** | Compilation failures, missing dependencies, type errors | LLM attempts to fix; escalate after max retries |
| **Runtime Errors** | Dev server crashes, import errors | Restart sandbox, retry build |
| **Timeout Errors** | Build takes too long, LLM times out | Retry with smaller context or escalate |
| **Sandbox Errors** | Container OOM, disk full, network issues | Recreate sandbox, retry |
| **LLM Errors** | API rate limits, invalid responses | Retry with backoff; try different model |

### 14.2 Recovery Flow

```
                    ┌─────────────┐
                    │   Error     │
                    │  Detected   │
                    └──────┬──────┘
                           │
                           ▼
                    ┌─────────────┐
                    │ Classify    │
                    │   Error     │
                    └──────┬──────┘
                           │
           ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
           ▼               ▼               ▼
    ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
    │ Recoverable │ │ Potentially │ │   Fatal     │
    │   (auto)    │ │ Recoverable │ │             │
    └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘
           │               │               │
           ▼               ▼               ▼
    ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
    │  Retry or   │ │ LLM Fix     │ │  Mark as    │
    │  Auto-fix   │ │ Attempt     │ │  Failed     │
    └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ └─────────────┘
           │               │
           │      ┌────────┴────────┐
           │      ▼                 ▼
           │ ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
           │ │ Success │     │ Exhausted   │
           │ └────┬────┘     │  Retries    │
           │      │          └──────┬──────┘
           │      │                 │
           └──────┴─────────────────┤
                                    ▼
                             ┌─────────────┐
                             │  Escalate   │
                             │  to Human   │
                             └─────────────┘
```

### 14.3 Error Classification

```typescript
// src/lib/errors/classifier.ts

export function classifyError(error: string, context: BuildContext): ErrorClassification {
  // Missing dependency
  if (error.includes('Module not found') || error.includes('Cannot find module')) {
    return {
      type: 'build_error',
      subtype: 'missing_dependency',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'llm_fix',
      hint: 'Add the missing package to dependencies'
    };
  }
  
  // Type errors
  if (error.includes('Type error') || error.includes('TS')) {
    return {
      type: 'build_error',
      subtype: 'type_error',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'llm_fix',
      hint: 'Fix TypeScript type issues'
    };
  }
  
  // Syntax errors
  if (error.includes('SyntaxError') || error.includes('Unexpected token')) {
    return {
      type: 'build_error',
      subtype: 'syntax_error',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'llm_fix',
      hint: 'Fix syntax error in generated code'
    };
  }
  
  // Out of memory
  if (error.includes('ENOMEM') || error.includes('out of memory')) {
    return {
      type: 'sandbox_error',
      subtype: 'oom',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'recreate_sandbox',
      hint: 'Sandbox ran out of memory, recreating'
    };
  }
  
  // Timeout
  if (error.includes('timeout') || error.includes('ETIMEDOUT')) {
    return {
      type: 'timeout',
      subtype: 'build_timeout',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'retry',
      hint: 'Build timed out, retrying'
    };
  }
  
  // LLM rate limit
  if (error.includes('rate_limit') || error.includes('429')) {
    return {
      type: 'llm_error',
      subtype: 'rate_limit',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'backoff_retry',
      hint: 'LLM rate limited, waiting and retrying'
    };
  }
  
  // LLM invalid response
  if (error.includes('Failed to parse LLM response')) {
    return {
      type: 'llm_error',
      subtype: 'invalid_response',
      recoverable: true,
      strategy: 'retry',
      hint: 'LLM returned invalid JSON, retrying'
    };
  }
  
  // Unknown - default to potentially recoverable
  return {
    type: 'build_error',
    subtype: 'unknown',
    recoverable: true,
    strategy: 'llm_fix',
    hint: 'Unknown error, attempting LLM fix'
  };
}
```

## 15. Security Considerations

### 15.1 Threat Model

| Threat | Mitigation |
|--------|------------|
| **API Key Exposure** | Keys stored in Wrangler secrets, never logged |
| **Sandbox Escape** | Cloudflare Sandboxes provide container isolation |
| **Malicious Code Generation** | Sandboxes are ephemeral, destroyed after use |
| **Data Exfiltration** | Sandboxes have limited network access |
| **Prompt Injection** | LLM output is always treated as untrusted code |
| **Denial of Service** | Configurable limits on concurrent builds |

### 15.2 Best Practices

1. **Secrets Management**
   - All API keys stored via `wrangler secret put`
   - Never logged or included in error messages
   - Rotated periodically

2. **Sandbox Isolation**
   - Each variant gets its own sandbox
   - Sandboxes destroyed after job completion
   - No persistent state between jobs

3. **Input Validation**
   - Prompt length limits
   - Framework/model validation against registries
   - Rate limiting per job

4. **Output Sanitization**
   - LLM responses parsed, not executed directly
   - Build output truncated in logs
   - Sensitive data stripped from reports

## 17. Implementation Guide (Vertical Slices)

This project uses **vertical slice architecture** — each slice delivers a complete, working feature end-to-end rather than building horizontal layers across the whole system.

### 17.1 Principles

Each slice must be:
- **Deployable** — You could ship it to users
- **Testable** — You can verify it works
- **Valuable** — It does something useful, even if limited

```
❌ Horizontal (risky):          ✅ Vertical (safe):
                                
Week 1: Entire DB schema        Week 1: Single build works E2E
Week 2: Entire API layer        Week 2: Can see build progress
Week 3: Entire workflow         Week 3: Auto-retry works
Week 4: Entire CLI              Week 4: Human escalation works
Week 5: Hope it works 🤞        Week 5: Multi-variant works
                                ...each week = something WORKS
```

---

### 17.2 Slice Overview

| Slice | Feature | Time Est. | Cumulative |
|-------|---------|-----------|------------|
| 1 | Hello World Build | 3-5 days | 5 days |
| 2 | Build Visibility | 2-3 days | 8 days |
| 3 | Auto-Recovery | 2-3 days | 11 days |
| 4 | Human Escalation | 3-4 days | 15 days |
| 5 | Multi-Model Parallel | 3-4 days | 19 days |
| 6 | Reports & Compare UI | 3-4 days | 23 days |
| 7 | Change Model | 1-2 days | 25 days |
| 8 | GitHub Export | 2 days | 27 days |
| 9 | Prompt Templates | 2-3 days | 30 days |
| 10 | Polish & Docs | 3-5 days | 35 days |

**Key insight:** After Slice 1 (~5 days), you have something that *works*. Every slice after that adds value to a working system.

---

### Slice 1: "Hello World" Build

**Goal:** User can trigger a build and get a deployed site.

**Scope:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Input:  POST /api/jobs { prompt, framework, model }       │
│                         (single variant only)               │
│                                                             │
│  Process:                                                   │
│    1. Create job record in D1 (minimal schema)             │
│    2. Call OpenRouter (hardcoded prompt template)          │
│    3. Write files to sandbox                                │
│    4. Run npm build                                         │
│    5. If success → deploy to Pages                         │
│    6. If fail → mark as failed (no retry yet)              │
│                                                             │
│  Output: GET /api/jobs/:id returns { status, deployedUrl } │
│                                                             │
│  CLI: build --prompt "..." --framework nextjs --model ...  │
│        status <jobId>                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**What to Build:**

1. **Project Scaffolding**
   - `wrangler.toml` with D1, R2, Workflows, Sandboxes configured
   - `package.json` with dependencies
   - `tsconfig.json`
   - Directory structure (see Section 16)

   CREATE TABLE jobs (
     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     prompt TEXT NOT NULL,
     framework TEXT NOT NULL,
     model TEXT NOT NULL,
     status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
     deployed_url TEXT,
     error_message TEXT,

   );

3. **API Endpoints**
   - `POST /api/jobs` — Create job, trigger workflow, return job ID
   - `GET /api/jobs/:id` — Return job status

4. **Simple Workflow** (no orchestrator yet, just one workflow)
   ```typescript
   // Single workflow that does everything
   async run(ctx, event) {
     const { jobId, prompt, framework, model } = event.payload;
     
     // Step 1: Generate code
     const code = await ctx.step.do('generate', () => 
       this.generateCode(prompt, framework, model)
     );
     
     // Step 2: Write to sandbox and build
     const buildResult = await ctx.step.do('build', () =>
       this.buildInSandbox(jobId, framework, code)
     );
     
     if (!buildResult.success) {
       await this.markFailed(jobId, buildResult.error);
       return { status: 'failed' };
     }
     
     // Step 3: Deploy
     const url = await ctx.step.do('deploy', () =>
       this.deployToPages(jobId, framework)
     );
     
     await this.markCompleted(jobId, url);
     return { status: 'completed', url };
   }
   ```

5. **LLM Client** (simplified)
   - Call OpenRouter with hardcoded system prompt
   - Parse JSON response to extract files
   - No metrics tracking yet

6. **Sandbox Manager** (simplified)
   - Scaffold project with `create-next-app`
   - Write generated files
   - Run `npm install && npm run build`
   - No preview URL yet

7. **Pages Deployment**
   - Use Wrangler to deploy build output
   - Return deployed URL

8. **CLI Commands**
   - `build -p <prompt> -v <framework>:<model>`
   - `status <jobId>`

### Slice 3: Auto-Recovery

**Goal:** Failed builds automatically retry with LLM fixes.

**Scope:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  New capabilities:                                          │
│    - On build failure, LLM is asked to fix the error       │
│    - Up to N retries (configurable, default 3)             │
│    - Each attempt logged with metrics                       │
│    - Error classification (what type of error)             │
│                                                             │
│  Config: --max-retries 3                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**What to Build:**

1. **Schema Additions**
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN max_retries INTEGER DEFAULT 3;
   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN current_attempt INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
   
   ALTER TABLE build_attempts ADD COLUMN error_type TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE build_attempts ADD COLUMN recovery_action TEXT;
   ```

2. **Error Classifier**
   ```typescript
   // src/lib/errors/classifier.ts
   export function classifyError(stderr: string): ErrorClassification {
     if (stderr.includes('Module not found')) {
       return { type: 'missing_dependency', recoverable: true };
     }
     if (stderr.includes('SyntaxError')) {
       return { type: 'syntax_error', recoverable: true };
     }
     // ... more patterns
   }
   ```

3. **Code Generator Updates**
   - New "error_fix" mode that includes error context
   - Prompt template for fixing specific errors

4. **Workflow Updates**
   ```typescript
   async run(ctx, event) {
     let attempt = 0;
     let code = await ctx.step.do('generate-initial', ...);
     
     while (attempt < maxRetries) {
       attempt++;
       const result = await ctx.step.do(`build-${attempt}`, ...);
       
       if (result.success) {
         // Deploy and complete
         return { status: 'completed' };
       }
       
       if (attempt < maxRetries) {
         // Ask LLM to fix
         code = await ctx.step.do(`fix-${attempt}`, () =>
           this.generateFix(code, result.error)
         );
       }
     }
     
     // Exhausted retries
     return { status: 'failed', error: lastError };
   }
   ```

5. **CLI Updates**
   - `build --max-retries 5` option
   - `watch` shows attempt number

**What's NOT Included:**
- Human escalation (still just fails after max retries)
- Multiple variants
- Reports

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] Build with missing dependency → LLM adds it → build succeeds
- [ ] Build with syntax error → LLM fixes it → build succeeds
- [ ] Can see "Attempt 1 failed... Attempt 2 succeeded" in watch
- [ ] After N failures, job is marked failed (not stuck forever)
- [ ] Each attempt's error is logged and visible

### Slice 4: Human Escalation

**Goal:** User can intervene when auto-recovery fails.

**Scope:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  New capabilities:                                          │
│    - After max retries, status = "waiting_for_human"       │
│    - Workflow pauses with waitForEvent                     │
│    - User can: retry, skip, cancel, provide instructions   │
│    - Workflow resumes based on user action                  │
│                                                             │
│  API: POST /api/jobs/:id/respond                           │
│                                                             │
│  CLI: respond <jobId> --action retry                       │
│        respond <jobId> --action fix --instructions "..."   │
│        respond <jobId> --action skip                       │
│        respond <jobId> --action cancel                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**What to Build:**

1. **Schema Additions**
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN waiting_for_human INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN escalation_reason TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN escalation_error TEXT;
   
   CREATE TABLE human_interventions (
     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     job_id TEXT REFERENCES jobs(id),
     requested_at TEXT,
     responded_at TEXT,
     action TEXT,
     instructions TEXT,
     outcome TEXT
   );
   ```

2. **API Endpoint**
   ```typescript
   // POST /api/jobs/:id/respond
   // Body: { action: 'retry' | 'fix' | 'skip' | 'cancel', instructions?: string }
   
   async function handleRespond(jobId: string, body: RespondRequest) {
     // Get the workflow instance
     const instance = await env.BUILDER.get(jobId);
     
     // Send event to resume workflow
     await instance.sendEvent({
       type: 'human_response',
       action: body.action,
       instructions: body.instructions
     });
     
     return { success: true };
   }
   ```

3. **Workflow Updates**
   ```typescript
   // After exhausting retries:
   await ctx.step.do('escalate', () => this.markWaitingForHuman(jobId, lastError));
   
   // Wait for human (24h timeout)
   const response = await ctx.step.waitForEvent('human_response', { timeout: '24h' });
   
   if (!response) {
     return { status: 'failed', reason: 'escalation_timeout' };
   }
   
   switch (response.action) {
     case 'retry':
       // Reset and try again
       break;
     case 'fix':
       // Generate with human instructions
       code = await this.generateWithInstructions(response.instructions);
       break;
     case 'skip':
       return { status: 'skipped' };
     case 'cancel':
       return { status: 'cancelled' };
   }
   ```

4. **CLI Commands**
   - `respond <jobId> --action retry`
   - `respond <jobId> --action fix --instructions "..."`
   - `respond <jobId> --action skip`
   - `respond <jobId> --action cancel`
   - Update `watch` to show escalation state and prompt for action

**What's NOT Included:**
- Multiple variants
- Change model option (coming in Slice 7)
- Reports

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] Build fails 3 times → status shows "waiting_for_human"

- [ ] `respond --action retry` resumes the build
- [ ] `respond --action fix --instructions "..."` feeds context to LLM
- [ ] `respond --action skip` marks job as skipped

2. **Two-Workflow Architecture**
   ```typescript
   // OrchestratorWorkflow - manages the job
   async run(ctx, event) {
     const { jobId, prompt, models, config } = event.payload;
     
     // Spawn all build workflows in parallel
     for (const model of models) {
       await ctx.step.do(`spawn-${model}`, () =>
         this.env.BUILDER.create({
           id: `${jobId}-${model}`,
           params: { jobId, buildId: `${jobId}-${model}`, prompt, model }
         })
       );
     }
     
     // Wait for all to complete
     let completed = 0;
     while (completed < models.length) {
       const event = await ctx.step.waitForEvent('build_done', { timeout: '2h' });
       completed++;
       // Update job progress
     }
     
     // Finalize job
     return { status: 'completed' };
   }
   
   // BuilderWorkflow - builds with one model (existing logic)
   async run(ctx, event) {
     // ... existing build logic from Slices 1-4
     
     // When done, notify orchestrator
     await this.notifyOrchestrator(jobId, buildId, status, metrics);
   }
   ```

   - `GET /api/jobs/:id` returns nested build statuses
   - `POST /api/jobs/:id/builds/:buildId/respond` for per-build escalation

1. **GitHub Integration**
   ```typescript
   // src/lib/deploy/github.ts
   export async function createAndPushRepo(
     sandbox: Sandbox,
     repoName: string,
     org: string,
     token: string
   ): Promise<string> {
     // Create repo via API
     const repo = await fetch('https://api.github.com/user/repos', {
       method: 'POST',
       headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
       body: JSON.stringify({ name: repoName, private: false })
     });
     
     // Push from sandbox
     await sandbox.exec(`
       git init &&
       git add . &&
       git commit -m "Generated by Nimbus" &&
       git remote add origin https://${token}@github.com/${org}/${repoName}.git &&
       git push -u origin main
     `);
     
     return repo.html_url;
   }
   ```

   │   ├── blog-astro.md
   │   └── blog-sveltekit.md
   ├── dashboard/
   │   └── dashboard-nextjs.md
   └── landing/
       ├── landing-nextjs.md
       └── landing-astro.md
   ```

3. **CLI Commands**
   - `nimbus templates` — List available templates
   - `nimbus start --template ecommerce-astro` — Start from template

4. **Integration with `nimbus start`**
   ```
   ? How would you like to provide your prompt?
     › Type it in
       Load from file
       Use a template
   
   [If "Use a template":]
   
   ? Select a category:
     › E-commerce
       Blog
       Dashboard
       Landing Page
   
   ? Select a template:
     › ecommerce-astro.md - E-commerce site with Astro
       ecommerce-nextjs.md - E-commerce site with Next.js
   
   ? Customize the template? (Y/n)
   ```

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] `nimbus templates` shows available templates by category
- [ ] Can start a job from a template
- [ ] Templates cover at least 3 frameworks
- [ ] Templates cover at least 3 use cases

**Files to Modify/Add:**
```
cli/src/commands/templates.ts    # New command
cli/src/commands/start.ts        # Add template option
cli/templates/                   # Template markdown files
```

**Estimated Time:** 2-3 days

---

### Slice 10: Polish & Documentation

**Goal:** Ready for public open source release.

**Scope:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  - Interactive setup script                                 │
│  - README with quick start guide                           │
│  - CONTRIBUTING guide                                       │
│  - Enhanced reports (Mermaid timelines)                    │
│  - nimbus list improvements                                │
│  - nimbus config improvements                              │
│  - nimbus run from nimbus.json                             │
│  - Polished error messages                                  │
│  - Input validation everywhere                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**What to Build:**

1. **Setup Script**
   ```bash
   #!/bin/bash
   # scripts/setup.sh
   echo "☁️  Nimbus Setup"
   wrangler login
   wrangler d1 create nimbus-db
   wrangler r2 bucket create nimbus-artifacts
   # ... prompt for API keys, run migrations, deploy
   ```

2. **Documentation**
   - README.md with badges, quick start, features
   - docs/getting-started.md
   - docs/configuration.md
   - docs/prompt-writing-guide.md
   - CONTRIBUTING.md

3. **CLI Polish**
   - `nimbus list` with pagination and filters
   - `nimbus config` with better first-time setup UX
   - `nimbus run` from nimbus.json with --yes flag
   - Better error messages with suggestions
   - Loading spinners and progress indicators

4. **Enhanced Reports**
   - Mermaid Gantt chart showing build timeline
   - Per-LLM-call breakdown table

5. **Polish**
   - Consistent error messages
   - Input validation with helpful errors
   - Loading spinners in CLI
   - Colors and formatting

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] New user can deploy in <10 minutes following README
- [ ] `nimbus list` shows past jobs with good formatting
- [ ] `nimbus config` guides users through first-time setup
- [ ] Error messages are clear and actionable
- [ ] Report includes timeline visualization

**Files to Modify/Add:**
```
scripts/setup.sh
README.md
CONTRIBUTING.md
docs/
  getting-started.md
  configuration.md
  prompt-writing-guide.md

cli/src/commands/list.ts         # Pagination, filters
cli/src/commands/config.ts       # Better first-time UX
cli/src/commands/run.ts          # --yes flag

src/lib/metrics/report.ts        # Add Mermaid diagram
```

**Estimated Time:** 3-5 days

---

### 17.3 How to Use This Guide

When implementing each slice, provide the AI assistant with:

1. **The full PRD** (this document) for context
2. **The specific slice section** defining scope
3. **The "Definition of Done" checklist**
4. **The "What's NOT Included" list** to prevent scope creep

**Example prompt for Slice 1:**

```
Here is the full PRD for Nimbus: [attach document]

Implement Slice 1: "Hello World" Build

Scope:
- Single model only (one LLM selected)
- POST /api/jobs and GET /api/jobs/:id endpoints  
- Minimal D1 schema (just jobs table with basic fields)
- Simple workflow: generate → build → deploy
- No retries, no escalation, no detailed metrics
- CLI: nimbus start (simplified) and nimbus watch commands only

Definition of Done:
- Can run: nimbus start, enter a prompt, select one model
- Get back a job ID
- Can run: nimbus watch <jobId> and see completed or failed
- If completed, get a working .pages.dev URL

What's NOT included (do not implement):
- Multiple models per job
- Retries / error recovery  
- Human escalation
- Detailed metrics tracking
- Reports or compare command
- GitHub export
- nimbus.json config files
- Prompt file loading

Start with project scaffolding: wrangler.toml, package.json, 
directory structure, and the minimal D1 schema.
```

---

### 17.4 Slice Dependencies

```
Slice 1 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶
         │
         ▼
Slice 2 (visibility) ───────────────────────────────────────▶
         │
         ▼
Slice 3 (auto-recovery) ────────────────────────────────────▶
         │
         ▼
Slice 4 (human escalation) ─────────────────────────────────▶
         │
         ▼
Slice 5 (multi-model) ──────────────────────────────────────▶
         │
         ├──────────────────┬───────────────────┐
         ▼                  ▼                   ▼
Slice 6 (compare)    Slice 7 (change model)   Slice 8 (GitHub)
         │                  │                   │
         └──────────────────┴───────────────────┘
                            │
                            ▼
                    Slice 9 (templates)
                            │
                            ▼
                    Slice 10 (polish)
```

Slices 6, 7, and 8 can be done in parallel after Slice 5.
Slice 9 can technically start after Slice 1, but benefits from the full pipeline.
Slice 10 should be last.

---

## 18. Testing Strategy

### 18.1 Unit Tests

Test individual components in isolation:

- **LLM Client:** Mock OpenRouter responses
- **Code Generator:** Test prompt construction and response parsing
- **Error Classifier:** Test classification of various error types
- **Framework Registry:** Test framework lookup and validation
- **Report Generator:** Test markdown output

### 18.2 Integration Tests

Test component interactions:

- **API → Database:** Test job creation and retrieval
- **Workflow → Sandbox:** Test code generation and building
- **Workflow → LLM:** Test retry and escalation flows

### 18.3 End-to-End Tests

Test complete flows:

- Create job → Build → Deploy → Generate report
- Create job → Build fails → Auto-recovery → Success
- Create job → Build fails → Escalation → Human response → Success

### 18.4 Test Utilities

```typescript
// tests/utils/mock-llm.ts
export function createMockLLMClient(responses: Map<string, string>): LLMClient {
  // Returns canned responses based on prompt content
}

// tests/utils/mock-sandbox.ts
export function createMockSandbox(buildResults: BuildResult[]): SandboxManager {
  // Returns predetermined build results
}
```

---

## 19. Future Considerations

### 19.1 Potential Enhancements

- **Web Dashboard:** Visual UI for job management and comparison
- **Custom Prompts:** User-defined system prompts for code generation
- **Fine-tuned Models:** Support for custom fine-tuned models
- **Team Features:** Multi-user support with authentication
- **CI/CD Integration:** GitHub Actions, GitLab CI triggers
- **Benchmark Suites:** Pre-defined test suites for consistent comparisons
- **Historical Trends:** Track model performance over time
- **Cost Budgets:** Set spending limits per job

### 19.2 Scaling Considerations

- **Sandbox Limits:** Monitor container provisioning times
- **LLM Rate Limits:** Implement token-bucket rate limiting
- **Database Growth:** Implement job cleanup/archival
- **R2 Storage:** Implement lifecycle policies for old reports

### 19.3 Community Features

- **Plugin System:** Allow community-contributed frameworks
- **Shared Benchmarks:** Public benchmark result sharing
- **Model Leaderboard:** Community-driven model rankings

---

## 20. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Job** | A single benchmarking session with one prompt and multiple variants |
| **Variant** | A specific framework + model combination being tested |
| **Framework** | Web framework used to build the site (Next.js, Astro, etc.) |
| **Model** | LLM used for code generation (Claude, GPT-4, etc.) |
| **Sandbox** | Isolated container where code is built and tested |
| **Workflow** | Cloudflare Workflow providing durable execution |
| **Escalation** | When the system asks a human for help |
| **OpenRouter** | Multi-provider LLM API gateway |
| **D1** | Cloudflare's serverless SQLite database |
| **R2** | Cloudflare's S3-compatible object storage |
| **KV** | Cloudflare's key-value store |
| **Pages** | Cloudflare's static site hosting platform |
| **Preview URL** | Temporary URL to view a site before deployment |

---

## Document Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0.0 | 2025-01-21 | Claude | Initial document |

---

*End of Document*

<content>1: # Nimbus
2: 
3: Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.
4: 
5: This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.
6: 
7: Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)
8: 
9: ## Current Focus
10: 
11: The current priority is launching Entire checkpoints in a sandbox execution pipeline:
12: 
13: - Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
14: - Upload a source bundle to the worker
15: - Queue a background checkpoint job
16: - Run install/build/test/lint inside Cloudflare Sandbox
17: - Persist job status and replayable events
18: 
19: ## What Works Today
20: 
21: - `deploy checkpoint` dry-run resolution and preflight
22: - Live checkpoint job creation with `--no-dry-run`
23: - Queue-backed worker processing (`CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE`)
24: - Deterministic installs with lockfile enforcement
25: - `watch` polling for queued/running/completed/failed jobs
26: 
27: Entire checkpoint notes:
28: 
29: - Checkpoint IDs from commit trailers (for example `checkpoint:be1b10a00b44`) resolve and run.
30: - The worker executes install/build/test/lint in Cloudflare Sandbox for that checkpoint source.
31: 
32: ## Known Limits (Expected Right Now)
33: 
34: - No persistent preview/deployed URL for checkpoint jobs yet
35: - Checkpoint live SSE stream output in CLI is not complete yet
36: - Checkpoint install support is currently for npm/bun lockfiles (pnpm/yarn are rejected)
37: 
38: ## Quick Start (Dev)
39: 
40: From repo root:
41: 
42: ```bash
43: source ~/.bash_profile
44: pnpm install
45: ```
46: 
47: Set up infra (safe to re-run):
48: 
49: ```bash
50: pnpm wrangler queues create nimbus-checkpoint-jobs
51: pnpm wrangler d1 migrations apply nimbus-db --remote
52: ```
53: 
54: Deploy worker:
55: 
56: ```bash
57: pnpm run deploy
58: ```
59: 
60: Point CLI to your worker URL:
61: 
62: ```bash
63: export NIMBUS_WORKER_URL="https://<your-worker>.workers.dev"
64: ```
65: 
66: Create and watch a live checkpoint job:
67: 
68: ```bash
69: pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint checkpoint:be1b10a00b44 --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
70: pnpm cli -- watch <job_id>
71: ```
72: 
73: You can also target any commit-ish:
74: 
75: ```bash
76: pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint HEAD --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
77: ```
78: 
79: ## Common Commands
80: 
81: ```bash
82: # Run worker tests
83: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus-worker test
84: 
85: # Run CLI tests
86: pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus test
87: 
88: # Run worker locally
89: pnpm dev
90: ```
91: 
92: ## CLI Surface (Current)
93: 
94: ```bash
95: nimbus start <prompt>
96: nimbus list
97: nimbus watch <job-id>
98: nimbus deploy checkpoint <checkpoint-id-or-commit-ish>
99: ```
100: 
101: Important checkpoint flags:
102: 
103: - `--project-root <path>`: required for many monorepos
104: - `--no-dry-run`: actually creates/queues a live job
105: - `--no-tests`, `--no-lint`: skip validation steps in metadata
106: - `--env-file`, `--env KEY=VALUE`: pass environment inputs for preflight
107: 
108: ## Notes
109: 
110: - Node 20+ is required.
111: - Nimbus currently targets self-hosted worker usage.
112: - If you hit `404` on `/api/checkpoint/jobs`, deploy the latest worker code.
113: 
114: ## License
115: 
116: MIT

(End of file - total 116 lines)
</content>

# Nimbus

Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.

This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.

Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)

## Current Focus

The current priority is launching Entire checkpoints in a sandbox execution pipeline:

- Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
- Upload a source bundle to the worker
- Queue a background checkpoint job
- Run install/build/test/lint inside Cloudflare Sandbox
- Persist job status and replayable events

## What Works Today

files.before:
# Nimbus

Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.

This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.

Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)

## Current Focus

The current priority is launching Entire checkpoints in a sandbox execution pipeline:

- Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
- Upload a source bundle to the worker
- Queue a background checkpoint job
- Run install/build/test/lint inside Cloudflare Sandbox
- Persist job status and replayable events

## What Works Today

- `deploy checkpoint` dry-run resolution and preflight
- Live checkpoint job creation with `--no-dry-run`
- Queue-backed worker processing (`CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE`)
- Deterministic installs with lockfile enforcement
- `watch` polling for queued/running/completed/failed jobs

Entire checkpoint notes:

- Checkpoint IDs from commit trailers (for example `checkpoint:be1b10a00b44`) resolve and run.
- The worker executes install/build/test/lint in Cloudflare Sandbox for that checkpoint source.

## Known Limits (Expected Right Now)

- No persistent preview/deployed URL for checkpoint jobs yet
- Checkpoint live SSE stream output in CLI is not complete yet
- Checkpoint install support is currently for npm/bun lockfiles (pnpm/yarn are rejected)

## Quick Start (Dev)

From repo root:

```bash
source ~/.bash_profile
pnpm install
```

Set up infra (safe to re-run):

```bash
pnpm wrangler queues create nimbus-checkpoint-jobs
pnpm wrangler d1 migrations apply nimbus-db --remote
```

Deploy worker:

```bash
pnpm run deploy
```

Point CLI to your worker URL:

```bash
export NIMBUS_WORKER_URL="https://<your-worker>.workers.dev"
```

Create and watch a live checkpoint job:

```bash
pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint checkpoint:be1b10a00b44 --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
pnpm cli -- watch <job_id>
```

You can also target any commit-ish:

```bash
pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint HEAD --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
```

## Common Commands

```bash
# Run worker tests
pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus-worker test

# Run CLI tests
pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus test

# Run worker locally
pnpm dev
```

## CLI Surface (Current)

```bash
nimbus start <prompt>
nimbus list
nimbus watch <job-id>
nimbus deploy checkpoint <checkpoint-id-or-commit-ish>
```

Important checkpoint flags:

- `--project-root <path>`: required for many monorepos
- `--no-dry-run`: actually creates/queues a live job
- `--no-tests`, `--no-lint`: skip validation steps in metadata
- `--env-file`, `--env KEY=VALUE`: pass environment inputs for preflight

## Notes

- Node 20+ is required.
- Nimbus currently targets self-hosted worker usage.
- If you hit `404` on `/api/checkpoint/jobs`, deploy the latest worker code.

## License

MIT

# Nimbus

Nimbus is currently an experiment and active work in progress.

This README is intentionally minimal while the product direction settles.

Entire docs: [docs.entire.io/introduction](https://docs.entire.io/introduction)

## Current Focus

The current priority is launching Entire checkpoints in a sandbox execution pipeline:

- Resolve an Entire checkpoint (or commit) to source
- Upload a source bundle to the worker
- Queue a background checkpoint job
- Run install/build/test/lint inside Cloudflare Sandbox
- Persist job status and replayable events

## What Works Today

- `deploy checkpoint` dry-run resolution and preflight
- Live checkpoint job creation with `--no-dry-run`
- Queue-backed worker processing (`CHECKPOINT_JOBS_QUEUE`)
- Deterministic installs with lockfile enforcement
- `watch` polling for queued/running/completed/failed jobs

Entire checkpoint notes:

- Checkpoint IDs from commit trailers (for example `checkpoint:be1b10a00b44`) resolve and run.
- The worker executes install/build/test/lint in Cloudflare Sandbox for that checkpoint source.

## Known Limits (Expected Right Now)

- No persistent preview/deployed URL for checkpoint jobs yet
- Checkpoint live SSE stream output in CLI is not complete yet
- Checkpoint install support is currently for npm/bun lockfiles (pnpm/yarn are rejected)

## Quick Start (Dev)

From repo root:

```bash
source ~/.bash_profile
pnpm install
```

Set up infra (safe to re-run):

```bash
pnpm wrangler queues create nimbus-checkpoint-jobs
pnpm wrangler d1 migrations apply nimbus-db --remote
```

Deploy worker:

```bash
pnpm run deploy
```

Point CLI to your worker URL:

```bash
export NIMBUS_WORKER_URL="https://<your-worker>.workers.dev"
```

Create and watch a live checkpoint job:

```bash
pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint checkpoint:be1b10a00b44 --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
pnpm cli -- watch <job_id>
```

You can also target any commit-ish:

```bash
pnpm cli -- deploy checkpoint HEAD --project-root packages/worker --no-dry-run
```

## Common Commands

```bash
# Run worker tests
pnpm --filter @dayhaysoos/nimbus-worker test

# Run CLI tests

# Run worker locally
pnpm dev
```

## CLI Surface (Current)

```bash
nimbus list
nimbus watch <job-id>
nimbus deploy checkpoint <checkpoint-id-or-commit-ish>
```

Legacy compatibility command (prompt-to-site path):

```bash

```

Important checkpoint flags:

- `--project-root <path>`: required for many monorepos
- `--no-dry-run`: actually creates/queues a live job
- `--no-tests`, `--no-lint`: skip validation steps in metadata
- `--env-file`, `--env KEY=VALUE`: pass environment inputs for preflight

## Notes

- Node 20+ is required.
- Nimbus currently targets self-hosted worker usage.
- If you hit `404` on `/api/checkpoint/jobs`, deploy the latest worker code.

Index: /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
===================================================================
--- /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
+++ /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
@@ -1,405 +0,0 @@
-# Slice 1: Persistence
-
-> **Branch:** `persistence`  
-> **Estimated Time:** 3-5 days  
-> **Theme:** Jobs are stored, trackable, and permanently deployed
-
----
-
-## Overview
-
-Currently, Nimbus builds are ephemeral - once the CLI disconnects or the sandbox times out, everything is lost. This slice adds persistence so users can:
-
-1. See a list of past jobs
-2. Get the status of any job by ID
-3. Have permanently deployed sites (Cloudflare Pages)
-
----
-
-## Current State (Slice 0)
-
-```
-CLI → POST /build (SSE stream) → Worker → Claude → Sandbox → Preview URL
-                                                              ↓
-                                                    (dies when sandbox dies)
-```
-
-- No database
-- No job history
-- Preview URLs are temporary
-- Can't check status after CLI disconnects
-
----
-
-## Target State (Slice 1)
-
-```
-CLI → POST /api/jobs → Worker → D1 (create job) → Workflow → Claude → Sandbox → Pages
-         ↓                                            ↓
-    Returns job_id                              Updates D1 status
-         ↓
-CLI → GET /api/jobs/:id → D1 → Returns status + deployed URL
-```
-
-- Jobs stored in D1 database
-- Permanent URLs via Cloudflare Pages
-- Can check status anytime
-- Job history with `nimbus list`
-
----
-
-## Features
-
-### 1. D1 Database
-
-Create a Cloudflare D1 database to store jobs.
-
-**Schema:**
-
-```sql
-CREATE TABLE jobs (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- e.g., "job_abc123"
-    prompt TEXT NOT NULL,
-    model TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- e.g., "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending|running|completed|failed
-    
-    -- Timestamps
-    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    started_at TEXT,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    
-    -- Output
-    preview_url TEXT,                       -- Sandbox preview (temporary)
-    deployed_url TEXT,                      -- Pages URL (permanent)
-    
-    -- Error info
-    error_message TEXT,
-    
-    -- Basic metrics (expanded in Slice 2)
-    file_count INTEGER,
-    
-    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed'))
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_status ON jobs(status);
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_created_at ON jobs(created_at);
-```
-
-**Setup commands:**
-
-```bash
-cd packages/worker
-
-# Create database
-npx wrangler d1 create nimbus-db
-
-# Add binding to wrangler.toml (will get database_id from above command)
-# [[d1_databases]]
-# binding = "DB"
-# database_name = "nimbus-db"
-# database_id = "<from create command>"
-
-# Run migration
-npx wrangler d1 execute nimbus-db --file=./migrations/0001_jobs.sql
-```
-
-### 2. New API Endpoints
-
-Replace `POST /build` (SSE) with proper REST endpoints:
-
-| Method | Endpoint | Description |
-|--------|----------|-------------|
-| POST | `/api/jobs` | Create job, return job ID |
-| GET | `/api/jobs` | List all jobs |
-| GET | `/api/jobs/:id` | Get job status |
-
-**POST /api/jobs**
-
-Request:
-```json
-{
-  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-}
-```
-
-Response (202 Accepted):
-```json
-{
-  "jobId": "job_abc123",
-  "status": "pending"
-}
-```
-
-**GET /api/jobs/:id**
-
-Response:
-```json
-{
-  "id": "job_abc123",
-  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
-  "status": "completed",
-  "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
-  "startedAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:01Z",
-  "completedAt": "2025-01-22T04:02:15Z",
-  "previewUrl": "https://8080-job-abc123.getnimbus.dev/",
-  "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/",
-  "fileCount": 3
-}
-```
-
-**GET /api/jobs**
-
-Response:
-```json
-{
-  "jobs": [
-    {
-      "id": "job_abc123",
-      "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
-      "status": "completed",
-      "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
-      "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/"
-    }
-  ]
-}
-```
-
-### 3. Cloudflare Pages Deployment
-
-After a successful build, deploy to Cloudflare Pages for a permanent URL.
-
-**Implementation approach:**
-
-Option A: Use Wrangler from inside sandbox
-```typescript
-// In sandbox after build succeeds
-await sandbox.exec(
-  `npx wrangler pages deploy ./dist --project-name=${jobId}`,
-  {
-    env: {
-      CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN,
-      CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
-    }
-  }
-);
-```
-
-Option B: Use Cloudflare API directly from worker
-```typescript
-// Read built files from sandbox, upload via API
-const files = await sandbox.readDir('/root/app/dist');
-const response = await fetch(
-  `https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/${accountId}/pages/projects/${projectName}/deployments`,
-  {
-    method: 'POST',
-    headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiToken}` },
-    body: createFormData(files)
-  }
-);
-```
-
-**Recommendation:** Start with Option A (simpler), switch to Option B if needed.
-
-**Required secrets:**
-```bash
-npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
-npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
-```
-
-### 4. CLI Updates
-
-**New command: `nimbus list`**
-
-```bash
-$ nimbus list
-
-  ID            Status      Model              Created         URL
-  job_abc123    completed   claude-sonnet-4    2 hours ago     https://job-abc123.pages.dev
-  job_def456    running     claude-sonnet-4    5 minutes ago   -
-  job_ghi789    failed      gpt-4o             1 day ago       -
-```
-
-**Updated: `nimbus watch`**
-
-Instead of relying on SSE, poll `GET /api/jobs/:id`:
-
-```typescript
-while (status !== 'completed' && status !== 'failed') {
-  const job = await fetch(`${WORKER_URL}/api/jobs/${jobId}`);
-  displayStatus(job);
-  await sleep(2000);  // Poll every 2 seconds
-}
-```
-
-**New: Show deployed URL at end**
-
-```
-┌  @dayhaysoos/nimbus
-│
-◇  Job created: job_abc123
-◇  Generating code...
-◇  Building...
-◇  Deploying to Pages...
-◇  Done!
-│
-└  Deployed: https://job-abc123.pages.dev/
-```
-
-### 5. Background Processing
-
-Currently, the build runs synchronously inside the request handler. For persistence, we need background processing.
-
-**Option A: Keep SSE but also write to D1**
-
-The current SSE approach can stay, just add D1 writes at each step:
-
-```typescript
-async function handleBuild(request, env) {
-  // Create job in D1
-  const jobId = `job_${generateId()}`;
-  await env.DB.prepare('INSERT INTO jobs ...').run();
-  
-  // Existing SSE streaming logic, but also update D1
-  sendEvent({ type: 'generating' });
-  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "running"...').run();
-  
-  // ... rest of build ...
-  
-  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "completed"...').run();
-}
-```
-
-**Option B: Use Cloudflare Workflows (from spec)**
-
-More robust, but more complex. Deferred to later slice.
-
-**Recommendation:** Start with Option A. It's simpler and the SSE streaming UX is nice. Add Workflows in a future slice when we need multi-variant parallel builds.
-
----
-
-## File Changes
-
-### New Files
-
-```
-packages/worker/
-├── migrations/
-│   └── 0001_jobs.sql           # Database schema
-├── src/
-│   ├── api/
-│   │   └── jobs.ts             # Job CRUD endpoints
-│   └── lib/
-│       └── deploy/
-│           └── pages.ts        # Pages deployment logic
-
-packages/cli/
-├── src/
-│   └── commands/
-│       └── list.ts             # New list command
-```
-
-### Modified Files
-
-```
-packages/worker/
-├── wrangler.toml               # Add D1 binding
-├── src/
-│   ├── index.ts                # Update routing
-│   ├── types.ts                # Add Env.DB type
-│   └── sandbox.ts              # Add Pages deployment step
-
-packages/cli/
-├── src/
-│   ├── index.ts                # Add list command, update watch
-│   └── commands/
-│       └── watch.ts            # Switch to polling
-```
-
----
-
-## Task Breakdown
-
-### Day 1: Database Setup
-- [ ] Create D1 database
-- [ ] Write migration SQL
-- [ ] Add D1 binding to wrangler.toml
-- [ ] Update Env type in types.ts
-- [ ] Test database connection
-
-### Day 2: API Endpoints
-- [ ] Create `POST /api/jobs` - creates job, starts build
-- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs/:id` - returns job status
-- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs` - lists all jobs
-- [ ] Update existing build logic to write to D1
-
-### Day 3: Pages Deployment
-- [ ] Add CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and ACCOUNT_ID secrets
-- [ ] Implement Pages deployment after successful build
-- [ ] Store deployed_url in D1
-- [ ] Test end-to-end deployment
-
-### Day 4: CLI Updates
-- [ ] Add `nimbus list` command
-- [ ] Update `nimbus watch` to poll API (or keep SSE + show final URL)
-- [ ] Show deployed URL at completion
-- [ ] Test full flow
-
-### Day 5: Polish & Edge Cases
-- [ ] Handle deployment failures gracefully
-- [ ] Add error messages to D1
-- [ ] Test with failed builds
-- [ ] Update README if needed
-
----
-
-## What's NOT in This Slice
-
-- Multiple models per job (Slice 5)
-- Auto-retry on build failures (Slice 3)
-- Human escalation (Slice 4)
-- Detailed metrics (tokens, cost, duration) (Slice 2)
-- Reports (Slice 6)
-- GitHub export (Slice 8)
-- Cloudflare Workflows (can add later for robustness)
-- Job cleanup/TTL (later slice)
-
----
-
-## Definition of Done
-
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus start "prompt"` and get a job ID
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus list` and see past jobs
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus watch <jobId>` and see status
-- [ ] Completed jobs have a permanent `*.pages.dev` URL
-- [ ] Jobs persist across CLI sessions (can check status later)
-- [ ] Failed jobs show error message
-
----
-
-## Open Questions
-
-1. **Keep SSE or switch to pure polling?**
-   - SSE gives nice real-time UX but adds complexity
-   - Polling is simpler and works with the REST API pattern
-   - Recommendation: Keep SSE for now, also write to D1
-
-2. **Pages project naming?**
-   - Option: `nimbus-{jobId}` → `nimbus-job-abc123.pages.dev`
-   - Option: Just `{jobId}` → `job-abc123.pages.dev`
-   - Need to handle naming conflicts
-
-3. **What to do with preview URL?**
-   - Keep exposing it during build (useful for debugging)
-   - Could remove once Pages deployment succeeds
-   - Or keep both URLs in the response
-
----
-
-## References
-
-- [Cloudflare D1 Docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/d1/)
-- [Cloudflare Pages API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/platform/api/)
-- [Full PRD - Section 17.2 Slice 1](../PRD.md) (if you save the full spec)

Index: /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
===================================================================
--- /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
+++ /Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/specs/persistence.md
@@ -1,405 +0,0 @@
-# Slice 1: Persistence
-
-> **Branch:** `persistence`  
-> **Estimated Time:** 3-5 days  
-> **Theme:** Jobs are stored, trackable, and permanently deployed
-
----
-
-## Overview
-
-Currently, Nimbus builds are ephemeral - once the CLI disconnects or the sandbox times out, everything is lost. This slice adds persistence so users can:
-
-1. See a list of past jobs
-2. Get the status of any job by ID
-3. Have permanently deployed sites (Cloudflare Pages)
-
----
-
-## Current State (Slice 0)
-
-```
-CLI → POST /build (SSE stream) → Worker → Claude → Sandbox → Preview URL
-                                                              ↓
-                                                    (dies when sandbox dies)
-```
-
-- No database
-- No job history
-- Preview URLs are temporary
-- Can't check status after CLI disconnects
-
----
-
-## Target State (Slice 1)
-
-```
-CLI → POST /api/jobs → Worker → D1 (create job) → Workflow → Claude → Sandbox → Pages
-         ↓                                            ↓
-    Returns job_id                              Updates D1 status
-         ↓
-CLI → GET /api/jobs/:id → D1 → Returns status + deployed URL
-```
-
-- Jobs stored in D1 database
-- Permanent URLs via Cloudflare Pages
-- Can check status anytime
-- Job history with `nimbus list`
-
----
-
-## Features
-
-### 1. D1 Database
-
-Create a Cloudflare D1 database to store jobs.
-
-**Schema:**
-
-```sql
-CREATE TABLE jobs (
-    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- e.g., "job_abc123"
-    prompt TEXT NOT NULL,
-    model TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- e.g., "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending|running|completed|failed
-    
-    -- Timestamps
-    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
-    started_at TEXT,
-    completed_at TEXT,
-    
-    -- Output
-    preview_url TEXT,                       -- Sandbox preview (temporary)
-    deployed_url TEXT,                      -- Pages URL (permanent)
-    
-    -- Error info
-    error_message TEXT,
-    
-    -- Basic metrics (expanded in Slice 2)
-    file_count INTEGER,
-    
-    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed'))
-);
-
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_status ON jobs(status);
-CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_created_at ON jobs(created_at);
-```
-
-**Setup commands:**
-
-```bash
-cd packages/worker
-
-# Create database
-npx wrangler d1 create nimbus-db
-
-# Add binding to wrangler.toml (will get database_id from above command)
-# [[d1_databases]]
-# binding = "DB"
-# database_name = "nimbus-db"
-# database_id = "<from create command>"
-
-# Run migration
-npx wrangler d1 execute nimbus-db --file=./migrations/0001_jobs.sql
-```
-
-### 2. New API Endpoints
-
-Replace `POST /build` (SSE) with proper REST endpoints:
-
-| Method | Endpoint | Description |
-|--------|----------|-------------|
-| POST | `/api/jobs` | Create job, return job ID |
-| GET | `/api/jobs` | List all jobs |
-| GET | `/api/jobs/:id` | Get job status |
-
-**POST /api/jobs**
-
-Request:
-```json
-{
-  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
-}
-```
-
-Response (202 Accepted):
-```json
-{
-  "jobId": "job_abc123",
-  "status": "pending"
-}
-```
-
-**GET /api/jobs/:id**
-
-Response:
-```json
-{
-  "id": "job_abc123",
-  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
-  "status": "completed",
-  "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
-  "startedAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:01Z",
-  "completedAt": "2025-01-22T04:02:15Z",
-  "previewUrl": "https://8080-job-abc123.getnimbus.dev/",
-  "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/",
-  "fileCount": 3
-}
-```
-
-**GET /api/jobs**
-
-Response:
-```json
-{
-  "jobs": [
-    {
-      "id": "job_abc123",
-      "prompt": "Build a landing page",
-      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
-      "status": "completed",
-      "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
-      "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/"
-    }
-  ]
-}
-```
-
-### 3. Cloudflare Pages Deployment
-
-After a successful build, deploy to Cloudflare Pages for a permanent URL.
-
-**Implementation approach:**
-
-Option A: Use Wrangler from inside sandbox
-```typescript
-// In sandbox after build succeeds
-await sandbox.exec(
-  `npx wrangler pages deploy ./dist --project-name=${jobId}`,
-  {
-    env: {
-      CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN,
-      CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
-    }
-  }
-);
-```
-
-Option B: Use Cloudflare API directly from worker
-```typescript
-// Read built files from sandbox, upload via API
-const files = await sandbox.readDir('/root/app/dist');
-const response = await fetch(
-  `https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/${accountId}/pages/projects/${projectName}/deployments`,
-  {
-    method: 'POST',
-    headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiToken}` },
-    body: createFormData(files)
-  }
-);
-```
-
-**Recommendation:** Start with Option A (simpler), switch to Option B if needed.
-
-**Required secrets:**
-```bash
-npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
-npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
-```
-
-### 4. CLI Updates
-
-**New command: `nimbus list`**
-
-```bash
-$ nimbus list
-
-  ID            Status      Model              Created         URL
-  job_abc123    completed   claude-sonnet-4    2 hours ago     https://job-abc123.pages.dev
-  job_def456    running     claude-sonnet-4    5 minutes ago   -
-  job_ghi789    failed      gpt-4o             1 day ago       -
-```
-
-**Updated: `nimbus watch`**
-
-Instead of relying on SSE, poll `GET /api/jobs/:id`:
-
-```typescript
-while (status !== 'completed' && status !== 'failed') {
-  const job = await fetch(`${WORKER_URL}/api/jobs/${jobId}`);
-  displayStatus(job);
-  await sleep(2000);  // Poll every 2 seconds
-}
-```
-
-**New: Show deployed URL at end**
-
-```
-┌  @dayhaysoos/nimbus
-│
-◇  Job created: job_abc123
-◇  Generating code...
-◇  Building...
-◇  Deploying to Pages...
-◇  Done!
-│
-└  Deployed: https://job-abc123.pages.dev/
-```
-
-### 5. Background Processing
-
-Currently, the build runs synchronously inside the request handler. For persistence, we need background processing.
-
-**Option A: Keep SSE but also write to D1**
-
-The current SSE approach can stay, just add D1 writes at each step:
-
-```typescript
-async function handleBuild(request, env) {
-  // Create job in D1
-  const jobId = `job_${generateId()}`;
-  await env.DB.prepare('INSERT INTO jobs ...').run();
-  
-  // Existing SSE streaming logic, but also update D1
-  sendEvent({ type: 'generating' });
-  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "running"...').run();
-  
-  // ... rest of build ...
-  
-  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "completed"...').run();
-}
-```
-
-**Option B: Use Cloudflare Workflows (from spec)**
-
-More robust, but more complex. Deferred to later slice.
-
-**Recommendation:** Start with Option A. It's simpler and the SSE streaming UX is nice. Add Workflows in a future slice when we need multi-variant parallel builds.
-
----
-
-## File Changes
-
-### New Files
-
-```
-packages/worker/
-├── migrations/
-│   └── 0001_jobs.sql           # Database schema
-├── src/
-│   ├── api/
-│   │   └── jobs.ts             # Job CRUD endpoints
-│   └── lib/
-│       └── deploy/
-│           └── pages.ts        # Pages deployment logic
-
-packages/cli/
-├── src/
-│   └── commands/
-│       └── list.ts             # New list command
-```
-
-### Modified Files
-
-```
-packages/worker/
-├── wrangler.toml               # Add D1 binding
-├── src/
-│   ├── index.ts                # Update routing
-│   ├── types.ts                # Add Env.DB type
-│   └── sandbox.ts              # Add Pages deployment step
-
-packages/cli/
-├── src/
-│   ├── index.ts                # Add list command, update watch
-│   └── commands/
-│       └── watch.ts            # Switch to polling
-```
-
----
-
-## Task Breakdown
-
-### Day 1: Database Setup
-- [ ] Create D1 database
-- [ ] Write migration SQL
-- [ ] Add D1 binding to wrangler.toml
-- [ ] Update Env type in types.ts
-- [ ] Test database connection
-
-### Day 2: API Endpoints
-- [ ] Create `POST /api/jobs` - creates job, starts build
-- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs/:id` - returns job status
-- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs` - lists all jobs
-- [ ] Update existing build logic to write to D1
-
-### Day 3: Pages Deployment
-- [ ] Add CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and ACCOUNT_ID secrets
-- [ ] Implement Pages deployment after successful build
-- [ ] Store deployed_url in D1
-- [ ] Test end-to-end deployment
-
-### Day 4: CLI Updates
-- [ ] Add `nimbus list` command
-- [ ] Update `nimbus watch` to poll API (or keep SSE + show final URL)
-- [ ] Show deployed URL at completion
-- [ ] Test full flow
-
-### Day 5: Polish & Edge Cases
-- [ ] Handle deployment failures gracefully
-- [ ] Add error messages to D1
-- [ ] Test with failed builds
-- [ ] Update README if needed
-
----
-
-## What's NOT in This Slice
-
-- Multiple models per job (Slice 5)
-- Auto-retry on build failures (Slice 3)
-- Human escalation (Slice 4)
-- Detailed metrics (tokens, cost, duration) (Slice 2)
-- Reports (Slice 6)
-- GitHub export (Slice 8)
-- Cloudflare Workflows (can add later for robustness)
-- Job cleanup/TTL (later slice)
-
----
-
-## Definition of Done
-
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus start "prompt"` and get a job ID
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus list` and see past jobs
-- [ ] Can run `nimbus watch <jobId>` and see status
-- [ ] Completed jobs have a permanent `*.pages.dev` URL
-- [ ] Jobs persist across CLI sessions (can check status later)
-- [ ] Failed jobs show error message
-
----
-
-## Open Questions
-
-1. **Keep SSE or switch to pure polling?**
-   - SSE gives nice real-time UX but adds complexity
-   - Polling is simpler and works with the REST API pattern
-   - Recommendation: Keep SSE for now, also write to D1
-
-2. **Pages project naming?**
-   - Option: `nimbus-{jobId}` → `nimbus-job-abc123.pages.dev`
-   - Option: Just `{jobId}` → `job-abc123.pages.dev`
-   - Need to handle naming conflicts
-
-3. **What to do with preview URL?**
-   - Keep exposing it during build (useful for debugging)
-   - Could remove once Pages deployment succeeds
-   - Or keep both URLs in the response
-
----
-
-## References
-
-- [Cloudflare D1 Docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/d1/)
-- [Cloudflare Pages API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/platform/api/)
-- [Full PRD - Section 17.2 Slice 1](../PRD.md) (if you save the full spec)
files.before:
# Slice 1: Persistence

> **Branch:** `persistence`  
> **Estimated Time:** 3-5 days  
> **Theme:** Jobs are stored, trackable, and permanently deployed

---

## Overview

Currently, Nimbus builds are ephemeral - once the CLI disconnects or the sandbox times out, everything is lost. This slice adds persistence so users can:

1. See a list of past jobs
2. Get the status of any job by ID
3. Have permanently deployed sites (Cloudflare Pages)

---

## Current State (Slice 0)

```
CLI → POST /build (SSE stream) → Worker → Claude → Sandbox → Preview URL
                                                              ↓
                                                    (dies when sandbox dies)
```

- No database
- No job history
- Preview URLs are temporary
- Can't check status after CLI disconnects

---

## Target State (Slice 1)

```
CLI → POST /api/jobs → Worker → D1 (create job) → Workflow → Claude → Sandbox → Pages
         ↓                                            ↓
    Returns job_id                              Updates D1 status
         ↓
CLI → GET /api/jobs/:id → D1 → Returns status + deployed URL
```

- Jobs stored in D1 database
- Permanent URLs via Cloudflare Pages
- Can check status anytime
- Job history with `nimbus list`

---

## Features

### 1. D1 Database

Create a Cloudflare D1 database to store jobs.

**Schema:**

```sql
CREATE TABLE jobs (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                    -- e.g., "job_abc123"
    prompt TEXT NOT NULL,
    model TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- e.g., "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending|running|completed|failed
    
    -- Timestamps
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    started_at TEXT,
    completed_at TEXT,
    
    -- Output
    preview_url TEXT,                       -- Sandbox preview (temporary)
    deployed_url TEXT,                      -- Pages URL (permanent)
    
    -- Error info
    error_message TEXT,
    
    -- Basic metrics (expanded in Slice 2)
    file_count INTEGER,
    
    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_status ON jobs(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_created_at ON jobs(created_at);
```

**Setup commands:**

```bash
cd packages/worker

# Create database
npx wrangler d1 create nimbus-db

# Add binding to wrangler.toml (will get database_id from above command)
# [[d1_databases]]
# binding = "DB"
# database_name = "nimbus-db"
# database_id = "<from create command>"

# Run migration
npx wrangler d1 execute nimbus-db --file=./migrations/0001_jobs.sql
```

### 2. New API Endpoints

Replace `POST /build` (SSE) with proper REST endpoints:

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/jobs` | Create job, return job ID |
| GET | `/api/jobs` | List all jobs |
| GET | `/api/jobs/:id` | Get job status |

**POST /api/jobs**

Request:
```json
{
  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
}
```

Response (202 Accepted):
```json
{
  "jobId": "job_abc123",
  "status": "pending"
}
```

**GET /api/jobs/:id**

Response:
```json
{
  "id": "job_abc123",
  "prompt": "Build a landing page",
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
  "status": "completed",
  "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
  "startedAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:01Z",
  "completedAt": "2025-01-22T04:02:15Z",
  "previewUrl": "https://8080-job-abc123.getnimbus.dev/",
  "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/",
  "fileCount": 3
}
```

**GET /api/jobs**

Response:
```json
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "job_abc123",
      "prompt": "Build a landing page",
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
      "status": "completed",
      "createdAt": "2025-01-22T04:00:00Z",
      "deployedUrl": "https://job-abc123.pages.dev/"
    }
  ]
}
```

### 3. Cloudflare Pages Deployment

After a successful build, deploy to Cloudflare Pages for a permanent URL.

**Implementation approach:**

Option A: Use Wrangler from inside sandbox
```typescript
// In sandbox after build succeeds
await sandbox.exec(
  `npx wrangler pages deploy ./dist --project-name=${jobId}`,
  {
    env: {
      CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN,
      CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
    }
  }
);
```

Option B: Use Cloudflare API directly from worker
```typescript
// Read built files from sandbox, upload via API
const files = await sandbox.readDir('/root/app/dist');
const response = await fetch(
  `https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/${accountId}/pages/projects/${projectName}/deployments`,
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiToken}` },
    body: createFormData(files)
  }
);
```

**Recommendation:** Start with Option A (simpler), switch to Option B if needed.

**Required secrets:**
```bash
npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
npx wrangler secret put CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
```

### 4. CLI Updates

**New command: `nimbus list`**

```bash
$ nimbus list

  ID            Status      Model              Created         URL
  job_abc123    completed   claude-sonnet-4    2 hours ago     https://job-abc123.pages.dev
  job_def456    running     claude-sonnet-4    5 minutes ago   -
  job_ghi789    failed      gpt-4o             1 day ago       -
```

**Updated: `nimbus watch`**

Instead of relying on SSE, poll `GET /api/jobs/:id`:

```typescript
while (status !== 'completed' && status !== 'failed') {
  const job = await fetch(`${WORKER_URL}/api/jobs/${jobId}`);
  displayStatus(job);
  await sleep(2000);  // Poll every 2 seconds
}
```

**New: Show deployed URL at end**

```
┌  @dayhaysoos/nimbus
│
◇  Job created: job_abc123
◇  Generating code...
◇  Building...
◇  Deploying to Pages...
◇  Done!
│
└  Deployed: https://job-abc123.pages.dev/
```

### 5. Background Processing

Currently, the build runs synchronously inside the request handler. For persistence, we need background processing.

**Option A: Keep SSE but also write to D1**

The current SSE approach can stay, just add D1 writes at each step:

```typescript
async function handleBuild(request, env) {
  // Create job in D1
  const jobId = `job_${generateId()}`;
  await env.DB.prepare('INSERT INTO jobs ...').run();
  
  // Existing SSE streaming logic, but also update D1
  sendEvent({ type: 'generating' });
  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "running"...').run();
  
  // ... rest of build ...
  
  await env.DB.prepare('UPDATE jobs SET status = "completed"...').run();
}
```

**Option B: Use Cloudflare Workflows (from spec)**

More robust, but more complex. Deferred to later slice.

**Recommendation:** Start with Option A. It's simpler and the SSE streaming UX is nice. Add Workflows in a future slice when we need multi-variant parallel builds.

---

## File Changes

### New Files

```
packages/worker/
├── migrations/
│   └── 0001_jobs.sql           # Database schema
├── src/
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── jobs.ts             # Job CRUD endpoints
│   └── lib/
│       └── deploy/
│           └── pages.ts        # Pages deployment logic

packages/cli/
├── src/
│   └── commands/
│       └── list.ts             # New list command
```

### Modified Files

```
packages/worker/
├── wrangler.toml               # Add D1 binding
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                # Update routing
│   ├── types.ts                # Add Env.DB type
│   └── sandbox.ts              # Add Pages deployment step

packages/cli/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                # Add list command, update watch
│   └── commands/
│       └── watch.ts            # Switch to polling
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Day 1: Database Setup
- [ ] Create D1 database
- [ ] Write migration SQL
- [ ] Add D1 binding to wrangler.toml
- [ ] Update Env type in types.ts
- [ ] Test database connection

### Day 2: API Endpoints
- [ ] Create `POST /api/jobs` - creates job, starts build
- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs/:id` - returns job status
- [ ] Create `GET /api/jobs` - lists all jobs
- [ ] Update existing build logic to write to D1

### Day 3: Pages Deployment
- [ ] Add CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and ACCOUNT_ID secrets
- [ ] Implement Pages deployment after successful build
- [ ] Store deployed_url in D1
- [ ] Test end-to-end deployment

### Day 4: CLI Updates
- [ ] Add `nimbus list` command
- [ ] Update `nimbus watch` to poll API (or keep SSE + show final URL)
- [ ] Show deployed URL at completion
- [ ] Test full flow

### Day 5: Polish & Edge Cases
- [ ] Handle deployment failures gracefully
- [ ] Add error messages to D1
- [ ] Test with failed builds
- [ ] Update README if needed

---

## What's NOT in This Slice

- Multiple models per job (Slice 5)
- Auto-retry on build failures (Slice 3)
- Human escalation (Slice 4)
- Detailed metrics (tokens, cost, duration) (Slice 2)
- Reports (Slice 6)
- GitHub export (Slice 8)
- Cloudflare Workflows (can add later for robustness)
- Job cleanup/TTL (later slice)

---

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Can run `nimbus start "prompt"` and get a job ID
- [ ] Can run `nimbus list` and see past jobs
- [ ] Can run `nimbus watch <jobId>` and see status
- [ ] Completed jobs have a permanent `*.pages.dev` URL
- [ ] Jobs persist across CLI sessions (can check status later)
- [ ] Failed jobs show error message

---

## Open Questions

1. **Keep SSE or switch to pure polling?**
   - SSE gives nice real-time UX but adds complexity
   - Polling is simpler and works with the REST API pattern
   - Recommendation: Keep SSE for now, also write to D1

2. **Pages project naming?**
   - Option: `nimbus-{jobId}` → `nimbus-job-abc123.pages.dev`
   - Option: Just `{jobId}` → `job-abc123.pages.dev`
   - Need to handle naming conflicts

3. **What to do with preview URL?**
   - Keep exposing it during build (useful for debugging)
   - Could remove once Pages deployment succeeds
   - Or keep both URLs in the response

---

## References

- [Cloudflare D1 Docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/d1/)
- [Cloudflare Pages API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/platform/api/)
- [Full PRD - Section 17.2 Slice 1](../PRD.md) (if you save the full spec)
